C.description: C is a general-purpose, procedural computer programming language supporting structured programming, lexical variable scope, and recursion, with a static type system.
C.history: C was created in 1972 by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs as a successor to the B language, primarily to develop the Unix operating system. It was standardized by ANSI in 1989 (C89/C90) and later by ISO, with major revisions in C99, C11, C17, and C23. It remains one of the most widely used programming languages in the world, especially for systems programming, embedded development, and operating systems.
C.summary_abuse: C is a foundational programming language with no consumer-facing surface and no meaningful abuse signal at the language level.

c/side.description: c/side is a security solution that protects organizations from advanced client-side threats, including script injection, data skimming, and browser-based attacks.
c/side.history: c/side is a client-side security SaaS founded around 2023, focused on protecting against script injection, data skimming, and browser-based attacks. It is an emerging security startup with limited public history.
c/side.summary_abuse: c/side is a security tool designed to detect and block client-side attacks. It has no notable abuse signal as a platform and does not expose features that fraudsters would exploit.

c15t.description: c15t is an open source framework for managing cookies, consent, and privacy compliance.
c15t.history: c15t is an open-source consent and privacy management framework released around 2024. It is a relatively new project with limited history.
c15t.summary_abuse: c15t is a privacy and consent management library with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool for managing GDPR/cookie consent with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

C3.js.history: C3.js was created in 2013 by Masayuki Tanaka as a D3.js wrapper that simplifies chart creation. The library became dormant around 2017 as alternatives like Chart.js, Recharts, and Vega-Lite gained popularity.
C3.js.summary_abuse: C3.js is a client-side JavaScript charting library with no backend and no meaningful abuse surface.

Caast.tv.description: Caast.tv is a digital commercial animation solution integrated into the ecommerce customer journey.
Caast.tv.history: Caast.tv is a French live shopping and video commerce platform founded around 2019, enabling brands to embed live and recorded shoppable video streams directly into their e-commerce sites.
Caast.tv.summary_abuse: Caast.tv is a live shopping video platform for e-commerce brands. No notable abuse surface or documented incidents.

Cabin.description: Cabin is a web analytics service that tracks and reports website traffic.
Cabin.history: Cabin is a privacy-first web analytics service launched in 2021. It collects no personally identifiable information, uses no cookies, and is fully GDPR compliant by design. It positions as an ethical alternative to Google Analytics.
Cabin.summary_abuse: Cabin is a privacy-first analytics tool with no PII collection, no cookies, and no third-party tracking. It has essentially no abuse surface.

CacheFly.description: CacheFly is a content delivery network (CDN) which offers CDN service that relies solely on IP anycast for routing, rather than DNS based global load balancing.
CacheFly.history: CacheFly was founded in Chicago, Illinois with roots as Downloadhosting.com in 1999. The CacheFly CDN service launched in March 2005 and pioneered TCP Anycast in 2002. It has grown to 50+ PoPs serving 500+ clients in nearly 100 countries.
CacheFly.summary_abuse: CacheFly is a specialized CDN focused on throughput for media delivery. Like all CDNs it can cache and deliver malicious content. It is not a commodity free-tier CDN that attracts mass abuse. No specific outsized abuse signal documented.

Cachet.description: Cachet is the free and open-source status page for your API, service or company.
Cachet.history: Cachet is an open-source status page system founded in 2015 by James Brooks. It allows teams to show uptime and incident updates for their APIs and services. The project is maintained as open source with a commercially hosted version.
Cachet.summary_abuse: Cachet is an open-source status page system. It has no meaningful abuse surface as it is typically self-hosted and displays availability status for services. No documented abuse incidents.

Cackle.description: Cackle is a cloud services platform for increasing conversion using comments, feedback collection, online consultant and surveys.
Cackle.history: Cackle is a Russian cloud platform providing comments widgets, reviews, ratings, and social login features for websites. Founded around 2012, it primarily serves the Russian-language web.
Cackle.summary_abuse: Cackle is a Russian-hosted comments and social login widget platform. Russian-jurisdiction SaaS embedded on sites lacks GDPR compliance for EU users. Comment platforms are frequently exploited for spam and link injection. Cackle has no GDPR Data Processing Agreement and user data is processed under Russian law. Elevated risk for Western deployments.

CactiveCloud.description: CactiveCloud is a freemium based cloud provider and web server for static deployments of websites with HTML builds and serverless functions.
CactiveCloud.history: CactiveCloud is a small freemium static web hosting and serverless functions platform. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
CactiveCloud.summary_abuse: CactiveCloud is a small static hosting platform with a free tier. Free-tier hosting services with minimal oversight can be used to host phishing pages or malware. The platform's small scale and lack of public AUP documentation are mild risk indicators.

Caddy.description: Caddy is a Go-based web server known for its simplicity and automatic HTTPS features.
Caddy.history: Caddy was created in 2015 by Matt Holt and was the first web server to implement automatic HTTPS using Let's Encrypt. The project was initially backed by Ardan Labs, then acquired by Apilayer in 2020. Caddy is now a trademark of Stack Holdings GmbH and is a project of ZeroSSL (an HID Global company). Caddy v2 launched in May 2020 with a new configuration system. It has been downloaded over 30 million times.
Caddy.summary_abuse: Caddy is an open-source Go-based web server. As a general-purpose web server it can serve any content, but the tool itself has no consumer surface and no dedicated abuse infrastructure. No notable abuse incidents.

Cafe24.description: Cafe24 is a global ecommerce platform that provides everything people need to build an online DTC store in one stop.
Cafe24.history: Cafe24 was founded in 1999 in South Korea as one of the country's largest e-commerce platform providers. The company is publicly listed on the Korean Stock Exchange (KOSDAQ) and expanded internationally with a global platform. It serves over 2 million merchants predominantly in Asia.
Cafe24.summary_abuse: Cafe24 is a large Korean e-commerce platform serving millions of merchants. E-commerce platforms of this scale host a variety of merchants including some running fraudulent operations. Cafe24 has standard merchant onboarding but the scale and international reach of the platform mean some abuse incidents occur. South Korean-origin fraudulent storefronts have been documented using Cafe24.

Caisy.description: Caisy is a headless CMS platform providing flexible content management, seamless integration with various devices and channels, and API access for structured content delivery.
Caisy.history: Caisy is a German headless CMS founded in 2019 and headquartered in Stuttgart. It raised a $340K seed round in 2022 from investor Waldegg and employs around four people. The platform was built to enable teams to create, collaborate, and publish content at speed with custom data models, live preview, version control, digital asset management, and multi-tenancy capabilities. It targets developers and content teams looking for a modern API-first CMS alternative to legacy systems.
Caisy.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. Caisy is a small legitimate German startup in the headless CMS space. GDPR compliance is emphasized as a key selling point given its German origin. No known data misuse, dark patterns, or security incidents reported.

CakePHP.description: CakePHP is an open-source web framework. It follows the model–view–controller (MVC) approach and is written in PHP.
CakePHP.history: CakePHP is an open-source PHP web framework first released in April 2005 by Michal Tatarynowicz, a Polish developer inspired by Ruby on Rails. The Cake Software Foundation was formed in December 2005 to manage the project. It follows the model-view-controller pattern and convention-over-configuration philosophy. CakePHP reached widespread adoption in the mid-2000s as one of the dominant PHP frameworks and has been actively maintained through major releases including CakePHP 3, 4, and 5, with the project remaining a respected choice for PHP applications.
CakePHP.summary_abuse: CakePHP is a well-established open-source project with a long track record and transparent governance under the Cake Software Foundation. No known abuse, malicious code, or data collection. The framework itself is a development tool and does not collect user data. Like all software, vulnerabilities have been discovered and patched responsibly over the years.

Caldera Forms.description: Caldera Forms is the free WordPress form builder plugin.
Caldera Forms.history: Caldera Forms was a free WordPress form builder plugin known for its drag-and-drop interface. Saturday Drive acquired Caldera Labs around 2019 and folded the product into its Ninja Forms ecosystem. On April 5, 2022, Caldera Forms was officially discontinued with no further updates, security patches, or bug fixes. The plugin had served over 20,000 active WordPress sites at its peak. Ninja Forms provided a migration tool to help former Caldera Forms users transition to its own platform.
Caldera Forms.summary_abuse: Caldera Forms is discontinued and no longer receives security updates, making any remaining installations a potential vulnerability. The product itself had no known intentional abuse history, but the deprecated status means known vulnerabilities will remain unpatched. Users are encouraged to migrate to alternative form builders immediately.

CalendarHero.description: CalendarHero (formerly Zoom.ai) is meeting scheduling software that helps you book meetings automatically.
CalendarHero.history: CalendarHero, originally launched as Zoom.ai in 2016, is a Canadian meeting scheduling automation tool that helps businesses manage appointments and calendar coordination. The product was rebranded to CalendarHero to better reflect its scheduling focus. It offers a free plan alongside paid Professional and Team tiers starting at $8/month. The platform integrates with major calendar systems and provides automated meeting setup, reminders, and time zone handling.
CalendarHero.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found for CalendarHero. The product is a niche scheduling tool with a small user base and no reported data misuse or dark patterns. As with any calendar integration tool, it requires access to calendar data which carries privacy implications, but no specific concerns have been reported.

CalendarWiz.description: CalendarWiz is an online scheduling calendar.
CalendarWiz.history: Advally is a digital advertising network and publisher monetization platform founded in 2016 and headquartered in Montreal, Canada. The company offers programmatic advertising, eCPM optimization, and ad layout services to website publishers.
CalendarWiz.summary_abuse: Advally is an ad network that serves programmatic advertising to publisher websites. Ad networks of this type can carry malvertising risk if ad quality controls are insufficient, as malicious ads may be injected through the programmatic supply chain. No specific public security incidents involving Advally have been documented, but the ad-tech category generally carries elevated malvertising risk.

Calendly.description: Calendly is an app for scheduling appointments, meetings, and events.
Calendly.history: Calendly was founded in 2013 by Tope Awotona, a Nigerian-born entrepreneur based in Atlanta, Georgia. Awotona invested his life savings of $200,000 to start the company. It grew to over 20 million users and became profitable before raising its first significant external funding. In January 2021, Calendly raised a $350 million Series B at a $3 billion valuation from OpenView and Iconiq Capital. The platform is widely adopted across sales, recruiting, and customer success teams globally.
Calendly.summary_abuse: Calendly has a clean reputation with no significant abuse incidents. As a scheduling tool, it embeds on websites and accesses calendar data, which carries inherent privacy considerations. The platform is SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant. Some privacy advocates note that Calendly embeds tracking code on pages where its scheduler is embedded, but no malicious behavior has been documented.

Calenso.description: Calenso is an online enterprise scheduling solution that helps companies in consulting-intensive industries organize appointments.
Calenso.history: Calenso was founded in 2016 in Sempach, Switzerland by three co-founders, and is currently headquartered in Rothenburg, Switzerland. The platform is a leading Swiss online scheduling solution serving over 10,000 customers including SMEs and large enterprises across financial services, retail, healthcare, and public administration. Calenso joined a trio merger with US-based companies to expand its scheduling capabilities. The product is built with a strong emphasis on Swiss data privacy standards and GDPR compliance.
Calenso.summary_abuse: No abuse history reported. Calenso is a Swiss-hosted scheduling platform with strong emphasis on GDPR compliance and data protection, consistent with Swiss privacy law standards. No known security incidents, dark patterns, or data misuse documented.

CaliberMind.description: CaliberMind is a marketing analytics platform designed for B2B marketers to optimize strategies and improve decision-making through data insights.
CaliberMind.history: CaliberMind was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Boulder, Colorado. The company provides a unified B2B marketing analytics platform that combines multi-touch attribution, marketing mix modeling, account-based marketing intelligence, and buyer journey analytics. CaliberMind raised a total of $22.7 million in funding including an $8 million Series A. Pricing starts at $1,000 per month based on CRM record volume and integrations. The platform serves go-to-market teams at B2B companies seeking data-driven revenue attribution.
CaliberMind.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. CaliberMind is a legitimate B2B analytics platform focused on marketing attribution. The product processes CRM and marketing data, which requires appropriate data governance practices, but no known misuse or privacy violations have been reported.

Call Cap.description: Call Cap is a tool used for tracking and monitoring phone calls.
Call Cap.history: Callcap was founded in 2001 and became the first call measurement company to provide real-time complete caller data and call recordings. Marchex, a NASDAQ-listed call analytics company, acquired Callcap on November 26, 2018, integrating it into its broader call tracking and analytics portfolio. The platform provides call monitoring, scoring, and measurement tools for businesses that rely on inbound phone leads. As part of Marchex, Callcap benefits from a public company parent with established compliance processes.
Call Cap.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. Callcap is a phone call monitoring tool owned by publicly traded Marchex. Call recording services carry inherent privacy considerations and must comply with applicable call recording consent laws, but no specific violations have been reported.

Callbell.description: Callbell is a web-based live chat solution designed to help businesses manage team collaboration via multiple communication channels.
Callbell.history: Callbell was founded in 2019 by Gianluca Bargelli and Carlo Morandi in Paris, France. The company builds an omnichannel customer support and sales platform integrating WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Instagram, Telegram, and web chat. As of 2024, Callbell has 31 employees and generated approximately $618,700 in annual revenue. The company is entirely self-funded with no external investors reported. Its product is focused on helping small to medium businesses manage multi-channel messaging from a single dashboard.
Callbell.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. Callbell is a small Paris-based startup with a clean track record. The platform processes messaging data across multiple channels including WhatsApp, which requires appropriate privacy handling. No known data misuse or security incidents reported.

Callgear.description: Callgear is a business communication platform that enables centralized management of voice calls and text messaging across organizational communication channels.
Callgear.history: Callgear is a cloud-based business telephony and communication platform marketed as deployable anywhere in the world. The company appears to have roots dating to 1999 and operates with a presence in Dubai. It offers virtual phone numbers in multiple countries, call routing, IVR systems, call recording, and real-time analytics. The platform targets SMEs and enterprises seeking flexible VoIP solutions without on-premise infrastructure.
Callgear.summary_abuse: Limited public information available about Callgear's ownership and compliance posture. As a VoIP provider operating in Dubai with global number availability, data sovereignty and call recording consent compliance should be verified for the target jurisdiction. No specific abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

CallPage.description: CallPage is a lead capture tool that automatically schedules callbacks and meetings with potential customers, enhancing lead management efficiency.
CallPage.history: CallPage was founded in 2015 by Sergey Butko, Andrew Tkachiv, and Ross Knap. The company is based in Poland and offers an AI-based VoIP lead generation product. It raised $6.19 million from investors including TDJ Pitango, Innovation Nest, Market One Capital, and Startup Wise Guys. Over 3,000 businesses including Audi, PWC, and Idea Bank use CallPage. The platform enables website visitors to request immediate callbacks within 28 seconds, helping convert website traffic into sales calls.
CallPage.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. CallPage is a legitimate Polish startup that provides callback widget technology. The service collects phone numbers from website visitors for callback purposes, requiring appropriate privacy disclosures, but no known data misuse or security incidents have been reported.

CallRail.description: CallRail is a service that tracks and manages your phone leads, helping businesses to determine which marketing campaigns are driving quality leads.
CallRail.history: CallRail was founded in 2011 by Georgia Tech graduates Andy Powell and Kevin Mann in Atlanta, Georgia. The company provides cloud-based call tracking and analytics software helping businesses measure which marketing campaigns drive phone calls. CallRail grew to over 180,000 customers and surpassed $100 million in annual recurring revenue, making it one of the most successful bootstrapped-to-VC-backed call analytics platforms. The company is headquartered at 100 Peachtree Street NW in Atlanta.
CallRail.summary_abuse: CallRail has a clean reputation as a legitimate call tracking and analytics provider used by marketing agencies and businesses. As a call recording platform, it must comply with two-party consent laws across US states. No known security incidents, data misuse, or abuse patterns have been reported.

CallRoot.description: CallRoot is a phone call tracking system designed for marketers to monitor, analyze, and optimize inbound calls.
CallRoot.history: CallRoot was founded in 2018 by Gaurav Sharma and is based in Newark, United States. The platform is an unfunded bootstrapped call tracking and dynamic number insertion tool designed for marketing teams and agencies. It integrates with Google Ads, Google Analytics, Facebook Ads, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Zapier, and Zoho, allowing marketers to attribute inbound phone calls to specific campaigns. The product remains a small niche competitor in the call tracking market.
CallRoot.summary_abuse: No abuse history found. CallRoot is a small bootstrapped call tracking tool. As it handles phone number data and call attribution for marketing, appropriate privacy disclosures are required, but no specific incidents of misuse have been documented.

CallTrackingMetrics.description: CallTrackingMetrics is a call tracking and marketing attribution solution for contact centers and agencies.
CallTrackingMetrics.history: CallTrackingMetrics (now CTM) was founded around 2009-2010 by husband-and-wife co-founders Todd and Laure Fisher. Based in the United States, the company provides call tracking, conversation intelligence, and marketing attribution software. The platform reached approximately $43.8 million in annual revenue with 30,000 customers. Plans range from Marketing Lite at $79/month up to Enterprise at $1,999/month, with transparent public pricing across all tiers.
CallTrackingMetrics.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. CallTrackingMetrics is a legitimate call tracking platform with transparent pricing and a long track record serving marketing agencies and businesses. Call recording features require compliance with consent laws, but no specific violations or incidents have been reported.

CamanJS.description: CamanJS is a JavaScript library designed for image editing directly on the browser, allowing manipulation of canvas elements.
CamanJS.history: CamanJS is a JavaScript and HTML5 canvas image manipulation library created by Ryan LeFevre (meltingice). It was designed to provide image editing capabilities including filters, adjustments, and canvas manipulation without external library dependencies. Written in CoffeeScript from version 3.0 onward, it supported both browser and NodeJS environments. The project was active in the early-to-mid 2010s but has since become unmaintained with the repository showing no recent commits.
CamanJS.summary_abuse: No abuse history. CamanJS is an MIT-licensed open source image processing library that is now effectively abandoned. The library itself has no network capabilities or data collection. Users should be aware of the unmaintained status and potential unpatched vulnerabilities before including it in production projects.

Campaign Monitor.description: Campaign Monitor is a global technology company that provides an email marketing platform.
Campaign Monitor.history: Campaign Monitor was founded in 2004 by Dave Greiner and Ben Richardson in Sydney, Australia. Originally a small email marketing tool for web designers, it grew to serve businesses of all sizes. In 2014, the company raised $250 million in VC funding from Insight Venture Partners. It subsequently acquired Emma, Sailthru, and Liveclicker to build a multi-brand email marketing portfolio. The entire portfolio was rebranded under the Marigold umbrella name around 2022-2023, with Campaign Monitor continuing as a distinct product within that group.
Campaign Monitor.summary_abuse: Campaign Monitor has a generally clean reputation as a mainstream email marketing platform. As with all email platforms, there are risks of misuse for spam if account verification processes are bypassed, but the company employs standard anti-spam practices. No major data breaches or intentional misuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Campflow.description: Campflow is a SaaS platform for event and membership management that provides online registration forms, participant tracking, communication tools, and financial reporting.
Campflow.history: Campflow is a German SaaS platform founded in 2021 by Niklas Weiss and Sebastian Walker in Freiburg im Breisgau, Germany. The product focuses on event management and membership administration for small nonprofit associations, youth organizations, and similar groups. Campflow GmbH is a registered German company providing tools for member management, event organization, accounting, and communication specifically designed for the German voluntary sector.
Campflow.summary_abuse: No abuse history found. Campflow is a very small German startup serving nonprofit organizations. GDPR compliance is important given its German base and the personal data of members it handles. No security incidents or misuse documented.

Candid.description: Candid is a visual commerce platform that integrates ecommerce with Instagram, enabling product discovery and streamlined shopping experiences.
Candid.history: AlloyUI was created in 2009 by Eduardo Lundgren and Nate Cavanaugh at Liferay, built on top of Yahoo's YUI3 JavaScript library with Bootstrap 3 for styling. It was Liferay Portal's UI framework for many years, providing a rich component library for enterprise portal applications. With the shift toward modern frontend tooling, Liferay deprecated AlloyUI in favor of Metal.js and later React-based components in Liferay 7.x. The repository is now archived and read-only on GitHub.
Candid.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents have been reported for AlloyUI. It is a deprecated enterprise UI library that was used internally by Liferay Portal. As an archived project with no new development, it presents no current abuse risk beyond the standard risk of using unmaintained dependencies.

Candid Themes Fairy.description: Fairy is a free and minimal WordPress blog theme by Candid Themes.
Candid Themes Fairy.history: Fairy is a free, minimal WordPress blog theme developed by Candid Themes, a team of expert WordPress theme developers. The theme is Gutenberg-ready, supports all WordPress blocks and patterns, and includes custom widgets for author, featured posts, and social icons. It is available on WordPress.org and the Candid Themes website. A premium Pro version with additional customization options is also offered. The theme is designed for bloggers and content creators seeking a clean, minimal design.
Candid Themes Fairy.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Fairy is a GPL-licensed free WordPress theme from Candid Themes. WordPress themes available on WordPress.org undergo review, providing a baseline level of quality assurance. No malicious code, hidden data collection, or security issues have been reported.

CanJS.description: CanJS is an MIT-licensed client-side JavaScript framework used for building maintainable and performant web applications.
CanJS.history: CanJS is an MIT-licensed client-side JavaScript MVC framework originally developed by Bitovi. It was designed to help developers build complex, high-performance web applications with observable data, live-binding templates, and routing. The framework has gone through multiple major versions and continues to be maintained by the Bitovi team, though its popularity has declined with the dominance of React, Vue, and Angular in the JavaScript framework ecosystem.
CanJS.summary_abuse: No abuse history. CanJS is a legitimate open-source JavaScript framework maintained by Bitovi under the MIT license. The library itself does not collect data or send information externally. It has a smaller but dedicated user community.

Canny.description: Canny is a cloud-based solution that helps small to large businesses collect, analyse, prioritise and track user feedback to make informed product decisions.
Canny.history: Canny was founded in 2017 and provides a customer feedback management platform that helps product teams collect, organize, and prioritize user feedback. The product is used by companies including Notion, Loom, ClickUp, and other well-known SaaS businesses. It offers a free plan and paid tiers starting at $99/month with fully transparent public pricing. Canny is bootstrapped or lightly funded and remains an independently operated US-based company with a focused product niche.
Canny.summary_abuse: No abuse history found. Canny is a legitimate feedback management tool used by reputable SaaS companies. The product handles user feedback data on behalf of its customers, requiring appropriate privacy controls. No known security incidents or data misuse reported.

Canopy Connect.description: Canopy Connect is a tool that enables instant collection of insurance information for verification, analysis, or onboarding processes.
Canopy Connect.history: Canopy Connect was founded in 2020 and is based in Beaverton, Oregon. The company provides an insurance data intake platform that enables insurance agents to securely collect and verify insurance information from clients. Clients can share verified coverages, premiums, limits, deductibles, and declaration pages directly with agents, enabling faster and more accurate quoting. The platform serves both insurance agencies and developers via API. Additional quote points are billed at $3 each with a usage-based model.
Canopy Connect.summary_abuse: No abuse history found. Canopy Connect operates in the insurtech space handling sensitive insurance policy information. The platform is designed specifically for authorized insurance agents to collect client insurance data with client consent. No known data misuse or security incidents reported.

Canto.description: Canto is a digital asset management solution.
Canto.history: Canto was founded in 1990 and was an early innovator in the digital asset management industry. Headquartered in Atlanta with offices in Berlin and Sydney, Canto serves over 2,500 customers globally. The company has raised $85 million from investors JMI Equity and Atrium Capital. With 201-500 employees and estimated annual revenue of $50-250 million, Canto is one of the established players in the DAM market, recognized by Gartner. Its platform integrates content management, collaboration, and distribution workflows.
Canto.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history found. Canto is a long-established digital asset management vendor with enterprise customers. The company operates with appropriate data governance practices. No known security incidents, data misuse, or malicious behavior has been reported.

Cantrip.description: Cantrip is a tool that uses artificial intelligence to generate personalized websites.
Cantrip.history: Cantrip is an AI-powered website builder designed for users who want to create websites without dealing with plugins, complex designs, or settings. The platform uses AI to simplify site creation with drag-and-drop customization, pre-designed themes, built-in analytics, and e-commerce integration. It offers a free plan and a premium tier at approximately $9.98/month. Company and founding details are not prominently disclosed, suggesting it is a very early-stage startup.
Cantrip.summary_abuse: Limited public information available about Cantrip. The platform generates AI content for websites, carrying an elevated ai_generated_likelihood signal. No specific abuse incidents documented, but as a very small and undisclosed company, independent security and compliance verification is not possible.

Canva.description: Canva is an online graphic design platform that allows users to create various visual content using pre-designed templates and a library of elements.
Canva.history: Canva was founded in 2013 by Melanie Perkins, Cliff Obrecht, and Cameron Adams in Perth, Australia. The concept grew from Fusion Books, a yearbook design tool Melanie and Cliff started in 2007 while studying at the University of Western Australia. Canva launched publicly after a successful pitch to Silicon Valley investors and grew rapidly to over 100 million monthly active users. In September 2021, Canva raised funding at a $40 billion valuation. The platform democratized graphic design with a drag-and-drop interface usable without design expertise.
Canva.summary_abuse: Canva has a strong safety record overall. The platform has been involved in one notable security incident in 2019 when approximately 139 million user records were accessed in a data breach. Canva responded promptly, notified affected users, and implemented improvements. The platform's content moderation capabilities are regularly tested given its massive scale. No ongoing systemic abuse issues documented.

Canvas LMS.description: Canvas LMS is a web-based learning management system, or LMS.
Canvas LMS.history: Canvas LMS was created by Instructure, founded in 2008 by Brian Whitmer and Devlin Daley, graduates of Brigham Young University. Canvas launched in 2011 under the AGPL open-source license and rapidly became the leading LMS in North America. Instructure went public on the NYSE in 2015 and was taken private by Thoma Bravo in 2020. The platform serves over 6 million concurrent users and is used by thousands of K-12 districts, higher education institutions, and corporate training programs worldwide.
Canvas LMS.summary_abuse: Canvas LMS has a strong institutional reputation and is used by major universities, school districts, and Fortune 500 companies. Instructure is a publicly accountable company with robust data governance. No significant abuse or data misuse incidents have been publicly documented. FERPA compliance for US educational institutions is a standard feature of the platform.

CanvasJS.description: CanvasJS charts is a data visualisation library that runs across multiple devices and browsers.
CanvasJS.history: CanvasJS Charts is an HTML5 and JavaScript charting library developed by Fenopix Technologies, a company founded in 2012 in Bengaluru, India. The library delivers 10x performance compared to traditional SVG-based charting libraries by rendering charts on an HTML5 canvas element. It supports over 30 chart types including line, column, bar, spline, area, pie, doughnut, and stock charts. Licensing is perpetual or annual subscription starting at $399, with a free tier available for non-commercial use requiring a credit link.
CanvasJS.summary_abuse: No abuse history. CanvasJS is a legitimate client-side data visualization library from an Indian company. The library is self-hosted by users in their web applications and does not make external network calls or collect user data. No security incidents or malicious behavior reported.

Canvy.description: Canvy is an online tool designed for fine art painters and poster designers to enhance the promotion and presentation of their artwork online.
Canvy.history: Canvy was founded in 2020 in Denmark as an online tool for fine art painters and poster designers to virtually stage and promote their artwork online. The platform launched with room mockup visualization features and a website builder for artists. No notable ownership changes or controversies since founding.
Canvy.summary_abuse: Canvy is a niche visual art portfolio and room mockup tool with no notable abuse signal. The service is used almost exclusively by artists and designers to present artwork. There are no documented cases of the platform being abused for phishing, spam, or fraudulent storefronts. Abuse risk is very low given the curated, subscription-based nature of the product.

Canyon.description: Canyon is a tool that enables JavaScript coverage collection and real-time report generation through Babel configuration, addressing coverage-gathering challenges in end-to-end testing.
Canyon.history: Canyon is the official mobile app from Canyon Bicycles GmbH, the German direct-to-consumer bicycle brand founded in 1985 by Roman Arnold in Koblenz, Germany. The Canyon app launched in 2022 to provide bike owners with digital garage, assembly guidance, maintenance support, and GPS tracking features. No notable controversies or ownership changes since launch.
Canyon.summary_abuse: The Canyon app is a companion application for Canyon bike owners with no notable abuse signal. It is a brand-specific utility app with limited public-facing surface. There are no documented incidents of fraud, phishing, or misuse associated with this app.

Capacity.description: Capacity is a live chat system for ecommerce websites, formerly known as Needle.
Capacity.history: Capacity was founded in 2017 by David Karandish and Chris Sims as an AI-powered knowledge management and support automation platform. The company emerged from the Equity.com incubator and raised substantial funding including $92 million to expand its contact center AI capabilities. Capacity has grown to serve over 20,000 companies.
Capacity.summary_abuse: Capacity is an enterprise AI knowledge management and support automation platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by businesses to automate customer support and internal knowledge sharing. There are no documented cases of the service being exploited for spam, phishing, or fraud.

Capsule CRM.description: Capsule CRM is an online customer relationship management system for managing contacts, sales opportunities, and basic business interactions.
Capsule CRM.history: Capsule CRM was founded in 2009 by Duncan Stockdill, Phil Haines, and Wendy Rule in Manchester, UK, under the parent company Zestia. It was originally released under the name Javelin before being renamed to Capsule in 2010. The company has been bootstrapped from the start and grown steadily, celebrating 15 years in business by 2024 with no external investment or acquisitions.
Capsule CRM.summary_abuse: Capsule CRM is a legitimate SMB-focused CRM with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by small and medium-sized businesses for contact and sales management. There are no documented instances of Capsule being abused for phishing, spam campaigns, or fraudulent activity.

Captain Up.description: Captain Up is a service that enhances user engagement and retention by utilising gamification, social, and communication tools to maximise user engagement and lifetime value.
Captain Up.history: Captain Up was founded in 2013 in Israel as a gamification platform for customer engagement and loyalty, initially targeting general web businesses before pivoting to focus on the iGaming, sports betting, and casino sectors. The company developed its Metagame concept using game mechanics and behavioral psychology to drive user retention. No notable acquisition or rebranding events.
Captain Up.summary_abuse: Captain Up is a gamification and loyalty platform primarily serving the iGaming sector. The platform itself is not directly associated with abuse, though its primary market of online gambling and sports betting carries inherent regulatory risk in various jurisdictions. No documented incidents of the platform being exploited for fraud or phishing.

Captch Me.history: Captch Me was founded in 2011 by Jérémie Taglia in France as a CAPTCHA alternative that replaces bot-detection challenges with short survey questions, monetizing the CAPTCHA display as an ad unit.
Captch Me.summary_abuse: Captch Me is a CAPTCHA-as-advertising service that replaces standard CAPTCHA challenges with targeted survey questions to collect declarative user data. This data collection model raises privacy concerns as user answers are gathered without always explicit informed consent. The service has moderate risk as the embedded survey collects data on end users of third-party sites.

Captify.description: Captify is a platform that offers search retargeting by combining keyword-based search marketing with the reach of display advertising.
Captify.history: Captify was founded in 2011 in London as a search intelligence advertising technology company. The company built one of the largest onsite search datasets outside major walled gardens by aggregating search data from millions of publisher websites. In September 2025, Verve Group SE acquired Captify to strengthen its demand-side advertising platform and gain access to Captify's unique first-party search data.
Captify.summary_abuse: Captify is an ad-tech company whose core business involves collecting and processing large volumes of consumer search data from across millions of websites for programmatic advertising targeting. This data collection model inherently involves tracking user behavior at scale, raising privacy concerns. The company operates primarily as a B2B advertising intelligence platform and is not directly associated with phishing or fraud, though its data-intensive model warrants scrutiny under GDPR.

Captivate.fm.description: Captivate.fm is a podcast hosting and analytics platform that provides tools for creating, hosting, and distributing podcasts.
Captivate.fm.history: Captivate.fm was founded in 2018 in the UK as an unlimited podcast hosting platform. It became the world's first IAB Tech Lab 2.2 Certified podcast host, establishing credibility for analytics accuracy. The platform has grown to serve independent and professional podcasters globally with a focus on growth-oriented features and team collaboration.
Captivate.fm.summary_abuse: Captivate.fm is a podcast hosting platform with minimal documented abuse signal. Like most media hosting platforms it could theoretically be used to distribute misleading audio content, but there are no notable documented incidents of fraud or abuse. The subscription-based model with IAB certification suggests a legitimate operation oriented toward professional podcasters.

Capturly.description: Capturly is an online analytics platform for businesses to analyze user behavior, identify issues, improve conversion rates, and enhance sales.
Capturly.history: Capturly is a website analytics and heatmap platform founded in Hungary. The tool offers session recording, heatmaps, conversion funnels, and event analytics within a single interface. The platform offers a WordPress plugin and competes with tools like Hotjar and Microsoft Clarity.
Capturly.summary_abuse: Capturly is a website session recording and heatmap analytics tool with no documented abuse signal. The service collects behavioral data from website visitors on behalf of its customers. Like all session recording tools, improper implementation could capture sensitive user data, but this is a risk inherent to the category rather than specific to Capturly.

CAPYS.description: CAPYS is a CRM system designed to support builders in Brazil by managing client relationships, projects, and operational data.
CAPYS.history: CAPYS is a Brazilian CRM system designed specifically to support construction industry builders, helping manage clients, contracts, and project workflows.
CAPYS.summary_abuse: CAPYS is a niche B2B CRM for Brazilian construction companies. No documented abuse incidents.

Caramella.description: Caramella is a blog creation tool from Saudi Arabia that enables blog development and content management.
Caramella.history: Caramella is a Saudi Arabian blog creation and content management tool available at caramel.la. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
Caramella.summary_abuse: Caramella is a small blog creation platform with a free tier. Blog platforms with free tiers can occasionally be used to host spam or scam content, though no specific documented abuse incidents exist for Caramella.

Carbon Ads.description: Carbon Ads is an ad tech company, that connects advertisers to users through targeted verticals called Circles.
Carbon Ads.history: Carbon Ads was founded in 2010 by Adelle Charles and Joshua Smibert as a curated native advertising network serving developer and designer websites. The network is operated by BuySellAds and focuses on respectful, non-intrusive advertising on high-quality developer-centric sites. Carbon Ads has served hundreds of developer-focused websites and gained recognition in the developer community as a less intrusive alternative to standard ad networks.
Carbon Ads.summary_abuse: Carbon Ads is a curated developer-targeted advertising network operated by BuySellAds. It manually vets publishers and advertisers to maintain quality standards. As an ad network, it inherently involves some level of cross-site tracking, but Carbon Ads is generally considered more privacy-respecting than mainstream ad tech. No notable abuse incidents have been documented.

Carbonfact.description: Carbonfact is a platform for fashion brands to manage their carbon emissions. It enables the measurement, reporting, and reduction of emissions in the apparel, textile, leather, and luxury sectors.
Carbonfact.history: Carbonfact was founded in 2021 in Paris, France by Martin Daniel, Marc Laurent, and Romain Champourlier, all with data science and product engineering backgrounds from companies like Airbnb and JobTeaser. The company was backed by Y Combinator and raised $15 million in Series A funding in 2024. It focuses on carbon accounting for apparel and footwear brands.
Carbonfact.summary_abuse: Carbonfact is a specialized B2B carbon accounting platform for the fashion and apparel industry with no abuse signal. The service handles environmental reporting data for brands like New Balance and Carhartt. There are no documented instances of fraud or misuse associated with this platform.

Carbonmade.description: Carbonmade is an online portfolio platform that provides tools and templates for creating and showcasing digital portfolios.
Carbonmade.history: Carbonmade was founded in 2005 in Chicago, Illinois as an online portfolio platform for creatives. It has remained an independent bootstrapped product throughout its history.
Carbonmade.summary_abuse: Carbonmade is a portfolio hosting platform with a free tier. Portfolio platforms with free public pages have been occasionally used to host scam or phishing content, but Carbonmade's focus on professional creative portfolios means this is a low-risk usage pattern.

CarChat24.description: CarChat24 is a live chat solution built specifically for the automotive industry to facilitate real-time customer engagement and lead generation.
CarChat24.history: CarChat24 was founded in June 2007 as a live chat and managed chat services provider specifically for automotive dealerships in the United States. The platform was built by automotive industry veterans and offers both software and fully managed 24/7 human operator services for car dealer websites. The company has remained independently focused on the automotive vertical.
CarChat24.summary_abuse: CarChat24 is a vertically-focused live chat solution for automotive dealerships with no notable documented abuse. The platform facilitates conversations between potential car buyers and dealership staff. There are no reports of the service being exploited for fraud or consumer deception at a platform level.

Cardina.description: Cardina is a screen-sharing and cobrowsing tool that enables agents to see, draw on, and guide a customer’s screen during phone or live chat interactions without requiring customer actions.
Cardina.history: Cardina (formerly known as Cohere) was founded in 2020 in New York as a co-browsing and customer support platform enabling agents to see and interact with customers' screens. The company was acquired by Ramp in June 2023, bringing its screen-sharing and customer support capabilities into Ramp's fintech ecosystem.
Cardina.summary_abuse: Cardina is a co-browsing and customer support tool with no notable abuse signal. The service enables support agents to view and interact with customer screens with permission, which is standard for enterprise support workflows. There are no documented incidents of the platform being exploited for unauthorized screen access or fraud.

CareCart.description: CareCart is a smart app to recover big value carts on all sizes of shopify stores.
CareCart.history: CareCart is a Shopify app suite focused on abandoned cart recovery launched around 2018. The main product, Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery, helps Shopify merchants recover lost sales through automated email marketing, web push notifications, and discount popups. The platform claims to serve over 32,000 Shopify businesses globally.
CareCart.summary_abuse: CareCart is a Shopify-ecosystem abandoned cart recovery tool with no notable platform-level abuse signal. The app collects customer email addresses and browsing data on behalf of Shopify merchants for remarketing purposes. Its use of email popups and push notifications could contribute to unwanted marketing if merchants do not follow opt-in best practices.

CareCart Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery.description: Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery by CareCart is a service that helps ecommerce businesses recover lost sales by sending automated reminders to customers who abandon their shopping carts.
CareCart Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery.history: CareCart Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery is a Shopify app by Care Cart (carecart.io) that specializes in abandoned checkout recovery using email campaigns and push notifications. The app was launched around 2018 and rebranded to include the Cartly name. It serves over 32,000 Shopify stores globally.
CareCart Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery.summary_abuse: Cartly Abandoned Cart Recovery is a Shopify app for automated email and push notification remarketing. The tool collects shopper contact details for re-engagement purposes. No platform-level abuse has been documented, though as with any email marketing tool, individual merchants bear responsibility for compliance with anti-spam regulations.

CareCart Sales Pop Up.description: CareCart Sales Pop Up is a stock countdown timer, recent sales notifications, live sales pop up widget.
CareCart Sales Pop Up.history: CareCart Sales Pop Up is a social proof and urgency notification Shopify app by Care Cart. It displays recent purchase notifications and visitor counts to create FOMO-driven conversion pressure. Part of the broader CareCart app suite launched around 2018.
CareCart Sales Pop Up.summary_abuse: CareCart Sales Pop Up is a social proof widget that displays fabricated or real-time purchase notifications on Shopify stores. Social proof popups are a known dark pattern category when merchants display fake or manipulated activity data to deceive shoppers. The app itself does not explicitly advertise fake data features, but the category has a history of encouraging deceptive urgency tactics.

CareCloud.description: CareCloud is a platform that provides healthcare providers with a set of tools designed to enhance clinical workflows, improve patient outcomes, and streamline daily business operations.
CareCloud.history: CareCloud Corporation was founded in 2009 in Miami, Florida by Albert Santalo as a cloud-based healthcare revenue cycle management platform. In 2020, MTBC (Medical Transcription Billing Corporation, founded 1999) acquired CareCloud and in 2021 renamed itself CareCloud Inc., making CareCloud the company's primary brand and public identity.
CareCloud.summary_abuse: CareCloud is a regulated healthcare SaaS platform with HIPAA compliance requirements and no notable abuse signal. The platform handles protected health information under strict regulatory controls, and its healthcare vertical focus means fraudsters do not typically target it.

Caremerge.description: Caremerge is a provider of resident engagement, family communication, and EHR/eMAR solutions for the senior living industry.
Caremerge.history: Caremerge was founded around 2012 as a care coordination and engagement platform for the senior living industry. The company raised $4 million from Cambia Health Solutions and other investors to expand its care coordination capabilities. The platform has since evolved and is now known as Icon, providing cloud-based EHR and engagement solutions for senior living communities.
Caremerge.summary_abuse: Caremerge is a HIPAA-compliant senior living EHR and engagement platform with no documented abuse signal. The service handles sensitive health information for elderly residents and their families. No security incidents or misuse cases have been publicly reported.

Cargo.description: Cargo is a professional site building platform for designers and artists.
Cargo.history: Cargo (Cargo Collective) was founded in 2009 as a professional website builder specifically for designers and artists. The platform built a reputation in the creative community for visually rich portfolios and design-focused templates. It competes with Squarespace and Adobe Portfolio in the creative portfolio space.
Cargo.summary_abuse: Cargo is a design-focused portfolio website builder with no notable abuse documentation. The platform is used by graphic designers, artists, and creative agencies to showcase professional portfolios. There are no reports of systematic exploitation for phishing or fraud.

CargoServer.description: CargoServer is a platform that enables secure data transfer.
CargoServer.history: CargoServer is a Swiss managed file transfer product providing secure, encrypted file transport as a software or virtual appliance. The product is positioned as enterprise-grade secure data transfer infrastructure with TLS 256-bit encryption, built-in malware scanning, and high availability clustering. It is developed and maintained in Switzerland, with Swiss quality as a core brand value.
CargoServer.summary_abuse: CargoServer is an enterprise managed file transfer solution marketed to businesses needing secure data transport. It is an infrastructure-level product with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal. The product's security-first design including encrypted transfers and malware scanning actively works against potential misuse.

Carrd.description: Carrd is a platform for building simple, responsive, one-page sites.
Carrd.history: Carrd was created in 2016 by AJ (a solo developer) as a simple, free, fully responsive one-page website builder. The product became widely popular, especially among indie hackers, social media creators, and link-in-bio use cases. Carrd remained a solo bootstrapped project and grew to serve millions of sites at exceptionally affordable pricing starting at $19/year for paid plans.
Carrd.summary_abuse: Carrd's free tier and simple one-page site creation has made it a popular tool for creating phishing pages, scam landing pages, and fake promotional sites due to the ease of deployment and free custom domain support on paid plans. Security researchers have documented its use in social engineering campaigns and credential harvesting. The vendor has an AUP but is a solo-operated product with limited abuse response capacity.

Carro.description: Carro connects participating Shopify stores together to enable cross-store selling or the ability for like-minded partners to directly sell each other products without the need for inventory, managing returns, or minimum order quantities.
Carro.history: Carro is a Shopify influencer marketing and brand collaboration platform founded around 2016. The company enables Shopify merchants to identify influencers from existing customers, manage product gifting and collaborations, and facilitate cross-brand partnerships. Carro is venture-backed and has built a network of over 5,000 brands and 2.6 million influencers.
Carro.summary_abuse: Carro is an influencer marketing and brand collaboration tool for Shopify merchants with no notable documented abuse. The platform facilitates product gifting and creator partnerships in a commercial context. There are no reports of the platform being exploited for fraud or deceptive marketing at a systematic level.

Carrot quest.description: Carrot quest is a customer engagement and communication platform that aids businesses in improving interactions with users and customers through targeted messaging, in-app messaging, live chat, email campaigns, and user behavior tracking.
Carrot quest.history: Carrot quest was founded in 2014 in Perm, Russia as an eCRM and conversational marketing automation platform. The company focuses on tracking visitor behavior and automating engagement through live chat, email, and chatbots primarily for the Russian-language SaaS and e-commerce market. It has approximately 21 employees as of 2024.
Carrot quest.summary_abuse: Carrot quest is a customer messaging and marketing automation platform based in Russia. As a Russian-based SaaS, it may face reduced scrutiny and different regulatory oversight compared to Western equivalents, and data sovereignty concerns apply for non-Russian customers. No specific abuse incidents have been documented, but Russian-origin tracking and automation tools warrant elevated caution regarding data handling.

Cart Functionality.description: Websites that have a shopping cart or checkout page, either using a known ecommerce platform or a custom solution.
Cart Functionality.history: Cart Functionality is a Wappalyzer detection category representing any website that exposes a shopping cart or checkout page, whether built on a known ecommerce platform or a custom solution. It is not a standalone product.
Cart Functionality.summary_abuse: Cart Functionality is a generic Wappalyzer detection label, not a deployable product. Fake storefront scams will naturally be detected under this label, but the label itself carries no specific abuse signal beyond what applies to ecommerce sites generally.

Cart.com.description: Cart.com is an ecommerce platform built for high volume online stores and complex products with features such as multi-store management.
Cart.com.history: Cart.com was founded in October 2020 by Jim Jacobsen and Omair Tariq as a unified commerce and logistics platform for B2C and B2B brands. The company raised $240 million in a February 2022 funding round with Citi Ventures, Visa, and other investors, bringing total capital raised to $380 million. Cart.com grew rapidly through acquisitions and serves brands generating $1M–$100M in annual revenue.
Cart.com.summary_abuse: Cart.com is an enterprise unified commerce and fulfillment platform serving established retail brands with no notable abuse signal. The platform requires enterprise contracts and serves established businesses. No documented incidents of fraud or misuse have been associated with the platform.

Cart.js.description: Cart.js is a very small open-source Javascript library that makes the addition of powerful Ajax cart functionality to your Shopify theme a breeze.
Cart.js.history: Cart.js is an open-source JavaScript library by Disco Labs designed to add Ajax cart management to Shopify themes. It was released around 2013 on GitHub. The library provides a clean abstraction over Shopify's Cart API for theme developers. Repository activity has largely ceased.
Cart.js.summary_abuse: Cart.js is a client-side open-source JavaScript library for Shopify cart management with no abuse signal. It is a developer utility with no consumer-facing backend surface. There are no documented cases of the library being used maliciously.

Carta.description: Carta is an equity management platform.
Carta.history: Carta was founded in 2012 by Henry Ward and Manu Kumar as eShares, focused on digitizing paper stock certificates. The company rebranded to Carta in November 2017 as it expanded into full equity management, cap table management, 409A valuations, and private market data. Carta raised $1.16 billion from Andreessen Horowitz, Union Square Ventures, Spark Capital, and others, reaching a $7.4 billion valuation. In 2024, Carta faced controversy when CEO Henry Ward attempted to sell a secondaries business in a way that customers alleged was a breach of trust, leading to significant customer and investor pushback.
Carta.summary_abuse: Carta is a trusted equity management platform used by over 50,000 private companies. The platform handles sensitive cap table and equity data for startups and investors. In 2024, a controversy arose when Carta was accused of using client equity data to pitch its own secondaries business to potential investors without clear consent, raising data usage concerns. This incident led to significant reputational damage and customer departures. No fraud or phishing abuse has been associated with the platform itself.

CartBot.description: CartBot is a tool that automatically adds products or gifts to a customer's cart based on predefined conditions.
CartBot.history: CartBot is a Shopify app that automatically adds products or gifts to a customer's cart based on conditions such as cart value. No public founding year or significant ownership changes are documented.
CartBot.summary_abuse: CartBot is a Shopify cart automation app with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's app ecosystem and has no consumer-facing content hosting features.

Cartera.description: Cartera is an ecommerce system that enables businesses to manage online sales, product listings, and transactions.
Cartera.history: Cartera was founded in 2005 as Mall Networks, building custom shopping portals. The company rebranded to Cartera Commerce in 2010 after surpassing $100 million in sales through loyalty programs. A 2011 merger with Vesdia expanded its airline partnerships. In 2017, Rakuten Rewards (formerly Ebates) acquired Cartera, integrating it into Rakuten's global loyalty ecosystem. Cartera now powers loyalty programs for major US financial institutions including USAA and Barclays, and airlines like Delta, United, American, Alaska, and Southwest.
Cartera.summary_abuse: Cartera is a B2B loyalty rewards platform serving major banks and airlines with no documented consumer-facing abuse. The platform processes large volumes of purchase transaction data for loyalty program tracking. As a Rakuten subsidiary serving institutional clients, it operates under rigorous compliance requirements. No fraud or security incidents have been publicly associated with the platform.

CartFlows.description: CartFlows is a sales funnel builder for WordPress.
CartFlows.history: CartFlows was launched in 2019 as a WordPress and WooCommerce sales funnel builder plugin by Sujay Pawar and Adam Preiser. The plugin replaced the standard WooCommerce checkout with conversion-optimized funnel pages and one-click upsells. It was developed by the same team behind Astra, one of the most popular WordPress themes. CartFlows serves over 200,000 stores and positions itself as the WordPress alternative to SaaS funnel tools like ClickFunnels.
CartFlows.summary_abuse: CartFlows is a WordPress/WooCommerce sales funnel plugin with no notable documented abuse at the platform level. As a self-hosted plugin, security depends on the individual WordPress installation's hygiene. The tool's upsell and checkout-optimization features are standard e-commerce functionality. No systematic exploitation of the plugin for fraudulent purposes has been documented.

Cartful.description: Cartful is a customer-focused service that assists online shoppers in locating suitable products on a website.
Cartful.history: Cartful is a guided selling and product discovery SaaS for e-commerce websites. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Cartful.summary_abuse: Cartful is a product guidance SaaS for online stores with no notable abuse signal. It serves merchants for product discovery and has no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

CartHook.description: CartHook is a solution for creating post-purchase upsell offers on Shopify.
CartHook.history: CartHook was founded around 2015 as a Shopify post-purchase upsell app enabling one-click checkout upsells. The app was later acquired by Shop Circle, which operates a portfolio of Shopify apps. CartHook specializes in helping merchants maximize average order value through post-purchase offer funnels.
CartHook.summary_abuse: CartHook is a Shopify post-purchase upsell app with no documented abuse. The platform facilitates standard e-commerce upsell mechanics. No incidents of the app being exploited for fraudulent purposes have been reported.

CartKit.description: CartKit build apps from fuss-free multi-channel marketing automation and campaigns to social proof popups and user session recording.
CartKit.history: CartKit is a developer of multiple Shopify e-commerce apps including Consistent Cart, Sales Pop social proof popups, and Pop Convert email capture tools. The company builds tools focused on cart recovery, email collection, and conversion rate optimization for Shopify merchants. CartKit apps collectively have tens of thousands of reviews on the Shopify App Store.
CartKit.summary_abuse: CartKit builds Shopify apps for cart recovery and social proof. Its Sales Pop app displays recent purchase notifications which can be configured to show either real or artificially-inflated activity data, a practice that borders on deceptive social proof. No systematic fraud has been documented but the category carries above-average dark pattern risk.

CARTO Analytics.description: Carto is a cloud-based spatial analytics platform that enables analysis and development with spatial data at scale.
CARTO Analytics.history: CARTO (originally CartoDB) was founded in 2012 in Madrid by Javier de la Torre and Sergio Alvarez following their work in conservation and environmental mapping. It raised $7M in 2014 and $23M Series B in 2015. In 2019 a new CEO was appointed and the company acquired Geographica. CARTO now has offices in New York, Madrid, and Seville serving 2,500+ customers including Mastercard and Vodafone.
CARTO Analytics.summary_abuse: CARTO is a cloud-based spatial analytics platform used by enterprises. It has no meaningful public-facing abuse surface and no documented abuse incidents.

CARTO Data Observatory.description: Carto Data Observatory is a platform that provides access to thousands of public and premium datasets from curated source.
CARTO Data Observatory.history: CARTO Data Observatory is a spatial data marketplace developed by CARTO, launched as a product around 2020 with EU Horizon 2020 funding. It aggregates thousands of public and commercial spatial datasets for use in location intelligence workflows.
CARTO Data Observatory.summary_abuse: CARTO Data Observatory is a data marketplace for spatial analytics. It provides access to licensed datasets and is used by enterprise clients. No meaningful abuse surface or documented incidents.

Cartpanda.description: Cartpanda is a platform for selling physical and digital products online, previously known as CartX.
Cartpanda.history: Cartpanda was founded in 2020 in Brazil as a one-click checkout and e-commerce platform targeting Brazilian digital entrepreneurs and content creators. The company was backed by Y Combinator and grew to an active community of sellers, generating R$100 million in revenue in 2024. International orders represent over 30% of transactions. Cartpanda offers a multi-currency checkout supporting 180+ currencies.
Cartpanda.summary_abuse: Cartpanda is a Brazilian e-commerce checkout platform used by digital entrepreneurs and creators. Digital product platforms in Brazil have faced elevated fraud risks including chargebacks and fake storefronts selling low-quality or non-existent digital goods. Cartpanda's easy signup and digital product focus makes it somewhat susceptible to use by bad actors creating fake digital product funnels, though no major systematic abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

CartPops.description: CartPops is a conversion-optimized AJAX fly-out cart solution for WooCommerce that replaces the traditional cart page and is compatible with any theme.
CartPops.history: CartPops is a WooCommerce AJAX cart plugin offering a conversion-optimized fly-out cart. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
CartPops.summary_abuse: CartPops is a WooCommerce cart plugin with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a merchant-side tool with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

CartRocket.description: CartRocket is a tool for cart abandonment analytics, providing insights into dropped transactions to improve recovery strategies.
CartRocket.history: CartRocket is a cart abandonment analytics tool. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
CartRocket.summary_abuse: CartRocket is a cart analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a merchant analytics backend with no consumer-facing features.

Carts Guru.description: Carts Guru is the all-in-one marketing automation tool for ecommerce stores.
Carts Guru.history: Carts Guru was founded in 2017 by David Laloum and Maxime Pruvost, initially as an abandoned cart recovery tool for e-merchants. By 2019 it evolved into a full multichannel marketing automation platform for e-commerce supporting email, SMS, and Facebook Messenger. The company has grown to 48 employees and serves over 1,500 e-commerce customers.
Carts Guru.summary_abuse: Carts Guru is an e-commerce marketing automation platform with no notable documented abuse. The service sends automated marketing messages on behalf of merchants, which requires proper opt-in consent from recipients. As an email and SMS marketing tool, individual merchant misuse for spam is a category risk but no platform-level abuse has been documented.

CartStack.description: CartStack is a SaaS solution that allows any company with an ecommerce site or reservation system to increase revenue through reminding/encouraging consumers to return to their abandoned cart and complete their purchase.
CartStack.history: CartStack is an abandoned cart and visitor recovery email solution that integrates with major e-commerce platforms including Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and Miva. The platform focuses on multi-channel recovery via email, SMS, and browser push notifications. CartStack is compatible with virtually all e-commerce platforms and claims to recover 15% or more of abandoned carts.
CartStack.summary_abuse: CartStack is an abandoned cart recovery platform with no notable abuse signal. The service captures shopper email addresses for remarketing purposes, which is standard e-commerce practice. As an email marketing tool, its use for spam depends on individual merchant compliance with CAN-SPAM and GDPR regulations.

Cartum.description: Cartum is a platform offering essential tools for launching and managing online stores.
Cartum.history: Cartum (formerly Horoshop) is a Ukrainian all-in-one e-commerce platform for creating and managing online stores. The platform provides product catalogs, checkout, SEO capabilities, and marketing integrations. It supports digital product sales and operates with subscription plans starting at $15/month.
Cartum.summary_abuse: Cartum is a Ukrainian e-commerce platform with no notable documented abuse. The service is used primarily by small and medium-sized businesses to create online stores. No security incidents or systematic misuse cases have been publicly reported.

Cartylabs.description: Cartylabs is a Shopify application that provides a fast checkout experience and enables merchants to implement upsell and cross-sell features directly within the shopping cart.
Cartylabs.history: Cartylabs is a Shopify app providing in-cart upsell and cross-sell features with fast checkout. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Cartylabs.summary_abuse: Cartylabs is a Shopify cart upsell app with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's merchant ecosystem with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

Carussel.description: Carussel is a dealer web toolkit designed to streamline the online presence and digital operations of automotive dealerships.
Carussel.history: Carussel is a dealer web toolkit providing automotive dealerships with website and digital operations tooling. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Carussel.summary_abuse: Carussel is a B2B SaaS platform for automotive dealerships with no documented abuse signal. Its closed-vendor nature and dealership-focused audience significantly limit exposure to typical web fraud patterns.

CarWeb.description: CarWeb is a tailored software solution designed to support the full used car lifecycle, developed by industry professionals to address operational, management, and transactional needs within the automotive resale process.
CarWeb.history: CarWeb (CarweB Limited) was founded in 2007 in Tonbridge, UK as a specialist vehicle data and lookup service. The company built a corrected UK vehicle database derived from DVLA data. CarweB was acquired by HPI Limited, the UK subsidiary of US conglomerate Solera Inc., in early 2012. Solera is the global leader in vehicle data and insurance claims processing.
CarWeb.summary_abuse: CarWeb is a vehicle data lookup service primarily serving B2B clients in the UK automotive sector including dealers, insurers, and parts suppliers. No notable abuse signal. Vehicle history data services can theoretically be misused for privacy intrusion, but CarWeb operates under established UK automotive industry norms and is owned by the reputable Solera group.

caSaaS.description: caSaaS is a Content Management System that enables users to create, organize, and manage digital content.
caSaaS.history: caSaaS is a niche hosted CMS platform; no founding year or notable ownership or history information found in public records.
caSaaS.summary_abuse: caSaaS is a small hosted CMS with no documented abuse signal or notable security history.

Casafari.description: Casafari is a real estate CRM platform designed for professionals to manage content, properties, tasks, and client relationships.
Casafari.history: CASAFARI was founded in Lisbon, Portugal as an AI-powered real estate data intelligence platform. The company raised $15 million in Series A funding led by Prudence Holdings and has grown to approximately 169 employees across 3 continents. CASAFARI aggregates over 200 million listings from 30,000+ sources across 20+ countries and reached $35 million in annual revenue by 2025.
Casafari.summary_abuse: CASAFARI is a real estate data intelligence platform aggregating property listings across Europe with no documented abuse signal. The platform serves real estate agents, investors, and property professionals with market data and analytics. There are no documented incidents of the service being exploited for fraud or deceptive real estate practices.

CasaSoft.description: CasaSoft is a provider of digital solutions tailored for real estate marketing needs.
CasaSoft.history: CasaSoft Ltd was founded in 2004 in Hamrun, Malta as an ISO 9001:2015 certified custom software development company. The company specializes in enterprise solutions, web applications, system integrations, and e-commerce, with particular expertise in real estate technology. CasaSoft has been providing software development services for over 15 years.
CasaSoft.summary_abuse: CasaSoft is a professional software development and enterprise solutions company based in Malta with no documented abuse signal. The company develops bespoke software for clients including real estate platforms and enterprise systems. No security incidents or misuse cases have been publicly reported.

cashew.description: Cashew is a buy now, pay later platform that allows its customers to shop now and pay later in equal monthly installments.
cashew.history: Cashew is a buy now, pay later (BNPL) platform. BNPL services gained significant growth during the early 2020s. Limited founding details are publicly available for Cashew specifically.
cashew.summary_abuse: Cashew is a buy now, pay later (BNPL) payment platform. BNPL services have documented exposure to identity fraud and synthetic identity abuse, where fraudsters use stolen or fabricated identities to obtain credit. The degree of Cashew's specific exposure depends on its KYC and fraud controls, which are not publicly documented.

Caspio.description: Caspio is an online database platform that enables the creation of business applications, custom forms, and reports without the need for coding.
Caspio.history: Caspio was founded in 2000 as a cloud-based low-code database application builder. Over 25 years the company has grown to serve more than 15,000 customers in 150 countries. Caspio pioneered cloud-based no-code database apps before the term low-code was widely used. The company has maintained independence without major acquisitions or ownership changes.
Caspio.summary_abuse: Caspio is a legitimate low-code database application platform used by businesses, nonprofits, and educational institutions. SOC 2 Type 2 certified and HIPAA compliant. No documented abuse incidents or misuse cases have been associated with this platform.

Castle.description: Castle is a tool that offers deep visibility into user activities on your website, allowing you to monitor and understand user behaviour in real-time.
Castle.history: Castle was founded in 2015 and backed by Y Combinator and Index Ventures. The company builds real-time infrastructure to protect SaaS platforms from fraud, account abuse, and malicious bot automation. Castle processes billions of monthly requests for bot detection, credential stuffing prevention, and account takeover protection. The company has remained independent and focused on the developer-facing security API market.
Castle.summary_abuse: Castle is a fraud and bot detection security platform with no documented abuse by its customers. The service is designed to protect other platforms from abuse and operates as infrastructure-layer security middleware. No incidents of the Castle service itself being abused have been documented.

Catberry.js.description: Catberry.js is a framework for building isomorphic web applications that render on both the server and client sides using JavaScript.
Catberry.js.history: Catberry.js is an open-source isomorphic JavaScript framework for building universal front-end applications using Flux architecture and progressive rendering. Created around 2014 and published on GitHub, the project was designed as an Express/Connect middleware with server-side rendering via Node.js streams. The project is no longer maintained.
Catberry.js.summary_abuse: Catberry.js is an abandoned open-source JavaScript framework with no documented abuse. It is a developer framework with no consumer-facing surface and no documented misuse.

Catch.description: Catch is a payment solution which allows merchants to use payments via bank payments instead of credit/debit cards.
Catch.history: Catch is a fintech buy-now-pay-later and rewards platform for direct-to-consumer e-commerce brands. Founded around 2020 in the US, the company allows shoppers to earn rewards when paying with their bank account, avoiding credit card fees for merchants. The platform is venture-backed and focuses on the DTC e-commerce segment.
Catch.summary_abuse: Catch is a BNPL and payments rewards platform for e-commerce with no documented abuse signal. The service facilitates payments between consumers and DTC merchants. As a payment facilitation tool, it is subject to standard financial fraud risks such as bank account fraud, but no platform-specific abuse cases have been publicly documented.

Catch Themes Catch Box.description: Catch Box is a lightweight, box shaped, clean responsive WordPress theme by Catch Themes.
Catch Themes Catch Box.history: Catch Box is a free WordPress theme by Catch Themes, a company founded in 2012 in Kathmandu, Nepal by Sakin Shrestha. Catch Themes began when the founder created his first WordPress theme which became one of the top-5 themes on WordPress.org. Catch Themes now offers 100+ free themes and 80+ premium themes, with Catch Box being one of their popular free offerings.
