Catch Themes Catch Box.summary_abuse: Catch Box is a GPL-licensed free WordPress theme by the reputable Catch Themes company with no documented abuse. WordPress themes themselves are not vectors for abuse by the theme vendor. No security vulnerabilities or misuse cases have been specifically documented for Catch Box.

Catch Themes Fotografie.description: Fotografie is a modern photography WordPress theme that comes with high-quality features and minimal design by Catch Themes.
Catch Themes Fotografie.history: Catch Themes Fotografie is a photography-focused WordPress theme by Catch Themes, a company founded in 2012 in Kathmandu, Nepal. Catch Themes offers an extensive portfolio of free and premium WordPress themes and plugins, with Fotografie targeting photography portfolios and creative professionals.
Catch Themes Fotografie.summary_abuse: Catch Themes Fotografie is a GPL-licensed WordPress photography theme by the reputable Catch Themes company with no documented abuse. No security vulnerabilities or misuse cases have been specifically documented.

CatchJS.description: CatchJS is a JavaScript error-logging tool that captures runtime errors and generates detailed reports for diagnostic analysis.
CatchJS.history: CatchJS is a JavaScript error monitoring and logging SaaS. No public founding year or notable ownership history is available.
CatchJS.summary_abuse: CatchJS is a developer-focused JavaScript error logging tool with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend observability tool for developers with no consumer-facing features.

CATS.description: CATS is an applicant tracking system designed to help professional recruiters manage job postings, candidate applications, and hiring workflows.
CATS.history: CATS (Candidate Applicant Tracking System) is an applicant tracking system for professional recruiters, developed around 2007. It provides open-source community and hosted commercial versions.
CATS.summary_abuse: CATS is a recruitment applicant tracking system with no meaningful public abuse surface. No documented abuse incidents.

Catylist.description: Catylist is an all-in-one platform for commercial real estate brokers, offering CRE data, reporting and marketing tools, and property listings.
Catylist.history: Catylist was founded in 2001 in Chicago by Paul Brockmeyer and Dustin Gellman as a commercial real estate broker platform with listings, CRM, and marketing tools. The company was acquired by Moody's Analytics CRE, making it part of Moody's Corporation's commercial real estate data and analytics division. Catylist serves over 70 REALTOR associations and thousands of CRE brokerages across North America.
Catylist.summary_abuse: Catylist is a commercial real estate platform owned by Moody's Corporation with no documented abuse signal. The platform serves professional real estate brokers and associations with property listings and CRM tools. No fraud or misuse cases have been publicly associated with the platform.

CausalFunnel.description: CausalFunnel is an AI platform that analyzes customer behavior to optimize business conversion rates.
CausalFunnel.history: CausalFunnel is an AI marketing analytics and profit maximization platform founded around 2019. The leadership team has backgrounds at Apple, Microsoft, Groupon, and Wall Street quantitative trading. The platform uses cookie-less AI technology (DeepID) to track and predict visitor purchase intent, and offers a Shopify app alongside standalone SaaS plans.
CausalFunnel.summary_abuse: CausalFunnel is an AI marketing analytics tool for e-commerce with no documented abuse signal. The platform collects behavioral data using cookie-less tracking technology to optimize marketing performance. No fraud or misuse cases have been associated with the platform.

CCV Shop.description: CCV Shop is a Dutch ecommerce platform that enables users to create and manage online stores efficiently.
CCV Shop.history: CCV Shop is a Dutch e-commerce platform part of CCV Group, a payment and software company. The platform targets Dutch and Belgian SMB merchants and has been operating since the early 2000s.
CCV Shop.summary_abuse: CCV Shop is a Dutch e-commerce platform builder. Like all e-commerce platforms it can be used to create fraudulent storefronts, but the Dutch-market focus and CCV Group ownership provide accountability. No major documented abuse incidents specific to CCV Shop.

CDK Global.description: CDK Global offered a range of software and digital marketing solutions designed to help automotive businesses manage their operations, improve customer engagement, and enhance their online presence. Their services included dealership management systems (DMS), customer relationship management (CRM) software, website and digital marketing services, and various other tools tailored to the automotive industry.
CDK Global.history: CDK Global was spun off from ADP in 2014 as a standalone automotive dealership software company. It provides dealer management systems (DMS), CRM, and digital marketing solutions to automotive retailers. In 2024 CDK suffered a significant ransomware attack that disrupted thousands of auto dealerships across North America for several weeks.
CDK Global.summary_abuse: CDK Global is enterprise dealership management software. In June 2024 it was hit by a major ransomware attack (attributed to BlackSuit) that took down its systems for weeks and affected approximately 15,000 North American car dealerships. The incident highlighted supply-chain software risk for the automotive retail sector.

CDN77.description: CDN77 is a content delivery network (CDN).
CDN77.history: CDN77 was founded in 2011 as a London-headquartered CDN provider with a focus on video streaming and high-throughput content delivery. It has grown to over 200 Tbps capacity across 6 continents and serves major video platforms including Starz and Rakuten TV.
CDN77.summary_abuse: CDN77 is a CDN provider primarily serving video streaming clients. Like all CDNs it can be used to accelerate delivery of malicious content, and CDN77 has historically appeared in threat intelligence reports for hosting phishing or malware infrastructure. The company publishes an abuse contact and AUP. The abuse rate is not significantly higher than comparable CDNs.

cdnjs.description: cdnjs is a free distributed JS library delivery service.
cdnjs.history: cdnjs was created in January 2011 by Ryan Kirkman and Thomas Davis. It partnered with Cloudflare in June 2011, which has since provided the CDN infrastructure. cdnjs serves over 13% of all websites globally and handles more than 250 billion requests per month.
cdnjs.summary_abuse: cdnjs is a widely-used public CDN for JavaScript libraries. There is a small but documented risk of supply-chain attacks via compromised library submissions, as has occurred with other public CDNs. Cloudflare's ownership provides strong infrastructure security and abuse monitoring. Its use of subresource integrity (SRI) hashes is recommended to mitigate tampering risks.

Cecil.description: Cecil is a CLI application, powered by PHP, that merge plain text files (written in Markdown), images and Twig templates to generate a static website.
Cecil.history: Cecil started as PHPoole, a PHP static site generator created by Arnaud Ligny, a French developer, around 2017. It was later rebranded to Cecil and has been actively maintained as a solo open-source project. The project uses PHP with Twig templating and is licensed under EUPL-1.2. It remains a niche tool maintained by a single developer with no corporate backing.
Cecil.summary_abuse: Cecil is a static site generator with no server-side runtime or hosted platform, meaning there is essentially no abuse surface. It generates static HTML files that are deployed independently by users. No notable abuse reports exist for Cecil.

Celebros.description: Celebros is a provider of conversion technology solutions for online shops, designed to improve user engagement and increase conversion rates.
Celebros.history: Celebros was founded in 2000 as a natural language site search and analytics provider for ecommerce, pioneering semantic search technology with multilingual support. The company built a customer base among online retailers in the US, Europe, and Asia. In February 2019, Bridgeline Digital acquired the Celebros product and technology assets, integrating it as part of the Bridgeline product portfolio for ecommerce search and merchandising.
Celebros.summary_abuse: Celebros is an ecommerce site search platform with no notable abuse signal. Its functionality is limited to on-site search and merchandising for retailers, with no user-generated content hosting or email sending capabilities that would make it attractive to abusers.

Celerant.description: Celerant is a retail management platform that integrates point of sale and ecommerce into a single system for streamlined business operations.
Celerant.history: Celerant Technology was founded in 1999 by Ian Goldman and Robert Goldman in Staten Island, New York, with the goal of providing integrated point-of-sale and retail management software. Over 25 years the company expanded to serve thousands of retailers with products including Stratus Enterprise for larger retailers and Cumulus Retail for small businesses. Celerant remains privately held and independent with approximately 220 employees across 7 US offices as of 2026.
Celerant.summary_abuse: Celerant is a retail management software platform used by brick-and-mortar and multichannel retailers. As a back-office and POS system rather than a consumer-facing hosted service, it has no notable abuse signal. The platform handles payment card data for retailers and maintains PCI compliance practices at the merchant level.

Celeritas.description: Celeritas offers transportation logistics services for package deliveries.
Celeritas.history: Celeritas was a small PHP web development framework hosted on SourceForge that layered on top of Web Macro to accelerate common web development tasks. The project had minimal traction compared to dominant PHP frameworks like Symfony and Laravel and appears to have been abandoned in its early stages with no further releases.
Celeritas.summary_abuse: Celeritas is an abandoned PHP framework with no hosted service or consumer-facing surface. There is no notable abuse signal associated with it.

Cellxpert.description: Cellxpert is a SaaS platform for affiliate and partner program management, providing tools for tracking, reporting, and optimizing performance-based marketing campaigns.
Cellxpert.history: Cellxpert was founded in 2008 by Assaf Dor and is headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel. The platform provides affiliate and performance marketing management software, with a particular focus on iGaming, Forex, and eCommerce sectors. The company has grown to serve over 500 brands with real-time tracking and reporting tools for managing affiliate programs.
Cellxpert.summary_abuse: Cellxpert serves the affiliate marketing space, including iGaming and Forex sectors which have elevated regulatory scrutiny. Affiliate tracking platforms can be exploited by fraudulent affiliates using fake traffic, click fraud, or fraudulent leads. However, Cellxpert's role is as the platform managing these relationships rather than hosting content directly. No major public abuse incidents have been reported.

Celum.description: Celum is a software developer that specialises in enterprise digital asset management and marketing content management systems.
Celum.history: CELUM was founded in 1999 in Linz, Austria by Michael Kraeftner. The company developed into a leading European digital asset management (DAM) and content collaboration platform, serving global enterprise brands including Lidl, 3M, HiPP, and CLAAS. CELUM has grown organically without venture capital, remaining founder-led and independent with over 100 employees and 900+ customers worldwide.
Celum.summary_abuse: CELUM is an enterprise digital asset management platform used by large brands to manage internal content and marketing assets. As a back-office DAM solution, it has no notable abuse signal. The platform is primarily used in closed corporate environments and does not expose a public-facing surface that would attract abusers.

CEMax.description: CEMax is a premium customer engagement platform.
CEMax.history: CEMax was founded in January 2012 as a customer experience management platform based in Cincinnati, Ohio. The company provides AI-powered self-service and agent-assisted CX tools for organizations including banks, hotels, and government offices.
CEMax.summary_abuse: CEMax is an enterprise customer engagement platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform serves B2B clients for customer experience management and has no consumer-facing anonymous features.

Cendyn.description: Cendyn (formerly NextGuest) is a hospitality focused content management system.
Cendyn.history: Cendyn was founded in 1996 by Robin Deyo and Charles Deyo in Boca Raton, Florida, originally to automate RFP management for hotels. For its first two decades the company focused on hotel sales and marketing tools. In 2016 Accel KKR made an initial investment, enabling exponential growth through acquisitions across CRS, RMS, digital marketing, and CRM. Cendyn now serves over 35,000 hotel customers in 150 countries and generates more than $20 billion in annual hotel revenue for clients.
Cendyn.summary_abuse: Cendyn is a hospitality CRM and marketing platform used by major hotel brands and chains. As a B2B enterprise system handling hotel guest data and marketing campaigns, it has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface. The platform processes significant amounts of guest PII for hotel loyalty and marketing, making data privacy practices the primary trust consideration rather than abuse vectors.

Censhare.description: Censhare is a commercial digital experience platform in the form of an enterprise content management system.
Censhare.history: Censhare was founded in 2001 in Munich, Germany, with the product launching in 2002 as CoWare Server. The platform evolved through version 2.0 under the censhare brand, and in 2008 the parent company CoWare AG was renamed censhare AG. In February 2019, censhare was acquired by DuMont Mediengruppe, a major German media and publishing group. The platform provides unified PIM, DAM, and WCM capabilities for large enterprises with global multilingual content operations.
Censhare.summary_abuse: Censhare is an enterprise content management platform used internally by large organizations to manage product information, digital assets, and multichannel content. It has no consumer-facing public surface and no notable abuse signal. The primary trust considerations are data governance and GDPR compliance given the volume of marketing and product data it manages.

Centium.description: Centium is a provider of technology solutions specializing in property and event management software for the hospitality industry, landmark world events, and the aviation sector.
Centium.history: Centium Software was founded in 2008 in Brisbane, Australia. The company provides hotel property management software (GuestPoint) and a separate all-in-one event management platform. Centium has powered over 350,000 events globally and serves the hospitality and events sectors across associations, education, government, and corporate industries.
Centium.summary_abuse: Centium Software provides hotel property management and event management software for hospitality businesses. As a B2B platform with no consumer-facing content hosting or email blast functionality, it has no notable abuse signal.

Centminmod.history: Centmin Mod is an automated LEMP stack installer and management tool for CentOS, AlmaLinux, and Rocky Linux, created and maintained by a single developer. It automates the installation and configuration of Nginx, PHP-FPM, and MariaDB with security hardening and performance optimization. The project has maintained annual versioned documentation sites (2025.centminmod.com, 2026.centminmod.com) and remains actively maintained as of 2026.
Centminmod.summary_abuse: Centmin Mod is a server configuration automation tool installed on private servers by system administrators. As a tool used to configure servers rather than a hosted service or library embedded in websites, it has no meaningful abuse surface and no notable abuse signal.

CentOS.description: CentOS is a Linux distribution that provides a free, community-supported computing platform functionally compatible with its upstream source, Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL).
CentOS.history: CentOS (Community ENTerprise Operating System) was first released in May 2004 as a free, community-supported rebuild of Red Hat Enterprise Linux source code. It became one of the most widely deployed Linux distributions for servers. In January 2014, Red Hat formally joined the CentOS governance board and employed its core developers while keeping CentOS independent. In December 2020, Red Hat and the CentOS Project announced that CentOS Linux would be discontinued at end of 2021, redirecting focus to CentOS Stream, the upstream development platform for future RHEL releases. CentOS Linux 8 reached end-of-life on December 31, 2021, years ahead of the originally promised 2029 date, prompting widespread negative reaction from the community and spawning successor projects AlmaLinux and Rocky Linux.
CentOS.summary_abuse: CentOS as a Linux distribution has no inherent abuse signal at the software level. Like any server operating system, it can be used to host malicious infrastructure, but this is not specific to CentOS and reflects the general nature of general-purpose server software. The distribution itself is now deprecated in favor of CentOS Stream.

Centra.description: Centra is the headless ecommerce platform.
Centra.history: Centra was founded in 2015 in Stockholm, Sweden by Frans Rosen and Piotr Zaleski as a headless API-first ecommerce platform focused on fashion and lifestyle brands. The company grew its ARR by approximately 50% annually and serves over 200 brand clients including Sandqvist, Nudie Jeans, and Eton Shirts. In 2021, Verdane invested EUR 10 million in Centra to fund international expansion, with H&M Group Ventures also among its investors.
Centra.summary_abuse: Centra is a headless SaaS ecommerce platform for fashion brands, handling storefronts and wholesale operations. As an enterprise B2B platform serving established brands rather than enabling arbitrary user-generated storefronts, it has minimal abuse surface. No notable fraud or abuse incidents have been reported for the platform.

Centribal.description: Centribal is a conversational platform designed for creating, managing, and training chatbots.
Centribal.history: Centribal is a Spain-based conversational AI and chatbot platform, operating as a Zendesk Master Partner and providing a no-code environment for building and deploying chatbots across WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Instagram, Telegram, and website channels. The platform integrates with CRMs including Zendesk, HubSpot, and Salesforce. The company has limited public history documented.
Centribal.summary_abuse: Centribal is a conversational AI chatbot platform. Like all chatbot and messaging platforms, it could theoretically be used to build deceptive bots, but no documented abuse incidents have been reported. The platform targets legitimate enterprise customer service use cases.

Cerkl.description: Cerkl is an internal communication software designed to support organizations of any size or structure.
Cerkl.history: Cerkl Broadcast is an employee and external communications platform founded in the United States that uses AI-driven personalization to deliver relevant content to employees and stakeholders via email, Slack, and Microsoft Teams. The company serves enterprise customers including Citrix, Paycor, Novant Health, and the University of Cincinnati. Pricing starts at $799 per month.
Cerkl.summary_abuse: Cerkl is an internal and external communications platform used by enterprises to send personalized newsletters and updates. As a permission-based communication tool for established organizations rather than a bulk cold-email service, its abuse surface is limited. No notable spam or abuse incidents have been publicly reported.

Ceros.description: Ceros is an interactive, no-code content creation platform designed for marketers and designers to develop engaging digital experiences.
Ceros.history: Ceros was originally founded as Crowd Fusion by Brian Alvey in 2007, then rebranded after acquiring London-based Ceros in 2012, with Simon Berg taking over as CEO. The platform evolved into a no-code interactive content design SaaS enabling creation of experiences like shoppable catalogs, lookbooks, and infographics. The company has raised $143 million in total funding, including a $100 million Series D round in July 2020 led by Sumeru Equity Partners. Notable clients include Peugeot, SAP, Unilever, and HP.
Ceros.summary_abuse: Ceros is an interactive content design platform used by marketing teams at major enterprises. The platform is used to create rich content experiences embedded on brand websites. Embedded interactive content created on Ceros could theoretically be used to host deceptive experiences, but the platform requires sales-gated enterprise subscriptions with vetted accounts, minimizing this risk. No notable abuse incidents have been reported.

Certishopping.description: Certishopping is an all-in-one marketing platform designed to accelerate business growth through integrated tools and automation.
Certishopping.history: Certishopping is a Paris-based customer review management and Google/Facebook Ads campaign optimization platform primarily serving French and European ecommerce merchants. The platform collects verified customer reviews and integrates with Google Shopping, Facebook, TikTok, and Pinterest. It serves over 1,200 websites and integrates with Shopify, WooCommerce, Wix, and other ecommerce platforms.
Certishopping.summary_abuse: Certishopping is a review management and advertising optimization platform. Review platforms can be exploited for fake review injection, but Certishopping claims to collect only authentic reviews. As a small French platform with limited reach, it has no notable documented abuse incidents.

Cevoid.description: Cevoid is an all-in-one user-generated content (UGC) platform designed for e-commerce brands.
Cevoid.history: Cevoid was founded in 2020 in Stockholm, Sweden, with backing from ACVC and angel investors from companies including iZettle, Fyndiq, and Grebban. The platform provides UGC (user-generated content) collection, loyalty, and review management for ecommerce brands, enabling shoppable galleries from customer and influencer content. The company had raised a pre-seed round as of 2022.
Cevoid.summary_abuse: Cevoid is a user-generated content platform for ecommerce brands. UGC platforms can be exploited to surface inappropriate or fake content, but Cevoid operates in a curated brand context where merchants control what content is displayed. As a young startup with limited adoption, no major abuse incidents have been publicly reported.

Cfixé.description: Cfixé is a scheduling platform that enables online booking for everyday services and allows professionals to embed a scheduling widget directly on their website.
Cfixé.history: Cfixe is a French SaaS platform targeting independent service professionals including freelancers, tradespeople, and local businesses, offering an all-in-one solution combining website creation, appointment booking, online payments, CRM, and customer reviews. The platform hosts data on French servers and positions itself as GDPR-sovereign. Pricing starts at approximately EUR 29 per month with no commission fees.
Cfixé.summary_abuse: Cfixe provides website and booking tools for independent professionals in France. As a small-scale platform hosting business websites with appointment booking, it could theoretically be used to create fake service businesses to collect deposits. However, as a subscription-based French platform with no free tier and local hosting, the abuse risk is relatively contained.

CFML.description: ColdFusion Markup Language (CFML), is a scripting language for web development that runs on the JVM, the .NET framework, and Google App Engine.
CFML.history: ColdFusion Markup Language (CFML) was created in 1995 by Allaire Corporation as a rapid web development scripting language. Allaire was acquired by Macromedia in 2001, which was in turn acquired by Adobe in 2005. Adobe ColdFusion continues as a commercial product while open-source alternatives like Lucee exist. CFML is largely legacy in the modern web development ecosystem.
CFML.summary_abuse: CFML is a server-side scripting language. At the language level it has no abuse surface; historically some CFML application frameworks had known vulnerabilities, but this is a general web platform concern, not specific to CFML.

cgit.description: cgit is a web interface (cgi) for Git repositories, written in C. licensed under GPLv2.
cgit.history: cgit was created by Jason A. Donenfeld (also known as zx2c4) and first released in December 2006 as a fast CGI-based web frontend for Git repositories written in C. It is widely used by projects that self-host their Git infrastructure, including the Linux kernel and WireGuard projects. No notable ownership changes; maintained by Donenfeld with occasional community contributions.
cgit.summary_abuse: cgit is a self-hosted git web interface with no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal; it is used by developers to browse private or public git repositories on their own servers.

Chabokan.description: Chabokan is a cloud services provider, offering a wide range of incorporated cloud services including Cloud Object Storage, DBaaS, BaaS, and PaaS.
Chabokan.history: Chabokan is an Iranian cloud platform offering PaaS, DBaaS, BaaS, and object storage services, primarily targeting developers and online businesses in Iran. The platform has been operating since at least March 2022, based on growth tracking data. It offers managed deployment for various technology stacks.
Chabokan.summary_abuse: Chabokan is an Iranian cloud hosting and PaaS provider. Cloud platforms operating outside of mainstream Western regulatory frameworks can present elevated abuse risk due to differences in takedown responsiveness and international cooperation. The platform's Iranian jurisdiction means it operates under different legal constraints than EU or US providers. No specific major abuse incidents are publicly documented.

Chai.description: Chai is a BDD / TDD assertion library for node and the browser that can be paired with any javascript testing framework.
Chai.history: Chai is a BDD/TDD JavaScript assertion library for Node.js and the browser, first released around 2011. It became one of the most widely used testing libraries in the JavaScript ecosystem, commonly paired with Mocha and Sinon. The library is maintained by the chaijs organization on GitHub, with v5 being the current major version featuring modern ESM support.
Chai.summary_abuse: Chai is a JavaScript testing assertion library used exclusively in development and test environments. It has no runtime presence in production web deployments and no abuse surface.

Chakra UI.description: Chakra UI is a simple, modular and accessible component library that gives you the building blocks you need to build your React applications.
Chakra UI.history: Chakra UI was created in 2019 by Segun Adebayo, a Nigerian UX engineer, with a focus on accessibility, composability, and ease of styling. The library quickly gained widespread adoption in the React ecosystem, reaching over 31,000 GitHub stars and 1.9 million monthly npm downloads. The chakra-ui GitHub organization expanded to include cross-framework tools like Ark UI and Zag.js.
Chakra UI.summary_abuse: Chakra UI is a React component library distributed via npm for use in development. It has no runtime server-side presence and is not used to host content or services. There is no abuse signal associated with this library.

Chameleon.description: Chameleon is a sophisticated no-code platform for product success, empowering SaaS teams to build self-service user onboarding, feature adoption, and feedback collection.
Chameleon.history: Chameleon was founded in 2015 by Pulkit Agrawal and Brian Norton in San Francisco, born from the founders' frustration with poor product onboarding experiences at earlier startups. The company raised an initial seed round from True Ventures and bootstrapped to profitability before raising a $13 million Series A in September 2022 led by Matrix Partners, bringing total funding to $14.8 million. The platform serves product and customer success teams at over 300 companies including Twilio, Highspot, and Fivetran.
Chameleon.summary_abuse: Chameleon is a product adoption platform that injects in-product UI elements like tooltips, modals, and walkthroughs into SaaS products. As an overlay tool running inside authenticated products used by known customers, its abuse surface is limited. However, if misused, it could theoretically be used to add deceptive UI patterns to legitimate software products. No such incidents have been reported.

Chameleon Power.description: Chameleon Power is a software platform that enables visualization, design, decoration, and selection of digital images and materials.
Chameleon Power.history: Chameleon Power is a product visualization and configuration software company focused on the home building supply industry, providing web visualizers, 3D/AR/VR renderings, and product configurators for windows, doors, and building products. Hyphen Solutions acquired all Chameleon Power assets and integrated it into their homebuilding technology platform. Notable clients include Ply Gem and Champion Windows.
Chameleon Power.summary_abuse: Chameleon Power is a product visualization software used by homebuilding suppliers and manufacturers to let customers configure and visualize products. As a B2B tool in the construction industry with no free tier or consumer-facing content hosting, it has no notable abuse signal.

Chameleon system.description: Chameleon system is an ecommerce and content management system all-in-one, capable of being integrated straight from the manufacturer.
Chameleon system.history: Chameleon system is an open-source CMS and ecommerce platform developed by ESONO AG, a Freiburg, Germany-based agency. Built on the Symfony PHP framework with an MIT license, it is designed for demanding digital platforms requiring both content management and commerce features. It is particularly suited for mid-size and large companies with complex requirements and targets the German-speaking market.
Chameleon system.summary_abuse: Chameleon system is an open-source CMS and ecommerce framework that is self-hosted by users. As a self-hosted platform with a relatively small user base concentrated in the German-speaking market, it has minimal notable abuse surface or documented abuse incidents.

Chamilo.description: Chamilo is an open-source learning management and collaboration system.
Chamilo.history: Chamilo LMS was founded in 2010 as a fork of Dokeos (itself derived from Claroline, created in 1999) by Yannick Warnier and others, with the Chamilo Association formally established in Belgium that year. The project headquarters moved to Spain in 2014. By 2021, Chamilo had been used by over 40 million people, with the majority of its user base in Latin America. The primary development company is BeezNest, based in Brussels.
Chamilo.summary_abuse: Chamilo is a self-hosted open-source LMS used by universities, governments, and training organizations primarily in Latin America and Europe. As a platform for course delivery and student management deployed by institutions on their own servers, it has minimal direct abuse surface. There are no notable abuse incidents publicly associated with Chamilo.

Channel.io.description: Channel.io is an all-in-one business communication platform that helps businesses connect with customers.
Channel.io.history: Channel.io (Channel Talk) was founded in 2014 by Josh Jaehong Kim and Red Siwon Choi in Seoul, South Korea. The platform provides an all-in-one business messaging OS combining live chat, CRM, and team collaboration tools. The company raised a $28 million Series C in 2020 and a Series D round in January 2025, with total funding over $41 million. As of 2024, Channel Talk reported $71 million in revenue and approximately 50,900 customers.
Channel.io.summary_abuse: Channel.io is a customer messaging and live chat platform used by businesses for customer support. Chat platforms can be used to conduct social engineering or phishing against users. As a platform targeting businesses rather than anonymous consumer-level chat, the abuse surface is moderate. No specific major abuse incidents have been reported for Channel.io.

ChannelAdvisor.description: ChannelAdvisor is a provider of cloud-based solutions to ecommerce companies.
ChannelAdvisor.history: ChannelAdvisor was founded in 2001 in Morrisville, North Carolina, as a multichannel ecommerce management platform helping brands list and sell across online marketplaces like Amazon, eBay, and Google. The company went public and made multiple acquisitions from 2005 through 2020. In September 2022, CommerceHub acquired ChannelAdvisor for $660 million. In December 2023, CommerceHub rebranded as Rithum, uniting ChannelAdvisor, Dsco, and Cadeera under a single brand. Rithum now processes over $50 billion GMV annually across 18,000+ customers.
ChannelAdvisor.summary_abuse: ChannelAdvisor (now Rithum) is a marketplace management platform used by established brands and retailers. As an enterprise platform with KYC requirements for sellers, it has a very low abuse surface. The platform does not host consumer-facing content and focuses on inventory and listing management for vetted business customers.

ChannelApe.description: ChannelApe is an ecommerce and inventory management solution for the footwear and apparel industry.
ChannelApe.history: ChannelApe was founded around 2016 by Jason DePietropaolo and Michael Averto as an ecommerce inventory intelligence and order management platform. The company raised $2.4 million in seed funding from Afore, Upside Partnership, and Supernode Ventures. In July 2024, Shopify acquired ChannelApe to expand its fulfillment and inventory management capabilities.
ChannelApe.summary_abuse: ChannelApe is a B2B ecommerce inventory management and order routing platform. As a backend operations tool for ecommerce brands and 3PLs, it has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Chaport.description: Chaport is a multi-channel live chat and chatbot software for business.
Chaport.history: Chaport is a customer messaging and live chat platform that has grown to serve over 30,000 teams globally. The platform offers multi-channel live chat, chatbots, and a knowledge base product. Pricing tiers include a free plan for 2 operators, a Pro plan at approximately EUR 39/month, and an Unlimited plan at EUR 129/month. Limited company founding history is publicly available.
Chaport.summary_abuse: Chaport is a live chat and customer messaging platform embedded on business websites. Chat widget providers with free tiers can potentially be embedded on phishing or scam sites to lend legitimacy, or used for social engineering. Chaport has a free tier which slightly elevates this risk, but no specific documented abuse incidents are known.

ChargeAfter.description: ChargeAfter is a platform that connects retailers and lenders to offer consumers personalized Point of Sale Financing options at checkout from multiple lenders.
ChargeAfter.history: ChargeAfter was founded in 2017 by Meidad Sharon and is headquartered in New York with R&D in Tel Aviv. The company provides an embedded lending network connecting merchants to a pre-integrated panel of lenders for point-of-sale financing. Visa invested in ChargeAfter in February 2020, followed by MUFG in June 2020. In March 2022, ChargeAfter raised $44 million in a Series B led by The Phoenix, with participation from Citi Ventures, Banco Bradesco, and MUFG, bringing total funding to $60 million.
ChargeAfter.summary_abuse: ChargeAfter operates as a BNPL and embedded lending network, connecting merchants with lenders. Consumer financing platforms can be exploited to enable fraud through fake merchant accounts or fraudulent loan applications. As a regulated fintech with major bank investors including Citi Ventures and Visa, it maintains financial compliance and KYC controls, limiting but not eliminating fraud exposure.

Chargebee.description: Chargebee is a PCI Level 1 certified recurring billing platform for SaaS and subscription-based businesses.
Chargebee.history: Chargebee was founded in 2011 by Krish Subramanian, KP Saravanan, Thiyagarajan T, and Rajaraman Santhanam in Chennai, India. The company raised seed funding from Accel in 2013 and opened a US office in Walnut, California. Through successive rounds (Series B in 2015 led by Tiger Global, Series C in 2018 led by Insight Ventures, and Series H in 2022 led by Tiger Global and Sequoia India for $250 million), Chargebee reached a $3.5 billion valuation, becoming India's 11th unicorn. The platform serves over 6,500 subscription businesses across 60+ countries.
Chargebee.summary_abuse: Chargebee is a subscription billing and revenue management platform used by SaaS and subscription businesses. As infrastructure for legitimate recurring billing, it processes significant payment data with full PCI-DSS and SOC 2 compliance. Its use in subscription fraud or involuntary churn manipulation is theoretically possible at the merchant level, but Chargebee itself maintains strong compliance posture and vendor controls.

Chart.js.description: Chart.js is an open-source JavaScript library that allows you to draw different types of charts by using the HTML5 canvas element.
Chart.js.history: Chart.js was created in 2013 by Nick Downie as an open-source JavaScript charting library. It quickly became one of the most popular charting libraries in the JavaScript ecosystem, accumulating over 60,000 GitHub stars. The project is maintained by the chartjs organization with active community contributions and regular releases.
Chart.js.summary_abuse: Chart.js is a JavaScript data visualization library used in browsers and Node.js applications. It has no server-side component, does not make network requests, and has no abuse surface.

Chartbeat.description: Chartbeat is a web analytics service that provides real-time data and insights into website performance, audience engagement, and content effectiveness for publishers and media organizations.
Chartbeat.history: Chartbeat was launched in April 2009 by Betaworks and Tony Haile as a real-time web analytics tool designed for publishers and news sites, at a time when Google Analytics lacked real-time capabilities. The company spun off from Betaworks in August 2010 and raised $3 million from Index, Ron Conway, Chris Sacca, and others. In April 2012, Chartbeat raised $9.5 million led by Draper Fisher Jurvetson. In July 2022, Cuadrilla Capital, a private equity firm, acquired Chartbeat. The platform continues to serve major news publishers and media organizations.
Chartbeat.summary_abuse: Chartbeat is a real-time content analytics platform primarily used by news publishers. It embeds a tracking script on publisher websites, collecting reader engagement data. As an analytics tracker, it raises typical privacy considerations about user data collection across publisher sites, but is not associated with malicious use or abuse. It is subject to GDPR requirements given its data collection scope.

Chaser.description: Chaser is a telephone callback system designed to connect users by initiating return calls through a central server.
Chaser.history: Chaser was founded in 2014 in the UK as an accounts receivable automation and credit control platform, originating from a Finance Director frustrated with manual payment chasing processes. Over a decade, the platform helped businesses recover over GBP 30 billion in late payments and saved more than 6 million hours of manual work. In 2024, Chaser was named best technology provider at the Credit Strategy Awards.
Chaser.summary_abuse: Chaser is an accounts receivable automation tool used by accountants and finance teams. It automates invoice payment chasing via email and SMS. As a business-to-business tool for established businesses and their accounting software integrations, it has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface.

Chat Chasers.description: Chat Chasers is a chat system designed for car dealers to streamline communication with customers.
Chat Chasers.history: Chat Chasers is a live chat system designed specifically for automotive dealerships. No public founding year or notable ownership changes are documented.
Chat Chasers.summary_abuse: Chat Chasers is a niche automotive dealership chat tool with no notable abuse signal. The service is vertically focused on car dealers with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

Chat Robot.description: Chat Robot is a live chat system.
Chat Robot.history: Chat Robot is a small live chat system with minimal public presence. No founding year or ownership information is publicly documented.
Chat Robot.summary_abuse: Chat Robot is a small live chat platform with no documented abuse incidents. Live chat tools in general have minimal direct fraud surface.

Chatango.description: Chatango is a website used for connecting to a large selection of users.
Chatango.history: Chatango was founded in 2007 by Alec Matusis, an MIT alumnus, in San Francisco, California. The platform provides embeddable chat rooms and a social network for public and private real-time conversations, primarily used by webmasters to add live chat to their sites. The company has remained a small bootstrapped operation with 1-10 employees.
Chatango.summary_abuse: Chatango's public chat rooms have historically attracted spam bots and inappropriate content due to the anonymous and open nature of the chat format. The platform allows anonymous participation, which lowers the barrier for abuse. It provides basic auto-moderation and spam protection tools. No major security incidents have been documented, but open public chat rooms are inherently prone to harassment and spam.

Chatbaby.description: Chatbaby is a service for creating custom ChatGPT chatbots tailored to website content.
Chatbaby.history: Chatbaby is a post-2022 AI chatbot builder that allows website owners to create ChatGPT-style chatbots trained on their own content. The platform emerged in the wave of AI chatbot building tools that followed the public release of ChatGPT. Limited information is available about the company's founding details or background team.
Chatbaby.summary_abuse: Chatbaby is a small AI chatbot builder with minimal public profile and no documented abuse incidents. The platform is used for building customer-facing chatbots and poses low direct abuse risk. The thin product layer over OpenAI APIs is a common characteristic of post-ChatGPT micro-SaaS tools.

Chatbase.description: Chatbase is an AI chatbot builder, it trains ChatGPT on your data and lets you add a chat widget to your website.
Chatbase.history: Chatbase was founded by Yasser Elsaid, a York University computer science graduate, and launched on February 4, 2023 at the height of the initial ChatGPT enthusiasm with a single tweet. The bootstrapped company grew remarkably fast, reaching $1M ARR in 5 months and growing to over $8M ARR by 2025 with an 18-person team, without taking venture capital funding.
Chatbase.summary_abuse: Chatbase is a rapidly growing AI chatbot builder that allows businesses to train chatbots on their own data. No notable abuse incidents have been documented. The platform requires account creation and is primarily used by legitimate businesses for customer support automation. Being bootstrapped with a strong reputation reduces the likelihood of significant abuse patterns.

ChatBot.description: ChatBot is a framework for AI chatbots, enabling users to create chatbots for various web services.
ChatBot.history: ChatBot is a chatbot building platform launched in February 2018 by Text (formerly LiveChat Software), a Polish company founded in 2002 and listed on the Warsaw Stock Exchange. The chatbot product originated from a hackathon project in 2016, with a beta version released in August 2017. Text operates a suite including LiveChat, ChatBot, and HelpDesk, serving over 28,000 paid customers in 150+ countries.
ChatBot.summary_abuse: ChatBot (chatbot.com) is a commercial chatbot building platform owned by publicly-traded Polish company Text. As a subscription platform used by legitimate businesses for customer service automation, it has a low abuse profile. Like any chatbot platform, it could theoretically be used to build deceptive bots, but its subscription model and enterprise focus reduce this risk.

ChatBotBuilder.description: ChatBotBuilderAI is an AI chatbot development platform that enables deployment of custom GPT-powered chatbots.
ChatBotBuilder.history: ChatBotBuilder (chatbotbuilder.ai) is a no-code AI chatbot building platform that is officially Meta-certified, enabling integration with Facebook and Instagram messaging. The platform allows users to deploy custom GPT-powered chatbots for websites, social media, email, and phone use. Limited company founding history is publicly available.
ChatBotBuilder.summary_abuse: ChatBotBuilder is a no-code chatbot platform offering a free tier and integration with Meta messaging channels. No-code chatbot builders with free tiers represent an elevated abuse risk, as they can be used to create deceptive bots for phishing, social engineering, or spam campaigns on WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, or Instagram. No specific documented abuse incidents are available for this platform specifically.

ChatBullet.description: ChatBullet is a platform that integrates chatbots and AI tools to automate customer interactions.
ChatBullet.history: ChatBullet is a small AI chatbot platform for automating customer interactions. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
ChatBullet.summary_abuse: ChatBullet is a small AI chatbot SaaS with no documented abuse incidents. Chatbot automation tools can be misused for phishing or spam, but no specific reports exist for ChatBullet.

ChatFood.description: ChatFood is a platform providing mobile ordering tailored for hospitality brands.
ChatFood.history: ChatFood was founded around 2019 and built an omnichannel ordering platform for hospitality brands, enabling social media ordering through Instagram, WhatsApp, and websites, plus dine-in and delivery ordering. The platform found its strongest market in the Gulf region with over 3,000 restaurant locations. In May 2023, Deliverect acquired ChatFood to expand its omnichannel capabilities. Co-founders Benjamin Mouflard and Vini Rodrigues remained with Deliverect post-acquisition.
ChatFood.summary_abuse: ChatFood is a restaurant online ordering platform now part of Deliverect. As a food ordering SaaS for restaurant businesses, it has a modest abuse surface primarily through potential for fraudulent restaurant accounts. No major abuse incidents are publicly reported.

ChatGen.description: ChatGen is a hybrid chatbot platform designed to facilitate communication with prospects, customers, and internal employees, aiming to reduce turnaround time and improve productivity.
ChatGen.history: ChatGen is a hybrid chatbot SaaS platform founded in Mumbai, India by Sai Prasanth D, Sambasiva Rao P, and Suvindh Sudevan under the legal entity DBYDX Technologies Private Limited. The founders spent two years researching business processes before building the platform, which targets enterprises needing virtual assistants for customer service and internal support. ChatGen is bootstrapped and has been exploring external funding for global expansion.
ChatGen.summary_abuse: ChatGen is an Indian enterprise chatbot platform targeting legitimate business use cases. As a bootstrapped startup with no free tier and B2B focus, its abuse risk is low. No documented abuse incidents are known.

Chatgo.description: Chatgo is a messenger widget designed for integration with websites and e-shops, enabling communication and engagement.
Chatgo.history: ChatGo is a small live chat and WhatsApp Business platform solution. The company offers a Cloud API and WhatsApp Business Platform integration along with chat support tools. Limited public information is available about its founding details or company background.
Chatgo.summary_abuse: ChatGo is a small live chat platform with limited public presence. No documented abuse incidents have been identified. The platform is used for legitimate business customer communication, primarily through WhatsApp integration. Abuse risk is low given the small user base.

Chatify.description: Chatify is a chat solution for websites seeking to enhance customer engagement.
Chatify.history: Chatify was founded in 2018 by John Dineen and Shane O'Leary in Cork, Ireland. The company grew from a decade of experience building customer engagement software. Notable clients include Microsoft, PennState, Olympus, and Arlo. The company is bootstrapped and remote-first.
Chatify.summary_abuse: Chatify is an AI-enhanced live chat software platform focused on legitimate business customer support use cases. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform targets established businesses and enterprise clients, which reduces abuse risk. The Irish headquarters provides GDPR compliance alignment.

Chative.description: Chative is a platform that provides live chat and chatbot solutions for real-time customer interactions.
Chative.history: Chative was founded around 2021 and is headquartered in Da Nang, Vietnam. The platform provides multilingual live chat, chatbot, and omnichannel customer support solutions. It serves businesses across multiple channels with AI-powered automation and has a Shopify app for ecommerce integration.
Chative.summary_abuse: Chative is a legitimate live chat and chatbot platform focused on business customer support. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is positioned for legitimate business use with Shopify and other ecommerce integrations. Low abuse risk given the customer support focus.

ChatLab.description: ChatLab is an AI-powered customer service agent that operates as a chatbot on websites to assist with user inquiries.
ChatLab.history: ChatLab is an AI-powered website chatbot for customer service. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
ChatLab.summary_abuse: ChatLab is a small AI customer service chatbot with no documented abuse incidents. AI chatbot tools can be misused for phishing if embedded on fraudulent sites, but no specific reports exist for ChatLab.

Chatlio.description: Chatlio is a live chat solution that integrates with Slack, enabling real-time communication and customer support directly within the Slack platform.
Chatlio.history: Chatlio was founded in 2014 by John Eberly in the Greater Seattle area. The company pioneered live chat integrated directly into Slack, being the first official live chat app fully integrated with Slack. This differentiated approach allowed businesses to chat with website visitors without switching tools. The company has remained an independent bootstrapped operation.
Chatlio.summary_abuse: Chatlio is a niche live chat tool built on Slack integration and used primarily by legitimate businesses for customer support. No documented abuse incidents are known. The Slack-based workflow makes it less attractive for abuse compared to standalone chat platforms, as it requires a Slack workspace.

Chatlyn.description: Chatlyn is an AI-powered customer engagement platform designed to automate client communication and optimize engagement, enhancing customer satisfaction.
Chatlyn.history: Chatlyn was founded in late 2022 by Nicolas Vorsteher, Michael Urbanek, and Matthias Haubner in Vienna, Austria. The company focuses on AI-powered guest communication for the hospitality industry, unifying communications across email, WhatsApp, SMS, and social media. In 2025, chatlyn raised an $8.6 million Series A round led by Smedvig Ventures and scaled to serve over 1,000 properties in 30 countries.
Chatlyn.summary_abuse: Chatlyn is a B2B hospitality communication platform serving hotel operators and is used exclusively for legitimate guest communication. No abuse incidents have been documented. The enterprise hotel-focused customer base and recent VC funding indicate a legitimate, well-monitored operation with low abuse risk.

ChatNode.description: ChatNode is a platform for building advanced AI chatbots that provide deep business insights, designed to enhance customer interactions through innovative technology.
ChatNode.history: ChatNode is an AI chatbot builder platform launched around 2022. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
ChatNode.summary_abuse: ChatNode is a small AI chatbot builder with no documented abuse incidents. Chatbot platforms can be used on phishing pages, but no specific abuse is on record for ChatNode.

ChatPlus.description: ChatPlus is a provider of chat and chatbot tool with (or without) AI in Japan and abroad.
ChatPlus.history: ChatPlus Co., Ltd. was founded in 2016 in Japan and has grown to become the No.1 AI chatbot provider in Japan with over 20,000 clients. The platform combines AI-driven scenario chatbots and live agent chat in a single embedded widget, with pricing starting from approximately 1,500 yen per month. ChatPlus has received multiple Japanese software awards including Boxil SaaS Award and ITreview awards.
ChatPlus.summary_abuse: ChatPlus is a Japanese chatbot and live chat platform serving primarily Japanese businesses. As a subscription-based platform focused on legitimate customer service use cases in the Japanese market, it has a low abuse profile. No notable abuse incidents have been documented.

Chatra.description: Chatra is a cloud-based live chat platform aimed at small businesses and ecommerce retailers.
Chatra.history: Chatra was founded in 2015 by Slava Olesik, Yaakov Karda, Maria Karda, and Artem Polikarpov in Arlington, Virginia. The team developed the app in about six months using MeteorJS. In 2022, Chatra was acquired by Brevo (formerly Sendinblue), a French marketing platform, integrating it into a broader suite of marketing and communication tools. No major controversies followed the acquisition.
Chatra.summary_abuse: Chatra is a legitimate live chat tool acquired by Brevo and primarily used for legitimate e-commerce customer support. No documented abuse incidents are known. As part of the Brevo ecosystem, it benefits from the parent company's trust and compliance infrastructure.

Chatroll.description: Chatroll is a chat platform designed for live events, enabling real-time audience engagement and interaction.
Chatroll.history: Chatroll was founded in 2007 by Jon McGee in Toronto, Ontario. The platform provides social chat functionality for online publishers, reaching millions of consumers through thousands of publishers via its embeddable chat plugin for videos, websites, and apps. The company has remained small with 2-10 employees.
Chatroll.summary_abuse: Chatroll's embeddable social chat widgets carry moderate abuse risk due to public-facing, anonymous participation. The platform has been used on media sites and live streaming events where trolling and spam can occur. No major documented vendor-specific abuse incidents exist, but the open nature of public chat widgets is inherently prone to harassment.

Chatsimple.description: Chatsimple provides AI chatbots for sales and support, facilitating lead generation and multilingual customer assistance.
Chatsimple.history: Chatsimple was founded in 2023 by Hao Sheng in Toronto, Canada. Sheng previously worked at Google and Cresta before launching Chatsimple as an AI conversational agent platform for sales and customer support. The company grew to serve over 25,000 businesses. In 2025 Chatsimple rebranded to Expertise AI. The company is backed by AWS Startup Showcase.
Chatsimple.summary_abuse: Chatsimple is an AI chatbot builder that became Expertise AI, primarily used by B2B companies for sales automation. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is positioned for legitimate lead generation and customer support automation with account requirements reducing low-quality use.

ChatStack.description: ChatStack is a self-hosted live chat software for websites.
ChatStack.history: Chatstack is a self-hosted PHP live chat software that allows website owners to deploy their own live chat system without relying on third-party SaaS services. The product offers unlimited operators and departments with an embedded chat window. As a self-hosted PHP application, it has been a niche choice for technically-minded website owners who prefer data sovereignty over convenience.
ChatStack.summary_abuse: Chatstack is a self-hosted PHP live chat application. As a self-hosted product installed on the user's own server, Chatstack itself has no abuse surface distinct from the server it runs on. The vendor does not process or store chat data from customer deployments. No notable abuse incidents are associated with Chatstack.

Chatt.description: Chatt is an all-in-one platform that provides businesses with reviews, messaging, and payment solutions.
Chatt.history: Chatt is a customer communication platform for local businesses, offering webchat, reviews, messaging, and payments under the brand CHATT. The platform is designed to help small businesses engage with customers more easily. Limited public information is available about its exact founding year and company background.
Chatt.summary_abuse: Chatt is a small business customer communication platform primarily used for legitimate local business customer engagement. No documented abuse incidents are known. The paid-only model with business identity requirements reduces potential for misuse.

ChatThing.description: Chat Thing is a platform that provides tools to build AI agents trained on custom content.
ChatThing.history: ChatThing was founded in 2022 in the United Kingdom as an AI bot builder platform allowing businesses to create custom ChatGPT-powered chatbots trained on their own data sources such as websites, Notion pages, and uploaded files. The platform grew during the 2022-2023 AI chatbot boom, targeting SMBs needing no-code AI customer support deployment.
ChatThing.summary_abuse: ChatThing is a niche AI chatbot builder with low abuse exposure. The platform requires user accounts and is used for legitimate customer support automation. No documented abuse incidents have been identified. The free tier is limited enough to deter mass misuse. Vendor publishes standard terms of service.

Chatway.description: Chatway is a live chat tool for websites, designed to facilitate customer engagement through real-time conversations.
Chatway.history: Chatway is a live chat product developed by Premio (Poptin LTD), an Israeli software company known for popup and marketing tools. Premio launched Chatway as a dedicated live chat solution for websites, offering a free tier and AI chatbot capabilities. The platform is distributed as a Shopify app and WordPress plugin.
Chatway.summary_abuse: Chatway is a legitimate business live chat tool developed by an established Israeli SaaS company. No abuse incidents have been documented. The platform is primarily used for customer support and lead generation by small businesses and ecommerce stores.

Chatwee.description: Chatwee is a live chat and instant messaging app designed to facilitate real-time communication on websites and online communities.
Chatwee.history: Chatwee has been operating as a live chat and community chat widget provider since 2012, based in Poznan, Poland. The company bootstrapped its development as a SaaS group chat solution for online communities, forums, and websites. No major ownership changes or acquisitions have occurred.
Chatwee.summary_abuse: Chatwee is a group chat widget for online communities and websites. The public-facing nature of community chat rooms means there is some potential for spam and inappropriate content. No major abuse incidents involving the vendor are documented. The company has been operating since 2012 as a GDPR-compliant Polish SaaS.

ChatWING.description: ChatWING is a live website chat system designed to facilitate real-time communication between businesses and website visitors.
ChatWING.history: Chatwing was founded in 2012 in Las Vegas, Nevada as a real-time live chat widget platform for websites and blogs. The company has remained a small bootstrapped operation with around 3 employees and approximately $330K in annual revenue as of 2025. It has maintained steady operation without major ownership changes or acquisitions.
ChatWING.summary_abuse: Chatwing provides embeddable chat rooms for websites, which carries moderate abuse risk due to the potential for spam and inappropriate content in public chat widgets. The platform has basic moderation tools. No major documented abuse incidents specific to the platform are known, though chat widgets embedded on third-party sites can attract spam bots.

ChatWith.io.description: ChatWith.io is a versatile platform enabling WhatsApp link management, statistics tracking, and specialised services like WhatsApp Business Dating and widgets for efficient customer engagement.
ChatWith.io.history: ChatWith.io was founded around 2022 and is headquartered in Madrid, Spain. The company raised approximately $1.14M from angel and early-stage investors including COREangels and ESC Venture Capital. The platform focuses on helping micro-SMEs and freelancers connect with customers via WhatsApp and AI-powered chatbots trained on custom data.
ChatWith.io.summary_abuse: ChatWith.io is a small AI chatbot builder focused on WhatsApp integration for small businesses. No notable abuse incidents have been documented. The product requires account registration and is targeted at legitimate business use cases. Abuse risk is low given the small user base and SMB focus.

Chatwoot.description: Chatwoot is a customer support tool for instant messaging channels.
Chatwoot.history: Chatwoot was originally developed in 2017 by Sojan Jose and Pranav Raj as a proprietary customer support tool. In 2019, the team open-sourced the project and rebuilt it. They incorporated as Chatwoot Inc. and went through Y Combinator's Winter 2021 batch, raising $1.6 million in seed funding. The project has grown to 22,000+ GitHub stars and is used as an open-source alternative to Intercom and Zendesk.
Chatwoot.summary_abuse: Chatwoot is an open-source customer support platform with a clean abuse profile. The self-hosted nature means most deployments are controlled by enterprise operators with no platform-level abuse vector. The cloud-hosted version requires account signup. No documented misuse incidents are known.

Chaty.description: Chaty is a communication tool that enables customer engagement through messaging apps like WhatsApp and Messenger.
Chaty.history: Chaty is a floating chat widget WordPress plugin and SaaS product developed by Premio, an Israeli company also known for the Poptin popup builder. The plugin allows website owners to add multi-channel chat buttons (WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Telegram) to their website. It has become a popular tool in the WordPress ecosystem.
Chaty.summary_abuse: Chaty is a chat widget plugin that embeds communication channel buttons on websites, primarily used for legitimate customer contact. No documented abuse incidents are known. The plugin simply links users to external messaging apps rather than hosting chat itself, reducing platform-level abuse risk.

Chayns.description: Chayns is a web platform that enables businesses and organizations to create customizable websites and apps, providing tools for digital communication, content management, and ecommerce integration.
Chayns.history: Chayns is a cloud-based operating system and community platform developed by Tobit Software, a German company based in Ahaus, Germany. The platform enables businesses and organizations to create community websites without programming. Over 100,000 companies and organizations use chayns sites, primarily in German-speaking regions. The product evolved from Tobit's enterprise software background.
Chayns.summary_abuse: Chayns is a German community platform used by local businesses and organizations for legitimate community communication. No documented abuse incidents are known. The German data hosting and GDPR compliance posture provide good privacy protection. The primarily German-market focus means very limited international abuse exposure.

Checkfront.description: Checkfront is a cloud-based booking management application and ecommerce platform.
Checkfront.history: Checkfront was founded in June 2010 by Jason Morehouse and Grant Jurgeneit in Victoria, British Columbia, Canada. The online booking management platform focused on tours, activities, and recreational businesses. The company processed over $350 million in annual bookings. In June 2023, Checkfront merged with Australian booking platform Rezdy.
Checkfront.summary_abuse: Checkfront is a B2B booking management platform used by tour operators and recreational businesses. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform handles payment processing and requires business identity, making it unsuitable for anonymous abuse. PCI-DSS compliance is maintained for payment handling.

Checkin.description: Checkin is a system that automates registration processes.
Checkin.history: Checkin.com Group was founded in 2017 in Stockholm, Sweden as an identity verification and KYC onboarding specialist. The company serves regulated industries including gambling, fintech, and travel. Checkin.com Group has been listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market since 2021 under the trading symbol CHECK. In 2021 they acquired GetID for $9.5M to add face biometrics.
Checkin.summary_abuse: Checkin.com is an identity verification and KYC platform used by regulated industries to prevent fraud and verify user identities. The platform is designed to reduce abuse rather than enable it. No documented misuse incidents are known. The company is publicly listed in Sweden and operates under strict KYC/AML regulatory frameworks.

Checkly.description: Checkly is the API and E2E monitoring platform for the modern stack: programmable, flexible and loving JavaScript.
Checkly.history: Checkly was founded in 2018 by Hannes Lenke and Tim Nolet in Berlin, Germany. Lenke previously founded TestObject and served as General Manager EMEA at Sauce Labs. The company raised a $20 million Series B led by Balderton Capital in 2024. Checkly pioneered monitoring-as-code built on Microsoft's Playwright framework and runs 32.5 million checks daily for customers including Vercel, 1Password, and Autodesk.
Checkly.summary_abuse: Checkly is a synthetic monitoring and API testing platform used exclusively by engineering teams for legitimate uptime and reliability monitoring. No abuse incidents are documented. The developer tooling nature and B2B customer base with paid plans make Checkly essentially abuse-free.

Checkout Champ.description: Checkout Champ is an ecommerce store optimizer designed to enhance store performance and profitability.
Checkout Champ.history: Checkout Champ is an ecommerce performance platform founded by Matt Martorano that bolts onto existing ecommerce tech stacks including Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and Magento. The platform focuses on high-converting checkout optimization, subscription billing, and multi-store management with over 500 integrations.
Checkout Champ.summary_abuse: Checkout Champ is an ecommerce checkout optimization tool used by legitimate online retailers. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform handles payments and requires business identity verification. Some checkout optimization tools in this space can be used to enable aggressive upsell dark patterns, but no specific documented issues exist for Checkout Champ.

Checkout.com.description: Checkout.com is an international payment platform that processes different payment methods across a variety of currencies.
Checkout.com.history: Checkout.com was founded in 2009 by Guillaume Pousaz as Opus Payments in Singapore, rebranded as Checkout.com in 2012 when headquarters moved to London. Pousaz operated without external funding for 7 years. In 2019, the company raised $230M and by 2022 achieved a $40 billion valuation making it the most valuable European fintech. The company processes billions in payments for enterprises globally.
Checkout.com.summary_abuse: Checkout.com is a major global payments processor used by large enterprises and regulated businesses. The company maintains strict KYC/AML compliance and PCI-DSS certification. Like all payment processors, it is occasionally implicated in cases where merchants use it for fraud, but Checkout.com maintains robust fraud detection and AML controls. No major systemic abuse incidents have been documented.

CheerPJ.description: CheerpJ is a WebAssembly-based Java Virtual Machine and OpenJDK distribution designed to run Java applications in modern browsers.
CheerPJ.history: CheerpJ is a WebAssembly-based JVM that runs Java applications in modern browsers without plugins, developed by Leaning Technologies, a UK company specializing in compiler technologies. The project began around 2017 and has gone through major versions including v2.0 with WebAssembly support and v3.0 in 2023 providing a full JVM in WebAssembly/HTML5. It is used to run legacy Java applications, applets, and Oracle Forms in modern browsers.
CheerPJ.summary_abuse: CheerpJ is a specialized technical tool for running Java in browsers, used primarily by enterprises needing to preserve legacy Java application access without plugins. No abuse incidents are documented. The tool has no consumer-facing surface and is used exclusively by developers and enterprises.

Chefpreneur.description: Chefpreneur is a platform providing solutions for starting or enhancing personal chef businesses.
Chefpreneur.history: Chefpreneur was founded in 2017 by Andres Hinojosa in San Diego, California. The platform began from Hinojosa's personal experience with burnout in the traditional restaurant industry and his desire to teach personal chef business skills. The faith-based organization provides training, coaching, and community for chefs launching personal chef businesses.
Chefpreneur.summary_abuse: Chefpreneur is a small online education platform for personal chef business training. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is a niche B2C training product with a faith-based community focus, posing virtually no risk of being used for malicious purposes.

Chekkit.description: Chekkit is an all-in-one review, messaging, and lead inbox software.
Chekkit.history: Chekkit was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Winnipeg, Canada. The platform provides review management, business messaging, webchat, and payment tools for small businesses. The company raised approximately $20K in seed funding.
Chekkit.summary_abuse: Chekkit is a small business messaging and review management platform. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is used for legitimate local business customer communications and reputation management. The small scale and local business focus mean limited abuse exposure.

Cherokee.history: Cherokee web server was first developed by Alvaro Lopez Ortega starting in 2006 and grew into a community-driven open-source project. The server became notable for being adopted by IoT device manufacturers including GoPro, Xiaomi, and Digi International. The first stable release was version 1.2.104 in April 2014. Development became dormant after the mid-2010s with the last commit in January 2023.
Cherokee.summary_abuse: Cherokee is an open-source web server with no documented abuse incidents. As a self-hosted web server, it has no platform-level abuse surface. The project is largely dormant and used by IoT device manufacturers and niche deployments.

CherryPy.description: CherryPy is an object-oriented web application framework using the Python programming language.
CherryPy.history: CherryPy was created by Remi Delon in 2002 as one of the earliest Python web frameworks, designed to be as Pythonic as possible. Version 2.0 was released in May 2005 and moved to BitBucket. Version 3.0 was released in December 2006 with much of the code rewritten under Robert Brewer as lead developer. The framework has remained in community maintenance and is one of the longest-lived Python web frameworks.
CherryPy.summary_abuse: CherryPy is a minimalist Python web framework with no platform-level abuse surface. As an open-source framework installed locally, there is no vendor-controlled component that could be abused. No security incidents specific to the framework itself are documented.

Chevereto.description: Chevereto is an image hosting software that allows you to create a full-featured image hosting website on your own server.
Chevereto.history: Chevereto is a self-hosted image and video hosting script created and maintained by Rodolfo Berrios in Chile since 2007, with the first public release in 2009. The software is a single-developer project that has been regularly updated. The free version (Chevereto-Free) was discontinued and the software moved to a fully commercial model. It is used to build custom image hosting services similar to Imgur.
Chevereto.summary_abuse: Chevereto-powered image hosting sites have historically been used for hosting CSAM, malware distribution, and copyright-infringing content. Because Chevereto is self-hosted software sold to third parties who run their own image hosting services, the abuse happens on those downstream deployments rather than at the vendor level. Sites running Chevereto with anonymous uploads have been repeatedly flagged for hosting illegal content. The software itself enables anonymous public image uploads when configured that way.

Chicago Boss.description: Chicago Boss is a web framework for Erlang.
Chicago Boss.history: Chicago Boss is an Erlang web framework inspired by Ruby on Rails, created by Evan Miller around 2010 with first releases documented that year. The framework gained a small following in the Erlang community. In January 2014, Evan Miller retired from the project and Zachary Kessin and Dmitry Polyanovsky took over development. The last release was in 2017 and the project has been effectively dormant since.
Chicago Boss.summary_abuse: Chicago Boss is a dormant Erlang web framework with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse incidents. The framework is self-hosted developer tooling with essentially zero abuse potential.

CHIIRP.description: CHIIRP is an AI-powered lead conversion tool for trade businesses that captures and manages calls and inquiries to support timely follow-up and improved lead conversion.
CHIIRP.history: CHIIRP is an AI-powered lead conversion tool targeting home services and trade businesses, providing automated SMS and email follow-up for inbound leads.
CHIIRP.summary_abuse: CHIIRP is an outbound SMS and email automation tool for trade businesses. Mass-SMS platforms carry inherent risk of being used for spam or unsolicited messaging. No specific large-scale abuse incidents documented for CHIIRP.

Chili Piper.description: Chili Piper is a suite of automated scheduling tools that help revenue teams convert leads.
Chili Piper.history: Chili Piper was founded in 2016 by Nicolas Vandenberghe and Alina Vandenberghe in New York City as a remote-first company. The company focuses on inbound conversion software for B2B revenue teams. Chili Piper raised $64.84M total including a $33M Series B in 2021 led by Tiger Global. Customers include Intuit, Spotify, Airbnb, and Gong.
Chili Piper.summary_abuse: Chili Piper is a B2B sales scheduling and inbound conversion platform used exclusively by enterprise and mid-market revenue teams. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is authenticated and used for legitimate sales operations with known business clients.

Chimpify.description: Chimpify is an inbound marketing platform that consolidates content management, lead generation, and campaign tracking to support data-driven audience engagement.
Chimpify.history: Chimpify was founded in 2017 in Bremerhaven, Germany, evolving from the German blogging community der Affenblog. The platform is an all-in-one creator marketing solution for coaches and small business owners, combining website builder, email marketing, online courses, and checkout. Data is stored on German servers, making it a privacy-focused GDPR-compliant alternative.
Chimpify.summary_abuse: Chimpify is a German creator marketing platform focused on coaches and small business owners. No documented abuse incidents are known. The German data hosting and strong GDPR compliance make it one of the more privacy-respectful options in the website builder space. Low abuse risk given the known business identity requirements.

Chimpmatic.description: Chimpmatic is a premium Contact Form 7 and Mailchimp integration plugin.
Chimpmatic.history: Chimpmatic is a WordPress plugin that integrates Contact Form 7 with Mailchimp without requiring code, allowing marketers to sync form submissions to Mailchimp lists with tags, groups, and GDPR-compliant opt-ins. It is a third-party Mailchimp integration plugin developed independently from Mailchimp/Intuit.
Chimpmatic.summary_abuse: Chimpmatic is a WordPress plugin for Mailchimp form integration. No documented abuse incidents are known. The plugin is used by WordPress site owners for legitimate email list management and does not have any consumer-facing abuse surface.

Chinese Menu Online.description: Chinese Menu Online is an online food ordering service.
Chinese Menu Online.history: Chinese Menu Online is a niche US-based platform providing online ordering solutions specifically for Chinese and Asian restaurants. The company offers branded websites with integrated online ordering. Limited public information is available about the company's founding year or leadership.
Chinese Menu Online.summary_abuse: Chinese Menu Online is a vertical-specific restaurant online ordering platform. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is used by legitimate local Chinese and Asian restaurants for customer ordering.

Chitika.history: Chitika was founded in 2003 by Venkat Kolluri and Alden DoRosario after leaving Lycos, and launched its online advertising service in 2004. The company operated from Westborough, Massachusetts. Chitika provided search-targeted contextual advertising and in 2013 announced a multi-year extension of its Yahoo partnership. On April 17, 2019, Chitika announced it was shutting down. Its assets were later purchased by Poll the People.
Chitika.summary_abuse: Chitika was an ad network that shut down in 2019. As an ad network, it was associated with typical ad-tech privacy concerns including tracking across websites. The company was implicated in serving low-quality and potentially deceptive ads typical of the display ad industry of its era. Since it was a pre-GDPR ad network operating through 2019, it collected user data without modern privacy standards.
