Choices.description: Choices.js is a lightweight, configurable select box/text input plugin.
Choices.history: Choices.js is an open-source JavaScript library providing a customizable select box and text input plugin, created as a modern alternative to the deprecated Chosen jQuery plugin. It is maintained by Joshua Johnson and the community on GitHub. No major ownership changes.
Choices.summary_abuse: Choices.js is a front-end UI library with no backend component and no abuse surface. It is used by developers to enhance select boxes and text inputs in web applications.

Chord.description: Chord is a video-enabled social community and communication platform completely customised to your brand.
Chord.history: Chord was founded in 2021 by Bryan Mahoney and Henry Davis, formerly Chief Technology Officer and Chief Commercial Officer at Glossier. The company started as Arfa before rebranding to Chord Commerce. They raised $18M in a Series A led by Eclipse Ventures and acquired data platform Yaguara. In 2023 they raised an additional $15M extension to their Series A.
Chord.summary_abuse: Chord is a headless commerce platform for enterprise DTC brands. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is exclusively B2B and used by established brands for ecommerce infrastructure.

Chorus.description: Chorus is the only all-in-one publishing, audience, and revenue platform built for modern media companies.
Chorus.history: Chorus.ai was founded in 2015 by Micha Breakstone, Russell Levy, and Roy Raanani in San Francisco. The company developed AI-powered conversation intelligence for sales teams, recording and analyzing prospect calls. In July 2021, ZoomInfo acquired Chorus.ai for approximately $575 million. The product now operates as ZoomInfo Chorus within the ZoomInfo suite.
Chorus.summary_abuse: Chorus.ai is a conversation intelligence and call recording platform owned by ZoomInfo. The main privacy concern is the recording and analysis of sales calls, which requires consent compliance in applicable jurisdictions. ZoomInfo itself has faced scrutiny for data collection practices. No documented abuse specific to the Chorus product is known.

Chosen.description: Chosen is a jQuery plugin that makes long, unwieldy select boxes much more user-friendly.
Chosen.history: Chosen was created by Patrick Filler for Harvest, a time-tracking SaaS company, and released as open-source. The library made unwieldy HTML select boxes more user-friendly with search and multi-select. It was widely adopted in the early 2010s but has been officially deprecated. The last version is 1.8.7. Development has stopped and the maintainers recommend alternatives.
Chosen.summary_abuse: Chosen is a deprecated front-end JavaScript library for styling select boxes. It has no backend component and no abuse surface.

chroma.js.description: chroma.js is a small-ish zero-dependency JavaScript library for all kinds of color conversions and color scales.
chroma.js.history: chroma.js was created by Gregor Aisch and released around 2011 as an open-source JavaScript library for color manipulation. It has no backing company and is widely used by data visualization developers.
chroma.js.summary_abuse: chroma.js is a color manipulation JavaScript library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure utility library with no server-side or consumer-facing surface.

Chronofresh.description: Chronofresh is an express transport service for food products.
Chronofresh.history: Chronofresh was launched in May 2015 as a subsidiary of Chronopost, which is owned by Geopost (DPDgroup), a subsidiary of La Poste group. Chronofresh provides express cold-chain delivery of fresh, chilled, and frozen products within France and internationally. By 2022, Chronofresh had reached revenues of €120 million, a 23% increase from 2021.
Chronofresh.summary_abuse: Chronofresh is a cold-chain express delivery service subsidiary of Chronopost/La Poste group. No documented abuse incidents are known. The service is used by food producers and e-commerce operators for legitimate temperature-controlled logistics.

Chronopost.description: Chronopost provides a domestic and international express shipping and delivery service.
Chronopost.history: Chronopost was founded in 1985 as SFMI (Société Française de Messagerie Internationale), a joint venture between La Poste and DHL, adopting its current name in 1992. It became France's leading express courier service and a member of the GeoPost group alongside DPD. In 2001, Chronopost established an alliance with FedEx for international air routing. The company has 3,800 employees and delivers 180 million packages per year in over 230 countries.
Chronopost.summary_abuse: Chronopost is a major French express delivery company and subsidiary of La Poste. No significant platform-level abuse incidents are documented. As with all parcel services, fraudulent tracking notifications using the Chronopost brand are commonly used in phishing scams targeting French consumers, but this is impersonation by third parties rather than platform misuse.

ChurnZero.description: ChurnZero is a real-time customer success platform that helps subscription businesses fight customer churn.
ChurnZero.history: ChurnZero was founded in 2015 by You Mon Tsang and Mark Heys, headquartered in Washington D.C. The company built a customer success platform for subscription businesses to reduce churn and grow revenue. ChurnZero became G2's number one Customer Success software, was named 2024 CODIE winner, and earned recognition from Forrester as a leading platform.
ChurnZero.summary_abuse: ChurnZero is a B2B customer success platform used exclusively by SaaS companies to manage their customer relationships. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is authenticated, paid, and used by professional customer success teams for legitimate business operations.

Ciklik.description: Ciklik is an ecommerce platform that empowers individuals to establish online stores and market their subscription-based products.
Ciklik.history: Ciklik was founded in 2019 in Paris, France as a subscription management platform for ecommerce. The company supports over 300 customers in sectors including food, media, sports, and pets. Ciklik partnered with Payplug to offer a plug-and-play PrestaShop subscription solution.
Ciklik.summary_abuse: Ciklik is a French B2B subscription management platform for ecommerce merchants. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is GDPR-compliant with French hosting and is used by legitimate merchants for subscription billing.

CIMcloud.description: CIMcloud is a customer interaction management platform that integrates ecommerce capabilities.
CIMcloud.history: CIMcloud is a customer interaction management platform integrating e-commerce with ERP systems. It targets B2B manufacturers and distributors.
CIMcloud.summary_abuse: CIMcloud is a B2B e-commerce and ERP integration platform. No significant abuse surface or documented incidents.

CINC.description: CINC is a lead acquisition and conversion platform for teams and top real estate agents.
CINC.history: CINC (Commissions Inc.) is a real estate lead generation and CRM platform founded in 2011 in Atlanta, Georgia. It provides IDX websites, lead capture, and follow-up tools for real estate agents and teams.
CINC.summary_abuse: CINC is a real estate CRM and lead generation platform. Real estate tech platforms can be used for spam marketing to home buyers and sellers. No major documented abuse incidents specific to CINC.

Circle.description: Circie is an all-in-one community platform for businesses that brings together engaging courses, discussions, members, live streams, chat, events, and memberships.
Circle.history: Circle was founded in 2019-2020 by Sid Yadav, Andrew Guttormsen, and Rudy Santino, all former Teachable employees. The company raised $33.3M across 5 funding rounds including a Tiger Global-led round at a $200M valuation. The platform grew to 12 million users across Circle communities by 2025 and a team of 180+ across 30+ countries.
Circle.summary_abuse: Circle is a community platform used by creators, educators, and brands. No documented abuse incidents are known. The paid-only model with community operator accountability reduces large-scale abuse risk. The platform can host any type of community content, requiring operators to enforce their own content policies.

Citizen Space.description: Citizen Space is an engagement platform by Delib that enhances democratic processes globally.
Citizen Space.history: Citizen Space is a digital public consultation platform developed by Delib, a UK-based digital democracy company operating since 2001. The platform was originally developed as a joint initiative with the UK government to improve statutory consultation. It has been in continuous development for over 15 years and is used by hundreds of government organisations worldwide.
Citizen Space.summary_abuse: Citizen Space is a government consultation platform used exclusively by public authorities for statutory engagement with citizens. No abuse incidents are documented. The platform is used by central governments, local authorities, and regulators for legitimate democratic processes.

Citrix.description: Citrix is a people-centric solutions provider offering software that enables employees to work and collaborate remotely, irrespective of their device or network.
Citrix.history: Citrix was founded in 1989 in Richardson, Texas by Ed Iacobucci, a former IBM developer. The company went public in 1995 and became a leader in thin client and remote desktop technology. In 2007, Citrix acquired XenSource to enter server virtualization. In 2022, Vista Equity Partners and Evergreen Coast Capital completed a $16.5 billion acquisition of Citrix, which merged with TIBCO Software to form Cloud Software Group.
Citrix.summary_abuse: Citrix is a foundational enterprise remote access and virtualization platform. Critical vulnerabilities in Citrix products have historically been exploited by threat actors, most notably the 2019 Citrix ADC vulnerability (CVE-2019-19781) which was widely exploited for ransomware deployment. These are security vulnerabilities rather than platform-level abuse. Citrix has a mature vulnerability disclosure and patching program.

CitrusLime.description: CitrusLime is an end-to-end cloud retail solution that supports inventory management, point-of-sale operations, and online store integration within a unified platform.
CitrusLime.history: Citrus-Lime was founded in 1999 by Neil McQuillan in Ulverston in the Lake District, England, combining a love of the outdoors with a software background. The company specialises in cloud-based point of sale and ecommerce solutions for cycle, outdoor, and equestrian independent retailers. The platform processed over £1 billion in retail transactions. The company employs 50 people and expanded to the US market in 2022.
CitrusLime.summary_abuse: Citrus-Lime is a niche B2B retail management software provider serving independent outdoor and cycling retailers. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is sold exclusively to legitimate retailers and handles in-store POS transactions.

CitrusPay.description: CitrusPay provides payement gateway and wallet services.
CitrusPay.history: CitrusPay was founded in 2011 in Mumbai, India by Jitendra Gupta, Amrish Rau, and Satyen Kothari as an online payment gateway and wallet for Indian businesses and consumers. The company raised $32.5M from Peak XV Partners, Ascent Capital, and Beenos. In 2016, PayU India acquired CitrusPay for approximately $130 million. The brand was subsequently integrated into PayU's India operations.
CitrusPay.summary_abuse: CitrusPay was an Indian payment gateway that was acquired by PayU India in 2016 and has been deprecated as a standalone brand. No major abuse incidents specific to the platform are documented. As a payment processor, it was subject to standard fraud risks.

City Hive.description: City Hive's all in one ecommerce platform for wine and spirit shops.
City Hive.history: City Hive was founded in 2014 and is headquartered in New York, USA. The company built an all-in-one ecommerce platform specifically for wine, beer, and spirits shops, facilitating connections within the three-tier alcohol distribution system. The platform helps liquor retailers sell online.
City Hive.summary_abuse: City Hive is a vertical ecommerce platform for licensed alcohol retailers operating in a regulated industry. No documented abuse incidents are known. The alcohol retail sector requires regulatory compliance including age verification, reducing the likelihood of significant platform abuse.

CityMail.description: CityMail is a private postal organisation operating in Sweden.
CityMail.history: CityMail was founded in 1991 in Stockholm, Sweden in a basement on Östermalm, created to challenge what was then a Swedish postal monopoly. The company delivers mail and packages to approximately 6 million individuals and businesses in Sweden, competing with state-owned PostNord. Previously owned by Posten Norge as Bring CityMail, it was acquired by Allegra Capital in 2018.
CityMail.summary_abuse: CityMail is a Swedish postal delivery company operating in a regulated postal market. No documented abuse incidents are known. As a physical mail carrier, its digital presence is primarily for business logistics.

CIVIC.description: Civic provides cookie control for user consent and the use of cookies.
CIVIC.history: CIVIC Cookie Control is a UK-based consent management platform (CMP) developed by Civic UK, founded around 2012. It provides customizable cookie consent banners and is one of the earlier CMPs focusing on the UK/EU market.
CIVIC.summary_abuse: CIVIC Cookie Control is a consent management platform with no meaningful abuse surface. It is a client-side tool that captures user consent preferences. No documented abuse incidents.

Civic Champs.description: Civic Champs is a volunteer management platform that streamlines event coordination, tracks volunteer hours, and simplifies communication between organizations and their volunteers.
Civic Champs.history: Civic Champs was founded in 2018 by Geng Wang, Chris Borland, and Michael Jeffery in Bloomington, Indiana. The company provides volunteer management software for nonprofits, helping organizations automate volunteer operations and convert volunteers to donors. The company raised $1.51M in funding and is incorporated as a Benefit Corporation.
Civic Champs.summary_abuse: Civic Champs is a nonprofit volunteer management platform. No documented abuse incidents are known. The platform is used exclusively by nonprofits for legitimate volunteer coordination.

CivicPlus.description: CivicPlus is a government community engagement tool designed to facilitate communication, collaboration, and public participation.
CivicPlus.history: CivicPlus originated from Networks Plus, co-founded by Ward Morgan and Tony Gagnon, which was incorporated in Kansas in 1998. The company evolved into CivicPlus under Icon Enterprises to provide website and technology solutions for local government. CivicPlus raised $474M from Insight Partners and BV Investment Partners. Today the platform is used by over 13,000 government organizations serving 340 million people in the US and Canada.
CivicPlus.summary_abuse: CivicPlus is a government technology platform used exclusively by local governments and public sector organizations. No documented abuse incidents are known. The highly regulated public sector customer base and compliance requirements make abuse essentially impossible.

CiviCRM.description: CiviCRM is a web-based suite of internationalised open-source software for constituency relationship management.
CiviCRM.history: CiviCRM was founded in 2005 by Dave Greenberg and Donald Lobo as an open-source constituent relationship management system for nonprofits, originally released as a Drupal module by CivicSpace Labs. The project has been governed as a California LLC operating as a not-for-profit. It has been the leading open-source CRM for nonprofits for 20 years, used by over 14,000 organizations worldwide.
CiviCRM.summary_abuse: CiviCRM is an open-source nonprofit CRM with no documented abuse incidents. As self-hosted software used exclusively by nonprofits, NGOs, and advocacy organizations, it has essentially zero abuse surface at the platform level.

CiviCRM plugins.description: CiviCRM is a web-based suite of internationalised open-source software for constituency relationship management.
CiviCRM plugins.history: CiviCRM plugins are extensions and add-ons developed by the CiviCRM community to extend the functionality of the CiviCRM open-source nonprofit CRM, which launched in 2005. The extension ecosystem evolved shortly after CiviCRM's creation, with community members contributing integrations for payment processors, CMS platforms, and specialized nonprofit workflows.
CiviCRM plugins.summary_abuse: CiviCRM plugins are open-source community extensions for the nonprofit CRM platform. No documented abuse incidents are known. The extension ecosystem is used exclusively by nonprofits and advocacy organizations.

CivicTheme.description: CivicTheme is an open source, inclusive and component-based design system. It was created so governments and corporations can rapidly assemble modern, consistent and compliant digital experiences.
CivicTheme.history: CivicTheme is an open-source Drupal 10 design system and theme created by Salsa Digital, an Australian digital agency, for use in Australian government websites and GovCMS. The project was initiated around 2021 to provide a WCAG-compliant, component-based design system aligned with Australian Government Architecture. It received recognition as part of the Australian Government Architecture (AGA) framework.
CivicTheme.summary_abuse: CivicTheme is an open-source Drupal theme for government websites. No abuse incidents are documented. The project is used exclusively for government website implementations in Australia.

CJDropshipping app.description: CJDropshipping is a dropshipping supplier and fulfillment service from China.
CJDropshipping app.history: CJDropshipping is a Chinese dropshipping and order fulfillment service founded around 2015, offering product sourcing, white-label packing, and shipping for e-commerce sellers worldwide. It integrates with Shopify and other e-commerce platforms.
CJDropshipping app.summary_abuse: CJDropshipping is a Chinese dropshipping fulfillment service. The dropshipping model is frequently associated with scam storefronts — fake online shops that collect payment but deliver counterfeit goods or nothing. CJDropshipping itself is a legitimate supplier, but it is widely used by fraudulent storefronts targeting consumers in Western markets. The company does not perform meaningful vetting of the merchants using its fulfillment services. Consumer fraud reports referencing CJDropshipping are common across complaint databases.

Ckan.history: CKAN (Comprehensive Knowledge Archive Network) was created in 2006 by Rufus Pollock and the Open Knowledge Foundation in the UK as an open-source data management system. It powers government open data portals worldwide, including data.gov (USA), data.gov.uk (UK), the European Union data portal, and hundreds of other national and regional portals. Written in Python using the Pylons framework, it provides robust dataset cataloging, metadata management, search, and API access. The project is stewarded by the CKAN Association and maintained by an active international community.
Ckan.summary_abuse: CKAN is a well-established open-source platform with no significant abuse concerns. It is widely used by government agencies and NGOs for open data publishing. The AGPL-3.0 license, active community, and public-sector adoption contribute to strong transparency and accountability. No notable incidents of data misuse or malicious use have been documented.

CKEditor.description: CKEditor is a WYSIWYG rich text editor which enables writing content directly inside of web pages or online applications. Its core code is written in JavaScript and it is developed by CKSource. CKEditor is available under open-source and commercial licenses.
CKEditor.history: CKEditor began in 2003 as FCKeditor, created by Brazilian developer Frederico Caldeira Knabben as an open-source WYSIWYG editor. CKSource was formally established in 2006. The editor was rebranded CKEditor 3 in 2009. CKEditor 4 launched in December 2012. The major CKEditor 5 rewrite launched in 2018 with a dual-license model requiring commercial licenses for proprietary use. CKEditor has reached 50M+ downloads and hundreds of millions of users in 20+ years.
CKEditor.summary_abuse: CKEditor is a rich text editor component embedded in many web applications. As a client-side editing widget it has no meaningful independent abuse surface. XSS vulnerabilities in rich text editors are a known class of issue, and CKEditor patches these promptly. No notable use in abuse campaigns documented.

Clarifai.description: Clarifai is a unified platform that enables creation and management of AI workloads while optimizing compute costs across preferred environments.
Clarifai.history: Clarifai was founded in 2013 by Matthew Zeiler, a deep learning researcher who won the ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge in 2013. Based in New York City, the company provides AI-powered image, video, and text recognition APIs. Clarifai raised over $101 million in venture capital across multiple funding rounds, attracting major investors. The platform offers visual recognition, natural language processing, and multi-modal AI capabilities targeting developers and enterprises across defense, retail, media, and healthcare sectors.
Clarifai.summary_abuse: Clarifai is a legitimate AI platform with no significant documented abuse patterns. Privacy considerations exist as the platform processes large volumes of images and video which may include sensitive personal data. The company's AI services are also used by government and defense customers, raising some civil liberties concerns around surveillance use cases. Users should review data retention and processing policies carefully.

Clarip.description: Clarip is an enterprise data privacy and risk management platform.
Clarip.history: Clarip was founded in 2016 by Andy Sambandam in Wilmington, Delaware. The company provides a consent management and data privacy compliance platform, helping businesses manage GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulation requirements. Clarip is a seed-stage startup focused on enterprise privacy management with tools for consent capture, data subject request handling, and cookie compliance.
Clarip.summary_abuse: Clarip is a privacy-focused consent management platform with no known abuse history. The company's product is designed to help organizations comply with privacy regulations, which aligns with a pro-privacy stance. As a small startup, limited independent security auditing has been conducted, but no malicious behavior or data misuse has been reported.

Claris FileMaker.description: Claris FileMaker is a cross-platform relational database application from Claris International.
Claris FileMaker.history: Claris International was originally founded in 1987 as a subsidiary of Apple Computer. It acquired the FileMaker database product in 1988 from Nashoba Systems. Over the decades, FileMaker became the flagship low-code database application platform, widely adopted by small and medium businesses. In 2019, Apple rebranded the subsidiary back to Claris International, expanding its product portfolio beyond FileMaker. Claris FileMaker remains the company's core product, enabling non-developers to build custom database applications for macOS, iOS, Windows, and the web.
Claris FileMaker.summary_abuse: Claris FileMaker is a mature, Apple-backed database platform with no significant abuse history. As an Apple subsidiary, it benefits from Apple's security practices and responsible disclosure program. The platform is used extensively by legitimate businesses for internal data management. No notable incidents of malicious use, privacy violations, or dark patterns have been documented.

Clarity.description: Clarity is an open-source design system that brings together UX guidelines, an HTML/CSS framework, and Angular components.
Clarity.history: Microsoft Clarity is a free behavioral analytics tool launched by Microsoft in 2020. It provides session recordings, heatmaps, and user interaction insights for website owners. Built on Microsoft's infrastructure, Clarity is offered completely free as a product to attract users into Microsoft's broader analytics and Azure ecosystem. It quickly gained popularity due to its zero-cost model and ease of integration, becoming a widely-used alternative to paid tools like Hotjar.
Clarity.summary_abuse: Microsoft Clarity collects detailed user session data including mouse movements, clicks, and scrolling behavior, which raises some privacy considerations. As a free product, Microsoft uses the data to improve its own products and services. The privacy policy discloses this data use. While no explicit malicious abuse has been documented, the extensive behavioral tracking means websites using Clarity are sharing their visitors' detailed interaction data with Microsoft. GDPR compliance measures are in place but the data sharing with Microsoft warrants disclosure to end users.

Clarivoy.description: Clarivoy is an automotive marketing campaign tool.
Clarivoy.history: Clarivoy was founded in 2009 by Steve White in Columbus, Ohio. The company provides multi-touch marketing attribution software specifically for automotive dealerships. It raised approximately $2 million in funding and focuses on helping car dealers understand which marketing channels drive vehicle sales. The platform uses probabilistic data matching to connect online advertising to offline dealership visits and vehicle purchases.
Clarivoy.summary_abuse: Clarivoy is a niche automotive marketing analytics platform with limited public information about its privacy practices. The company's cross-channel tracking for automotive dealerships involves collecting consumer journey data. No major documented abuse incidents exist, but the low transparency around data handling and limited compliance certifications are concerns. The platform is primarily used by automotive dealers and marketing agencies.

Claspo.description: Claspo is a pop-up builder designed to generate and capture leads, increase sales, and grow subscriber lists.
Claspo.history: Claspo was founded in 2021 in Kharkiv, Ukraine, as a popup and widget builder for websites. The platform enables marketers to create lead capture forms, exit-intent popups, and promotional banners without coding. As a bootstrapped startup from Ukraine, Claspo targets e-commerce and digital marketing professionals seeking an easy-to-use popup solution. The company launched with a freemium model and expanded its template library and targeting capabilities.
Claspo.summary_abuse: Claspo is a popup builder tool that by its nature enables the creation of potentially intrusive user interfaces such as exit-intent popups, countdown timers, and aggressive lead capture forms that may contribute to dark patterns on websites where it is deployed. No direct malicious behavior by Claspo itself has been documented. The platform is a young startup with limited security transparency. Users deploying Claspo should be mindful of how popup campaigns are designed to avoid manipulative UX.

ClassCreator.description: ClassCreator is a school class reunion planning tool that helps organize events, manage guest lists, and track RSVPs, streamlining the coordination of reunions.
ClassCreator.history: ClassCreator is a website platform designed for school class reunions, allowing former classmates to create group websites, share memories, and coordinate reunion events. The platform has been operating since the mid-2000s and caters primarily to high school and college alumni groups in the United States. It provides tools for member registration, photo sharing, message boards, and event planning without requiring technical expertise.
ClassCreator.summary_abuse: ClassCreator is a niche reunion website platform with limited public security information. The platform collects personal information including graduation years, contact details, and biographical data from alumni members. No major security incidents or data abuse have been publicly documented, but as a small independent platform, third-party security auditing is likely absent. Users should be aware that personal information shared on the platform has limited privacy protections.

Classeh.description: Classeh is a LMS that allows user to participate in webinars and also use LMS options like messanger,finances,homework,quiz and some extra options like sending messages and more.
Classeh.history: Classeh is a school management platform based in Portugal, providing digital tools for educational institutions including attendance tracking, grade management, communication between teachers and parents, and administrative workflows. The platform targets K-12 schools in Portugal and other Portuguese-speaking markets. Limited public information is available about the company's precise founding date and funding history.
Classeh.summary_abuse: Classeh is a small educational management platform focused on the Portuguese market. As a school management system, it handles sensitive data about minors including attendance records, grades, and family contact information. No public incidents of data breaches or abuse have been documented. As a European company, it falls under GDPR obligations for student data protection. Limited external security validation is available given the company's small size.

ClassFit.description: ClassFit is a provider of a booking and scheduling software for fitness centers.
ClassFit.history: ClassFit is a fitness class scheduling and booking platform designed for fitness studios, gyms, and independent instructors. The software enables class scheduling, member management, payment processing, and attendance tracking. It operates in the boutique fitness software market, competing with platforms like Mindbody. Limited public information is available about the company's founding date and background.
ClassFit.summary_abuse: ClassFit is a small fitness scheduling platform with limited publicly available information about its security practices and company background. No documented abuse incidents or privacy violations have been reported. As a small operator in the fitness software space, its resources for security auditing are likely minimal. Users should review its data handling practices before entrusting customer payment and personal health information to the platform.

Classnames.description: Javascript utility for conditionally joining classNames together.
Classnames.history: Classnames is a tiny open-source JavaScript/TypeScript utility library created by Jed Watson in 2014. It provides a simple function to conditionally join CSS class names together, addressing a common need in React component development. The library became one of the most downloaded npm packages in the world, with hundreds of millions of weekly downloads, as it is used directly or as a dependency in countless React applications and component libraries.
Classnames.summary_abuse: Classnames is a minimal utility library with no known security vulnerabilities or abuse potential. Its codebase is extremely small and straightforward, making it easy to audit. The MIT license, high download counts, and widespread review by the developer community provide strong confidence in its integrity. The library has no network calls, no data collection, and no external dependencies.

Classy.description: Classy is an online fundraising platform.
Classy.history: Classy was founded in 2006 by Scot Chisholm and others in San Diego, California, as an online fundraising platform for nonprofits. The company built a feature-rich fundraising suite including campaign pages, event registration, peer-to-peer fundraising, and donation management. Classy raised significant venture capital and grew to serve thousands of nonprofits. In 2022, GoFundMe acquired Classy to expand into the nonprofit fundraising market. The combined entity operates under the GoFundMe Pro brand for nonprofits while retaining the Classy product name.
Classy.summary_abuse: Classy is a legitimate nonprofit fundraising platform with a strong track record in the charitable sector. As a payments-handling platform, it is PCI DSS compliant and processes donations for nonprofits. Under GoFundMe ownership, it benefits from a larger security infrastructure. No significant fraud or abuse patterns specific to Classy have been documented. Users should note that donor data and payment information are shared with GoFundMe's parent infrastructure.

Clayful.description: Clayful is a Korean-based ecommerce platform focused on link functionality and commerce platform development.
Clayful.history: Clayful is a headless commerce API platform founded in South Korea around 2017. The service provides a backend-as-a-service for building e-commerce applications with APIs for products, orders, customers, and content management. Targeting developers building custom storefronts, Clayful offers a flexible alternative to traditional monolithic e-commerce platforms. The company is based in Seoul and operates primarily in the Asian market.
Clayful.summary_abuse: Clayful is a niche headless commerce platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a developer-focused backend service, it handles e-commerce transaction data and customer information. The limited public information about the company's security posture and the absence of compliance certifications makes independent verification difficult. No evidence of malicious behavior has been found.

CleanCore.description: CleanCore is an AI-driven CRM designed to automate operations for cleaning companies.
CleanCore.history: CleanCore is an AI-driven CRM platform built specifically for residential and commercial cleaning businesses, offering scheduling, client management, and invoicing. The product appears to have been launched post-2020 as part of the vertical SaaS trend for home services companies. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
CleanCore.summary_abuse: CleanCore has no notable abuse signal. It is a vertical CRM tool for cleaning service businesses with no anonymous signup, no content hosting, and no consumer-facing features that would attract fraudsters.

CleanTalk.description: CleanTalk is an anti-spam plugin system that provides spam protection for various types of websites.
CleanTalk.history: CleanTalk is an anti-spam cloud service founded around 2013, headquartered in Ukraine. The platform provides spam protection for websites, particularly WordPress and other CMS platforms, blocking spam registrations, comments, and form submissions. It operates as a cloud-based spam filter using a shared blacklist database of known spam sources. The service integrates with major CMS platforms via plugins and is popular among small and medium-sized website owners.
CleanTalk.summary_abuse: CleanTalk processes website traffic data to identify spam patterns, which means it receives information about website visitors from all sites that use its service. This creates a data aggregation concern as visitor IP addresses and form submissions are analyzed through CleanTalk's centralized service. While the primary purpose is spam prevention, the collection of visitor data across multiple websites warrants careful consideration in a privacy context. No major breaches or explicit misuse incidents have been documented.

Clear.description: Clear is a platform that offers secure identity verification.
Clear.history: CLEAR (formerly Verified Identity Pass) is an identity verification platform founded in 2010 by Caryn Seidman-Becker in New York City. The company offers biometric identity services allowing members to skip airport security lines and verify identity at sports venues, healthcare facilities, and other locations using fingerprints and iris scans. CLEAR went public (NYSE: YOU) in 2021. The company has grown to over 12 million members and operates at major US airports and venues.
Clear.summary_abuse: CLEAR collects highly sensitive biometric data including fingerprints and iris scans from millions of members, creating significant privacy risks if the data is breached or misused. The company has faced scrutiny over its data retention practices and the extent to which it shares biometric data with government agencies or third parties. In 2024, CLEAR faced controversy when it was revealed that it had been selling subscriber data to law enforcement. The company handles extraordinarily sensitive personal data and operates in a regulatory gray area for biometric data in the US.

Clearbit Reveal.description: Clearbit Reveal identifies anonymous visitors to websites.
Clearbit Reveal.history: Clearbit was founded in 2014 by Alex MacCaw and Harlow Ward in San Francisco. The company built a data enrichment and B2B intelligence platform that identifies website visitors by matching IP addresses to company records. Clearbit Reveal specifically identifies which companies are visiting a website without requiring visitor login. In 2023, HubSpot acquired Clearbit for an undisclosed sum, integrating its data capabilities into HubSpot's CRM and marketing platform.
Clearbit Reveal.summary_abuse: Clearbit Reveal is a privacy-invasive tool that de-anonymizes website visitors by reverse-engineering IP addresses to identify companies and individuals. This practice operates in a legal gray area under privacy regulations like GDPR, which requires consent for personal data processing. The service aggregates data from many sources to build extensive profiles of individuals and companies. Privacy advocates have flagged IP-to-company resolution as a form of covert surveillance. While widely used in B2B marketing, it raises significant consent and transparency concerns for website visitors.

Clearlink.description: Clearlink connects brands with customers who are likely to convert and provides information on partnerships and career opportunities.
Clearlink.history: Clearlink is a performance marketing company founded in 2003 in Salt Lake City, Utah. The company operates as an affiliate marketing and digital advertising services firm, specializing in customer acquisition for telecommunications, insurance, and home services industries. Clearlink connects consumers with service providers through online marketing channels and operates comparison websites. The company has faced regulatory scrutiny over its marketing practices in the past.
Clearlink.summary_abuse: Clearlink operates in the performance marketing and lead generation space, which carries inherent risks of aggressive marketing practices. The company has been involved in controversy regarding misleading advertising and dark patterns in online marketing. As an affiliate marketer, its business model incentivizes clicks and conversions which can create misaligned incentives around consumer transparency. Organizations using Clearlink's services should carefully review the consumer experience and disclosure practices.

Clearout.description: Clearout is a bulk email validation service designed to clean email lists and improve deliverability.
Clearout.history: Clearout is an email validation and verification service founded around 2017 in India. The platform provides real-time email verification, bulk email list cleaning, and email finder services. It helps businesses improve their email deliverability by identifying invalid, disposable, and risky email addresses before sending campaigns. The service offers an API for integration with marketing platforms and CRMs.
Clearout.summary_abuse: Clearout is an email verification platform with standard data handling practices for the industry. The service processes email addresses submitted by customers, which are sensitive personal data. There is some inherent risk in using email enrichment and verification services as they may cross-reference addresses against databases that were built from various sources. No significant abuse incidents have been documented for Clearout specifically. Users should ensure their use complies with applicable anti-spam laws.

ClearSale.description: ClearSale offers fraud management and chargeback protection services.
ClearSale.history: ClearSale was founded in 2001 in São Paulo, Brazil, as a fraud prevention and e-commerce fraud detection company. The company combines machine learning technology with human review to detect fraudulent transactions. ClearSale has grown to serve thousands of e-commerce merchants worldwide and went public on the Brazilian stock exchange. The company is known for its chargeback guarantee model where it covers the cost of approved transactions that later result in fraud.
ClearSale.summary_abuse: ClearSale is a fraud detection platform that by design processes large volumes of consumer purchase data and behavioral signals to identify suspicious transactions. The company's tracking capabilities are extensive, collecting device fingerprints, browsing behavior, and transaction patterns across its merchant network. While this data collection serves legitimate fraud prevention purposes, it creates a large aggregated dataset of consumer behavior. No major data breaches have been publicly reported, but the extensive data collection warrants scrutiny.

Clearstream.description: Cloudstream is a communication platform that provides texting and email services for congregational outreach and engagement.
Clearstream.history: Clearstream is an SMS and text messaging platform founded around 2014 and based in the United States. The service focuses on church and non-profit organizations, providing mass text messaging, two-way SMS communication, and subscriber management. Clearstream serves faith communities, charities, and civic organizations looking to communicate with members via text message. The platform integrates with church management systems and other CRMs.
Clearstream.summary_abuse: Clearstream is a text messaging platform focused on religious and non-profit organizations, with no notable abuse incidents documented. As an SMS platform, it collects mobile phone numbers and message content, which are sensitive personal data. The platform's focus on non-profit and religious communities means it handles member contact information that requires proper consent management. The company appears to maintain TCPA compliance for US messaging laws. No major security incidents have been reported.

Cleave.js.description: JavaScript library for formatting input text content when you are typing.
Cleave.js.history: Cleave.js is an open-source JavaScript library created by Max Huang (nosir) in 2016. It provides automatic input formatting for HTML input fields, including credit card numbers, phone numbers, dates, and custom patterns. The library became popular for improving form UX in web applications by automatically adding formatting characters as users type. It is distributed via npm and used in millions of websites globally.
Cleave.js.summary_abuse: Cleave.js is a pure client-side input formatting library with no network calls, no data collection, and no external dependencies beyond the browser. It operates entirely in the user's browser to format input fields. The library has no abuse potential as it does not transmit any data. The Apache-2.0 license and open-source nature allow full code inspection and verification.

Cleeng.description: Cleeng is a platform that enables the sale and protection of premium video content through access control and secure streaming features.
Cleeng.history: Cleeng was founded in 2011 in Amsterdam, Netherlands, as a subscription and pay-per-view monetization platform for digital media publishers. The company provides subscriber management, payment processing, and content gating capabilities for broadcasters, OTT video platforms, and content publishers. Cleeng serves media companies looking to monetize their digital content through subscriptions, rentals, and one-time purchases. The platform has grown to serve global media brands.
Cleeng.summary_abuse: Cleeng is a digital subscription management platform with no notable abuse history. As a payment processing intermediary, it handles subscriber payment data and requires PCI DSS compliance. The platform is used by media companies for legitimate subscription management. As a Dutch company, it is subject to GDPR. No significant security incidents or privacy violations have been reported. The platform's white-labeling capabilities mean end consumers may interact with Cleeng-powered services without knowing it.

Clerk.description: Clerk is a user management platform.
Clerk.history: Clerk was founded in 2021 by Colin Sidoti, Braden Sidoti, and others, and is headquartered in New York City. The company provides developer-focused user authentication and identity management as a service, offering pre-built UI components for sign-up, sign-in, profile management, and multi-factor authentication. Clerk raised $30 million in Series A funding in 2022 and positioned itself as an alternative to Auth0 with stronger developer experience and React component support. The platform is particularly popular in the Next.js and React ecosystem.
Clerk.summary_abuse: Clerk is a developer authentication service with no documented abuse incidents. As an identity provider, it handles authentication data including email addresses, passwords, OAuth tokens, and session data for applications using its service. Clerk has SOC 2 Type II certification and maintains a responsible disclosure policy. The company's focus on developer experience means its security documentation and practices are well-published. No significant privacy violations or security breaches have been reported.

Clerk.io.description: Clerk.io is an all-in-one ecommerce personalisation platform.
Clerk.io.history: Clerk.io was founded in 2012 in Copenhagen, Denmark, as an e-commerce personalization and product recommendation platform. The company provides AI-powered product recommendations, search, email campaigns, and audience segmentation for online retailers. Clerk.io integrates with major e-commerce platforms including Shopify, Magento, and WooCommerce. The company serves European and global e-commerce merchants looking to increase conversion rates through personalization.
Clerk.io.summary_abuse: Clerk.io is an e-commerce personalization platform that tracks visitor behavior across online stores to build recommendation models. This behavioral tracking is core to the product's functionality. As a Danish company, it is subject to GDPR and EU data protection laws. No significant security incidents or data misuse have been documented. The platform's tracking of shoppers across e-commerce sites is a privacy consideration that website owners should disclose to their customers.

Clever.description: Clever is an identity platform for education that helps schools and applications secure identities, manage data access, and support classroom learning.
Clever.history: Clever was founded in 2012 by Tyler Bosmeny and Dan Carroll in San Francisco as a single sign-on platform for K-12 education in the United States. The platform allows students and teachers to log into educational applications using their school credentials, eliminating the need to manage separate passwords for multiple apps. Clever has grown to connect over 95,000 schools and serves millions of students. The company raised significant venture capital including a Series C round and serves as critical infrastructure for US school districts.
Clever.summary_abuse: Clever handles authentication data for millions of K-12 students, including minors, which creates significant privacy responsibilities. The platform has faced scrutiny over the scope of student data it accesses and shares with third-party educational applications. FERPA compliance and student privacy laws apply to its operations. In 2021, Clever was scrutinized for how it shares student data with application developers. The company maintains COPPA and FERPA compliance documentation but the aggregation of student identity data across thousands of school districts creates a high-value target for data breaches.

Cleverbridge.description: Cleverbridge is a all-in-one ecommerce and subscription billing solution for software, (SaaS) and digital goods.
Cleverbridge.history: Cleverbridge was founded in 2005 in Cologne, Germany, as a global e-commerce platform for software and digital goods companies. The company acts as a merchant of record, handling international payment processing, subscription management, tax compliance, and fraud prevention for software publishers. Cleverbridge serves major software vendors looking to sell globally without managing payment infrastructure. The company expanded to Chicago and operates globally, processing transactions in over 100 currencies.
Cleverbridge.summary_abuse: Cleverbridge is an established German e-commerce platform for software with strong compliance credentials including PCI DSS, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 certifications. As a merchant of record, it takes on significant compliance and security responsibilities. The company processes payment data on behalf of software vendors globally. No significant data breaches or security incidents have been publicly documented. The merchant-of-record model means consumers may be billed by Cleverbridge without always recognizing the company's name.

CleverData.description: CleverData is a CDP platform that manages customer data and enables personalized user experiences through robust data processing capabilities.
CleverData.history: CleverData is a customer data platform founded around 2013 in Russia. The company provides a CDP solution for collecting, unifying, and activating customer data for marketing and analytics purposes. CleverData serves Russian-market businesses with tools for customer segmentation, behavioral analytics, and marketing automation. The platform processes user behavior data from websites and mobile apps.
CleverData.summary_abuse: CleverData is a Russian-based customer data platform that processes large volumes of consumer behavioral data. Russian technology companies operating with user data face additional scrutiny given Russian data sovereignty laws that may require data to be stored within Russia and potentially accessible to Russian authorities. Limited international compliance certifications and transparency reduce confidence in the platform's privacy practices for non-Russian users. The high-data-volume nature of CDPs means significant consumer privacy risk if data practices are inadequate.

CleverInsight.description: CleverInsight is a tool that helps businesses measure customer satisfaction and design loyalty programs to improve customer retention.
CleverInsight.history: CleverInsight is a Danish-developed customer satisfaction and loyalty measurement platform offering CSAT, NPS, and employee satisfaction tools. The company has over 10 years of experience in customer experience measurement and provides both software and consulting services. It remains a small independent company serving the Nordic and European market.
CleverInsight.summary_abuse: CleverInsight has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B customer satisfaction survey tool serving legitimate businesses. The platform sends satisfaction surveys to customers on behalf of client companies, with no anonymous signup or consumer-facing content hosting.

CleverReach.description: CleverReach is email marketing software featuring a web form designed for newsletter creation and distribution.
CleverReach.history: CleverReach was founded in 2000 in Rastede, Germany, as an email marketing platform. The company is one of Germany's established email marketing service providers, offering newsletter creation, campaign management, marketing automation, and analytics. CleverReach focuses heavily on GDPR compliance and data privacy, positioning itself as a German alternative to US-based email marketing platforms. The platform serves small and medium-sized businesses as well as agencies across Germany and Europe.
CleverReach.summary_abuse: CleverReach is a German email marketing platform with a strong focus on GDPR compliance. As a German company subject to strict EU data protection laws, it has robust privacy documentation and is ISO 27001 certified. The platform processes subscriber email lists and campaign data. No significant security incidents or privacy violations have been documented. CleverReach's German jurisdiction and privacy-first positioning make it a relatively trusted choice for European email marketing.

CleverTap.description: CleverTap is a SaaS based customer lifecycle management and mobile marketing company headquartered in Mountain View, California.
CleverTap.history: CleverTap was founded in 2013 by Sunil Thomas, Anand Jain, and Suresh Kondamudi in San Francisco, with engineering operations in Mumbai, India. The company provides a customer engagement and retention platform for mobile-first businesses, combining analytics, segmentation, push notifications, in-app messaging, and personalized campaigns. CleverTap raised over $100 million in venture capital and serves major brands across emerging markets including India, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East. The company went public on Indian stock exchanges in 2023.
CleverTap.summary_abuse: CleverTap is a heavy-tracking engagement platform that collects detailed mobile user behavior data including app events, user properties, and engagement metrics across millions of users. The company's data collection practices for mobile tracking are comprehensive. As a platform based in India and the US, it processes data under both GDPR and India's data protection laws. No major public data breaches have been reported. The platform's extensive behavioral tracking and user profiling create significant privacy implications for end users of apps that use CleverTap.

Clevy.description: Clevy is a platform that automates internal processes and queries within organizations, streamlining operations and improving efficiency.
Clevy.history: Clevy is a French conversational AI and chatbot platform founded around 2017. The company provides tools for building enterprise chatbots and virtual assistants for HR, IT helpdesk, and customer service use cases. Clevy focuses on the French-speaking enterprise market, offering an AI-powered FAQ and knowledge base automation platform. The company is based in Paris, France.
Clevy.summary_abuse: Clevy is a small French conversational AI company with no documented abuse incidents. The platform processes employee and customer conversations, which may contain sensitive personal and business information. As a French company, it is subject to GDPR and EU AI regulations. Limited external security validation is available given the company's small size. No significant security or privacy incidents have been publicly reported.

Click & Pledge.description: Click & Pledge is an all-in-one digital fundraising platform.
Click & Pledge.history: Click & Pledge was founded in 1999 and is based in the United States. The company provides online fundraising and payment processing solutions specifically for nonprofits and charitable organizations. The platform integrates with Salesforce and provides tools for donation processing, event registration, grant management, and peer-to-peer fundraising. Click & Pledge is one of the longer-standing nonprofit payment platforms in the market.
Click & Pledge.summary_abuse: Click & Pledge is a nonprofit-focused payment processing platform with no significant documented abuse concerns. As a payment processor, it is PCI DSS compliant. The platform serves charitable organizations and handles donation transactions. No major data breaches or security incidents have been publicly reported. The long operating history (since 1999) provides some confidence in operational stability.

Clickaine.description: Clickaine is a traffic ad network that helps advertisers drive targeted traffic to their websites through various digital marketing solutions, offering a platform for publishers to monetize their traffic effectively.
Clickaine.history: Clickaine was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Prague, Czech Republic. It operates as a global ad-tech platform serving both advertisers and publishers with self-serve capabilities. The network claims over 240 million daily users and thousands of premium publishing partners. No notable ownership changes or acquisition events are documented.
Clickaine.summary_abuse: Clickaine is an ad network operating in the performance advertising space, including push notifications and popunders, formats frequently associated with aggressive or misleading advertising. Ad networks of this type are regularly implicated in distributing low-quality or deceptive ads, including fake antivirus alerts, subscription traps, and adult content on non-adult sites. Clickaine states it monitors creatives for misleading ads, but enforcement rigor is unclear and such networks are commonly exploited by bad actors.

Clickbank.history: ClickBank was founded in 1998 by Tim and Eileen Barber in a garage in San Diego, California, becoming one of the earliest and largest affiliate marketing platforms for digital products. It has since grown to pay out over $7.3 billion in affiliate commissions and serves over six million clients worldwide. The company has remained independently owned and has never missed an affiliate payment since launch.
Clickbank.summary_abuse: ClickBank has a well-documented history of hosting products associated with spam, misleading health claims, get-rich-quick schemes, and aggressive upsell funnels. A significant share of affiliates promote low-quality or deceptive digital products including weight-loss supplements with dubious claims, financial trading schemes, and dubious online courses. The platform has faced recurring regulatory scrutiny and consumer complaints. While ClickBank has tightened product vetting over the years, abusive marketing practices by affiliates remain a persistent concern.

Clickbooq.description: Clickbooq is a website builder specially designed for photographers to showcase their work.
Clickbooq.history: Clickbooq launched in 2007 as one of the first dedicated portfolio website builders for photographers and creative professionals. The platform focuses on photographers, architects, and artists, offering mobile-friendly portfolio websites with customizable templates and SEO features. It has remained independently operated with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
Clickbooq.summary_abuse: Clickbooq is a niche portfolio website builder for creative professionals with no notable abuse signal. The platform does not offer mass-email, hosting of arbitrary content, or anonymous signups at scale. No documented incidents of abuse related to the platform exist.

Clickbrainiacs.description: Clickbrainiacs is a click fraud monitoring software for AdWords PPC campaigns that detects and helps prevent invalid or malicious ad clicks.
Clickbrainiacs.history: ClickBrainiacs is a Romanian digital marketing agency founded in 2017 by Sorin Vilcea and headquartered in Romania. The agency specializes in SEO, Google Ads, and Meta Ads services for businesses ranging from startups to large companies, managing 250+ clients across 40+ industries. It operates as a small independent agency and is a certified Google Partner.
Clickbrainiacs.summary_abuse: ClickBrainiacs is a small digital marketing services agency with no notable abuse signal. As a service agency rather than a platform allowing user-generated content, its abuse surface is minimal. No documented incidents of misuse associated with the company exist.

ClickBus.description: ClickBus is an online platform that enables users to search, compare, and book bus travel routes.
ClickBus.history: ClickBus was founded in 2012 in São Paulo, Brazil, as an online bus ticketing platform for Latin America. The company provides a marketplace for purchasing intercity bus tickets online, working with bus companies across Brazil and other Latin American countries. ClickBus raised venture capital funding and became one of the leading online bus ticketing platforms in Brazil. The company targets travelers seeking convenient online booking for intercity bus travel.
ClickBus.summary_abuse: ClickBus is an online bus ticketing marketplace for Latin America with standard e-commerce practices. As a travel ticketing platform, it processes passenger personal data including names, CPF (Brazilian tax ID), contact information, and payment data. No major security incidents have been reported. The company operates primarily in Brazil and is subject to Brazil's LGPD data protection law. PCI DSS compliance covers its payment processing.

ClickCease.description: ClickCease is an ad fraud and click-fraud detection and protection service software.
ClickCease.history: ClickCease was founded in 2015 in Tel Aviv, Israel, as a click fraud protection service for Google Ads and Facebook Ads campaigns. The platform identifies and blocks fraudulent clicks from competitors, bots, and click farms to protect advertisers' ad budgets. ClickCease monitors traffic patterns, IP addresses, and behavioral signals to detect click fraud. The company serves thousands of businesses and digital marketing agencies globally.
ClickCease.summary_abuse: ClickCease is a click fraud detection service that operates by intensively monitoring website visitor behavior, IP addresses, and device fingerprints across all sites using its service. This creates a cross-site tracking profile of visitors. While the stated purpose is to detect ad fraud, the surveillance-heavy approach raises privacy concerns. The company is Israeli and processes data from global advertisers' websites. No major security incidents have been reported, but the extensive visitor tracking warrants consideration in privacy-conscious deployments.

ClickChat.description: Click Chat is a web-based solution that enables chat installation on any website, facilitating real-time communication and customer engagement.
ClickChat.history: Click Chat is a Russian-market live chat SaaS for website customer engagement, accessible at click-chat.ru. It provides a chat widget installable on any website. Details on founding date and corporate history are not publicly documented.
ClickChat.summary_abuse: Click Chat is a small Russian live chat platform with no documented abuse incidents. Russian-hosted SaaS operates outside GDPR frameworks. Live chat tools can in theory be embedded on phishing pages to add credibility, but no specific abuse pattern is documented for this vendor.

ClickDesk.description: ClickDesk is a live chat and online engagement software that enables real-time interaction with website visitors.
ClickDesk.history: ClickDesk was founded around 2011 and is based in India. The company provides a combined live chat, helpdesk, and voice/video chat support platform for businesses. ClickDesk differentiates itself by offering audio and video chat capabilities alongside traditional text chat. It targets small and medium businesses looking for customer support software and integrates with popular helpdesk and CRM systems.
ClickDesk.summary_abuse: ClickDesk is a small live chat and support platform with no documented abuse incidents. As a chat platform, it handles customer service conversations which may contain personal information. The Indian-based company has limited compliance certifications but operates in a straightforward business model. No significant security incidents have been publicly reported.

ClickDimensions.description: ClickDimensions is a SaaS marketing automation platform built on the Microsoft Windows Azure platform.
ClickDimensions.history: ClickDimensions was founded in 2010 in Atlanta, Georgia, as a marketing automation platform built natively inside Microsoft Dynamics CRM. The company provides email marketing, web intelligence, campaign automation, surveys, and social media tools that integrate tightly with Microsoft Dynamics 365. ClickDimensions became a leading marketing automation solution for Microsoft Dynamics users and was acquired by investment firm Expedition Capital Partners. The company serves thousands of Dynamics CRM customers globally.
ClickDimensions.summary_abuse: ClickDimensions is a marketing automation platform integrated with Microsoft Dynamics CRM. The platform processes marketing data and tracks website visitor behavior for campaign analytics. As a platform operating within Microsoft's CRM ecosystem, it benefits from Microsoft's compliance framework. No significant security incidents or privacy violations have been publicly documented. SOC 2 certification provides some assurance of security practices.

ClickFreeze.description: ClickFreeze is a tool that detects and blocks click fraud in Google Ads campaigns to improve performance and reduce wasted ad spend.
ClickFreeze.history: ClickFreeze is a click fraud detection and prevention SaaS for Google Ads campaigns, launched around 2020. It monitors advertising traffic and automatically blocks fraudulent clicks to reduce wasted ad spend. The company is small and independently operated with limited public corporate history.
ClickFreeze.summary_abuse: ClickFreeze is a click-fraud protection tool for advertisers and has no notable abuse signal itself. The platform analyzes inbound ad traffic for fraud patterns and does not host content or provide user-generated features that would attract fraudsters.

ClickFunnels.description: ClickFunnels is an online sales funnel builder that helps businesses market, sell, and deliver their products online.
ClickFunnels.history: ClickFunnels was founded in 2014 by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson in Boise, Idaho. The company provides a sales funnel builder and marketing automation platform primarily targeting online entrepreneurs, coaches, and information product sellers. ClickFunnels grew rapidly through Brunson's aggressive internet marketing community building and became one of the most recognized names in the online sales funnel software space. The company bootstrapped to over $100 million in revenue without venture capital.
ClickFunnels.summary_abuse: ClickFunnels has significant concerns around dark patterns and aggressive marketing practices. The platform is widely used by internet marketers to create high-pressure sales funnels with countdown timers, false scarcity, aggressive upsells, and other persuasion techniques that regulatory bodies have flagged as dark patterns. The FTC has taken action against various ClickFunnels users for misleading marketing claims. The platform itself processes heavy tracking data and is associated with the MLM-adjacent internet marketing community. While not technically abusive, the marketing ecosystem around ClickFunnels poses consumer protection risks.

ClickGUARD.description: ClickGUARD is anti-click fraud prevention software designed to detect and block invalid or fraudulent clicks in online advertising campaigns.
ClickGUARD.history: ClickGUARD was founded around 2017 as a Google Ads click fraud prevention platform. The service monitors Google Ads traffic to detect and block fraudulent clicks from bots, competitors, and click farms. ClickGUARD works directly with the Google Ads API to automatically block invalid traffic sources. The platform is positioned as a specialized click fraud tool for businesses investing heavily in Google Ads campaigns.
ClickGUARD.summary_abuse: ClickGUARD is a click fraud prevention service that operates by monitoring all visitor traffic to websites running Google Ads campaigns. Like other click fraud tools, it collects detailed visitor data including IP addresses, user agents, and behavioral signals across all its client sites to build fraud detection models. While the product serves a legitimate purpose, the cross-site data collection raises privacy considerations. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

ClickHeat.history: ClickHeat is an open-source website heatmap tool created by Stephane Rios of Dugout Labs, France, around 2006. It was one of the early free heatmap tools available to webmasters, allowing self-hosting of click tracking data. The project was distributed via SourceForge and was popular in the early 2010s before commercial alternatives like Hotjar emerged. Development appears to have ceased, with the project now considered abandoned.
ClickHeat.summary_abuse: ClickHeat is an abandoned open-source heatmap project with no commercial entity behind it. As a self-hosted tool, all data collected stays on the deploying organization's own servers, which is a privacy advantage over SaaS alternatives. The abandonment of the project means it no longer receives security updates, which poses a risk for installations running outdated versions. GPL-2.0 license allows full code inspection. No commercial abuse is possible given the project's non-commercial nature.

ClickHelp.description: ClickHelp is an AI-powered all-in-one documentation tool designed to create, manage, and publish technical content.
ClickHelp.history: ClickHelp was founded around 2011 and is based in Ukraine. The company provides a cloud-based technical documentation portal for software companies, allowing technical writers to create, manage, and publish online help documentation, user manuals, knowledge bases, and API documentation. ClickHelp competes with tools like MadCap Flare and Confluence in the technical writing market. Despite being based in Ukraine, the company has maintained operations through geopolitical challenges.
ClickHelp.summary_abuse: ClickHelp is a technical documentation platform with no documented abuse concerns. The platform hosts technical documentation content for software companies and manages user access to documentation portals. No significant security incidents or privacy violations have been reported. As a Ukrainian company subject to EU-influenced GDPR compliance practices, it maintains standard data protection policies. The platform is used for legitimate documentation publishing and poses minimal abuse risk.

clickio.description: Clickio Consent Tool collects and communicates consent both to IAB Framework vendors and to Google Ads products.
clickio.history: Clickio was founded in 2015 in London, UK. The company is a Google Certified Publishing Partner that provides a Consent Management Platform (CMP) for publishers, handling GDPR/IAB Framework consent and Google Ads product consent signals. The platform also offers ad optimization and site performance tools for publishers. Clickio serves publishers needing compliant consent collection under GDPR and the IAB TCF standards.
clickio.summary_abuse: Clickio is a publisher consent management and ad technology platform with a low direct abuse signal. The tool is embedded in publisher websites and collects user consent for advertising. As an ad-tech adjacent tool it carries some privacy risk from tracking vendors in the consent framework, but no documented fraud, phishing, or scam incidents are associated with Clickio itself.

ClickOnce.description: ClickOnce is a Microsoft .NET deployment technology that enables the creation of self-updating Windows-based applications that can be installed and run with minimal user interaction.
ClickOnce.history: ClickOnce is a Microsoft deployment technology introduced in 2005 as part of .NET Framework 2.0 and Visual Studio 2005. It enables Windows desktop applications to be deployed and auto-updated with minimal user interaction and without requiring admin rights. It evolved through .NET Framework versions up to 4.8, and was later adapted for .NET 5+ (Windows-only). No ownership changes; remains part of Microsoft's developer toolchain.
ClickOnce.summary_abuse: ClickOnce has minimal abuse signal as a deployment technology primarily used for enterprise and internal applications. Historically, attackers have occasionally abused ClickOnce to deliver malware by tricking users into running malicious .application files, but this is not a dominant use case. Microsoft has responded with security improvements over time including code signing requirements and sandbox restrictions.

ClickReport.description: ClickReport is a platform that provides PPC click fraud prevention by detecting and blocking invalid or malicious ad traffic.
ClickReport.history: ClickReport is a PPC click fraud detection and prevention SaaS tool focused on protecting Google, Yahoo, and Bing ad campaigns. It provides daily click reports, a four-tiered warning system, and real-time alerts to help advertisers identify and block fraudulent clicks. The company operates as a small independent SaaS with no notable acquisition or ownership events.
ClickReport.summary_abuse: ClickReport itself is a tool for detecting ad fraud rather than a source of abuse. Its primary function is defensive — monitoring PPC campaigns for click fraud. No notable abuse signal exists for the platform itself. The tool's data access to ad account information could theoretically be misused if credentials were compromised, but no documented incidents exist.

Clicksign.description: Clicksign is a platform offering document signing services.
Clicksign.history: Clicksign was founded in Brazil around 2013 by Marcelo, Michael, and Daniel with the objective of simplifying electronic document signing. It became the first and only Brazilian electronic signature company certified with both ISO 27001 and ISO 27701. The platform has processed over 10 million documents and serves a primarily Brazilian and Latin American market.
Clicksign.summary_abuse: Clicksign is a legal electronic signature platform operating under Brazilian law, with ISO 27001 certification and AES-256 encryption. Its use case is strictly document signing for businesses, limiting abuse potential. No documented incidents of misuse for fraud or phishing exist. The platform requires user authentication and maintains signed document audit trails.

Clickskeks.description: Clickskeks is an all-in-one cookie consent management platform for businesses.
Clickskeks.history: Clickskeks is an Austrian cookie consent management platform. The name is German slang for 'cookie' (Keks). No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
Clickskeks.summary_abuse: Clickskeks is a cookie consent SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It helps businesses achieve GDPR compliance and has no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

ClickTale.description: ClickTale is a SaaS solution enabling organisations to gain visual in-page analytics.
ClickTale.history: ClickTale was founded in 2006 in Israel as a pioneer in website heatmap and session-recording analytics. It gained traction as one of the first tools to record mouse movements and clicks at scale. In July 2019, Contentsquare acquired ClickTale, creating a combined entity serving over 600 enterprise clients including 30% of the Fortune Global 100. The ClickTale brand has since been deprecated as Contentsquare absorbed the product and technology.
ClickTale.summary_abuse: ClickTale records detailed user behavior including mouse movements, clicks, and session replays, which raises significant privacy concerns, particularly around inadvertent capture of sensitive form data. The platform is widely used by enterprises and its data collection practices have drawn scrutiny from privacy advocates. Contentsquare has implemented masking capabilities and GDPR tooling to address these concerns, but the nature of session recording inherently collects granular behavioral data.

Clicktools.description: Clicktools is a feedback system developed by CallidusCloud for collecting and analyzing customer input across various channels.
Clicktools.history: Clicktools was a UK-based enterprise survey and CRM integration SaaS. In January 2011, SurveyMonkey acquired a 49.9% stake in the company. In September 2014, Callidus Software (CallidusCloud) acquired Clicktools outright to expand its Lead to Money suite. When SAP acquired CallidusCloud in 2018, Clicktools became part of SAP's portfolio and is now absorbed into SAP SuccessFactors, with the original brand largely deprecated.
Clicktools.summary_abuse: Clicktools is an enterprise survey platform now part of SAP's portfolio with no notable abuse signal. Its use is restricted to enterprise CRM integrations and customer feedback collection. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

Clicktripz.description: Clicktripz is a platform that provides innovative software solutions to serve the needs of suppliers, publishers, advertisers and travelers.
Clicktripz.history: ClickTripz was founded in 2010 by Ross Weber, Eric Bjorndahl, and David Bjorndahl as a travel advertising and technology platform. It built a network serving clients including Tripadvisor, Expedia, and Booking.com across 31 languages. In 2025, the company rebranded to Tiki, signaling a shift toward AI-powered destination marketing.
Clicktripz.summary_abuse: ClickTripz is a travel-industry advertising platform with limited abuse signal. It operates B2B and serves large travel brands, reducing direct consumer abuse risk. Travel advertising networks can occasionally serve misleading fare ads or redirect users through affiliate chains, but no specific documented incidents of significant abuse are associated with ClickTripz.

ClickUp.description: ClickUp is a project management software suitable for businesses of all sizes and industries.
ClickUp.history: ClickUp was founded in 2017 by Zeb Evans and Alex Yurkowski as an internal tool for their marketing agency, then launched publicly in 2018. It raised $35M Series A in 2020, $100M Series B in late 2020, and a $400M Series C at a $4B valuation in 2021 led by Andreessen Horowitz and Tiger Global. Total funding reached $537.5M. ClickUp has grown into one of the most feature-rich project management platforms on the market.
ClickUp.summary_abuse: ClickUp is a mainstream project management SaaS with minimal direct abuse signal. Legitimate enterprise and SMB use overwhelmingly dominates. Occasional phishing pages have been hosted using ClickUp docs, leveraging the trusted domain, but ClickUp actively enforces its AUP and takes down abusive content. The platform does not present unusual risk relative to other productivity SaaS tools.

Clickx.description: Clickx is a marketing analytics platform that lets users generate reports and gain insights into marketing campaigns, product pages, ROI, and more.
Clickx.history: Clickx is a US-based digital marketing platform and white-label agency fulfillment service targeting small businesses and marketing agencies. It offers SEO, PPC, social media, and AI visibility services with a focus on white-label reselling for agencies. The company has remained independently operated with no documented acquisitions.
Clickx.summary_abuse: Clickx is a small digital marketing services and white-label agency platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a B2B service provider for small businesses and agencies. No documented incidents of fraud, phishing, or platform abuse exist.

Clicky.description: Clicky is web an analytics tool which helps you to get real-time analysis including spy view.
