Clicky.history: Clicky is a real-time web analytics service developed by Roxr Software, Ltd., founded in 2006 and based in Portland, Oregon. It was one of the earliest real-time web analytics tools and built a loyal following among privacy-conscious website owners as an alternative to Google Analytics. The company has remained independently owned and operated with no acquisitions.
Clicky.summary_abuse: Clicky is a web analytics service with minimal abuse signal. Its tracking script is embedded on websites to measure visitor behavior, raising standard web analytics privacy concerns, but the platform does not sell PII or provide ad targeting data. No documented incidents of significant misuse or abuse are associated with Clicky.

Cliengo.description: Cliengo is a platform that automates website conversations using Artificial Intelligence to enhance customer engagement and drive sales.
Cliengo.history: Cliengo is an Argentine conversational marketing SaaS founded in 2017 by Andres Snitcofsky, headquartered in Buenos Aires. The platform offers chatbots and CRM tools for customer service and sales automation across web, WhatsApp, and Instagram. The company bootstrapped to $7M ARR by 2024 without external funding.
Cliengo.summary_abuse: Cliengo is a chatbot and CRM platform primarily used by Latin American SMBs with no notable abuse signal. The platform provides standard customer communication automation and does not facilitate anonymous content hosting or mass unsolicited outreach. No documented incidents of platform abuse exist.

Client Chat Live.description: Client Chat Live is a tool that converts website visitors into clients through live chat functionality.
Client Chat Live.history: Client Chat Live was founded in 2008 and specializes in live chat software and 24/7 operator services exclusively for law firms and legal businesses. The company has worked with over 500 law firms since its founding. It remains a small independently operated SaaS focused entirely on the legal vertical.
Client Chat Live.summary_abuse: Client Chat Live is a niche live chat and managed service platform for law firms with no notable abuse signal. The platform handles inbound inquiries for legal clients and is not a public-facing tool that can be exploited for spam, fraud, or phishing at scale. No documented incidents of platform misuse exist.

Client Diary.description: Client Diary is a scheduling and booking system used to manage appointments, track availability, and organize client interactions in one platform.
Client Diary.history: Client Diary is an Australian salon and beauty business management software providing appointment booking, client records, and business management tools. It is a small independent SaaS focused on the Australian market, particularly praised for responsive customer support and affordability. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Client Diary.summary_abuse: Client Diary is a niche salon management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. The platform manages appointment bookings and client records for beauty businesses and does not facilitate public content hosting or anonymous signups. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

Clientacquisition.description: Clientacquisition is a provider of tailored infrastructure solutions that drive growth.
Clientacquisition.history: Clientacquisition.io is a US-based business coaching and client acquisition consulting platform focused on helping online service businesses scale to high-ticket revenue levels. It offers programs including a 90-day Growth Creator Incubator. The company operates as a small independent venture with no notable acquisitions or external funding.
Clientacquisition.summary_abuse: Clientacquisition.io is a business coaching platform with no notable technical abuse signal. The platform's focus on high-ticket sales coaching and lead generation consulting can raise concerns about aggressive marketing tactics common in the coaching industry, but no specific documented incidents of fraud or platform abuse exist.

Clientify.description: Clientify is a marketing and sales automation platform designed to help businesses grow.
Clientify.history: Clientify is a Spanish CRM and marketing automation platform founded in 2014 and based in Almeria, Spain. It targets Spanish-speaking SMBs and agencies in Spain and Latin America, offering integrated CRM, email marketing, WhatsApp automation, and landing pages. The platform differentiates itself by deep WhatsApp Business integration and bilingual support for the Hispanic market.
Clientify.summary_abuse: Clientify is a CRM and marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used by Spanish-speaking SMBs for legitimate sales and marketing workflows. Its WhatsApp integration follows Meta API guidelines. No documented incidents of platform misuse exist.

ClientJS.description: ClientJS is a JavaScript library for generating browser fingerprints, exposing all the browser data-points.
ClientJS.history: ClientJS is an open-source JavaScript library for device fingerprinting and browser data collection, published on GitHub by jackspirou under the Apache 2.0 license. The library exposes browser and device data points used to generate 32-bit integer fingerprint hashes without requiring any external dependencies. Development activity has been minimal for several years, making it effectively dormant.
ClientJS.summary_abuse: ClientJS is a browser fingerprinting library that can be legitimately used for fraud detection and session management, but fingerprinting technology is also frequently employed to track users across sites without consent, circumvent cookie-based opt-outs, and build persistent user profiles. Its use in web analytics and ad-tech contexts raises significant privacy concerns. No malicious hosting of the library itself is documented, but its capabilities are dual-use.

ClientXCMS.description: ClientXCMS is a content management system that provides a drag-and-drop interface, customisable templates, user and media management, and website analytics to help businesses manage their website content.
ClientXCMS.history: ClientXCMS is a French open-source CMS and billing automation platform for web hosting companies, developed primarily for the French-speaking market. It is hosted on GitHub and provides modules for billing, client management, and service automation. The project is maintained by a small team and competes with established tools like WHMCS and FOSSBilling.
ClientXCMS.summary_abuse: ClientXCMS is a hosting billing and client management platform with no notable abuse signal. It is self-hosted software used by hosting providers to manage billing and client accounts. No documented incidents of platform misuse exist.

CLIKdata.description: CLIKdata is a provider of digital marketing and data services that support business growth by enhancing engagement and lead generation.
CLIKdata.history: CLIKdata is a Florida-based digital marketing and data services company led by founder and CEO Sean Marra. It claims over 30 years of marketing expertise and provides AI-driven marketing automation tools. No notable ownership changes are documented.
CLIKdata.summary_abuse: CLIKdata is a digital marketing and data services platform operating in the lead generation and digital advertising space. Marketing automation tools in this category can be misused for spam or unsolicited outreach, but no specific documented abuse incidents are on record for CLIKdata.

Clinch.description: Clinch delivers hyper-personalized creative experiences and consumer intelligence across all channels.
Clinch.history: Clinch was founded in 2013 by Oz Etzioni and is headquartered in New York. It is an AI-powered omnichannel personalization and dynamic creative optimization platform serving brands in retail, e-commerce, automotive, and travel. The company has raised venture funding and serves enterprise advertising clients with programmatic and social media ad personalization.
Clinch.summary_abuse: Clinch is an enterprise ad personalization platform used by major brands for programmatic advertising. Ad-tech platforms of this type collect significant behavioral data for targeting purposes, which raises privacy concerns but is typical for the sector. No specific documented incidents of platform abuse or misuse exist.

Clinic Sites.description: Clinic Sites is a content management system that enables health clinic owners to manage clinic information and patient records.
dom: a[href*='clinicsites.co'] > img[alt*='Site Powered By Clinic Sites']
Clinic Sites.history: Clinic Sites is a healthcare website builder closely associated with Jane App, a practice management platform. It allows clinics to create professional websites that sync with their Jane practice management accounts, turning website visitors into booked appointments. The platform is aimed at allied health clinics primarily in Canada.
Clinic Sites.summary_abuse: Clinic Sites is a niche healthcare website builder for clinics with no notable abuse signal. The platform is used for professional medical and allied health clinic websites and does not facilitate anonymous content hosting or user-generated content. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

ClinicSense.description: ClinicSense is a practice management software solution that reduces administrative costs and workload in healthcare clinics.
ClinicSense.history: ClinicSense is a Canadian clinic management SaaS focused on massage therapists and allied health professionals, offering SOAP notes, online booking, invoicing, and appointment reminders. The platform serves over 8,500 massage therapists and is HIPAA and PIPEDA compliant. It integrates with Square for in-person payments. The company operates independently with no documented acquisitions.
ClinicSense.summary_abuse: ClinicSense is a healthcare practice management platform serving massage therapists with HIPAA compliance and strong data handling practices. The platform handles sensitive personal health information but does so within a regulated healthcare context. No documented incidents of abuse or misuse exist.

Clinked.description: Clinked is a cloud-based platform for secure file sharing, project management, and team collaboration with client portals.
Clinked.history: Clinked is a UK-based white-label client portal and collaboration platform founded around 2012. It serves over 3,000 businesses in 40 countries with secure document sharing, task management, and client communication tools. The platform holds ISO 27001 certification and compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, PCI, and FIPS standards. The company remains independently operated.
Clinked.summary_abuse: Clinked is a secure B2B client portal platform with ISO 27001 certification and strong security posture. Its use case is restricted to business document sharing and collaboration, limiting public abuse surface. No documented incidents of misuse or fraud associated with the platform exist.

Clio.description: Clio is a legal software platform for managing clients, cases, and billing within law practices.
Clio.history: Clio was founded in 2007 by Jack Newton and Rian Gauvreau in British Columbia, Canada, as the first cloud-based legal practice management software. It raised $250M Series D in 2019, $110M Series E at $1.6B valuation in 2021, $900M Series F in 2023, and reached a $5B valuation in 2025. Clio has become the dominant player in cloud legal software, serving law firms globally.
Clio.summary_abuse: Clio is the dominant cloud legal practice management platform used by thousands of law firms globally. It handles sensitive attorney-client privileged information. The platform enforces strict access controls and holds SOC 2 Type 2 attestation. No notable abuse signal or documented misuse incidents exist; Clio's use case is inherently regulated by legal professional obligations.

Clipara.description: Clipara is a platform that helps businesses connect with customers using video by adding video content to websites, emails, and SMS without coding.
Clipara.history: Clipara was launched in November 2022 as a shoppable video widget platform for Shopify e-commerce stores. It enables merchants to embed Instagram Reels, TikTok videos, and YouTube clips directly on their product pages and homepages to boost conversion. The company remains a small independent SaaS startup with no documented acquisitions.
Clipara.summary_abuse: Clipara is a video widget SaaS for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal. The platform embeds shoppable videos on merchant websites and operates as a B2B tool. It does not facilitate user-generated public content hosting or anonymous signups. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

Clipboard.js.history: Clipboard.js was created by Zeno Rocha around 2014 as a modern alternative to Flash-based clipboard copy tools. It uses the Selection and execCommand browser APIs to copy text without any dependencies, weighing only ~3KB gzipped. The library gained widespread adoption and is one of the most-starred JavaScript utility libraries on GitHub.
Clipboard.js.summary_abuse: Clipboard.js is a frontend utility library for copying text to the clipboard with no notable abuse signal. As a foundational browser utility with no network calls or data collection, it presents no meaningful abuse surface. Legitimate use completely dominates.

Clock PMS.description: Clock PMS is a hotel booking software designed to streamline reservations, manage guest information, and optimise hotel operations.
Clock PMS.history: Clock Software was incorporated in 1994 in Bulgaria, launching its first Windows-based property management system ClockFront in 1996. The company transitioned to cloud-based hotel management software and now operates as Clock PMS+, serving over 1,500 hotels in 65 countries ranging from boutique properties to groups with 50+ properties.
Clock PMS.summary_abuse: Clock PMS is a hotel property management system used exclusively by hospitality businesses. It manages reservations, billing, and guest data within a controlled business context. No notable abuse signal or documented misuse incidents exist.

Clockwork.description: Clockwork is a development tool for PHP available right in your browser.
Clockwork.history: Clockwork SMS is an SMS API product originally developed by Mediaburst, a UK company founded in 2000 in Manchester. It was previously known as the Mediaburst SMS API before being rebranded as Clockwork. In 2019, Commify acquired Mediaburst from Stericycle Inc. In April 2021, Clockwork SMS and its parent Mediaburst transitioned into the TextAnywhere brand under Commify.
Clockwork.summary_abuse: Clockwork SMS is a business SMS API that, like all SMS messaging platforms, can be abused for spam, phishing, and smishing campaigns. SMS APIs have historically been exploited by fraudsters for one-time password interception, scam delivery, and unsolicited marketing. TextAnywhere and Clockwork enforce AUP policies, but SMS abuse is an industry-wide concern for API-based messaging platforms.

Clonando.description: Clonando is a CMS for creating and cloning landing pages without requiring coding knowledge.
Clonando.history: Clonando is a no-code landing page cloning and CMS tool. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Clonando.summary_abuse: Clonando is a landing page cloning tool explicitly designed to copy existing pages without coding. This capability is a known abuse vector as it can facilitate rapid duplication of legitimate brand pages for phishing campaigns. No specific documented incidents are on record but the tool's core feature set carries inherent phishing risk.

Clorder.description: Clorder is a digital platform for ordering and marketing designed to reduce costs and enhance sales for businesses.
Clorder.history: Clorder is a US-based online ordering system for restaurants, enabling food businesses to take orders through their own website and mobile app, avoiding commission fees from third-party delivery apps. The platform also provides marketing services and a delivery network. It remains a small independently operated SaaS with no documented acquisitions.
Clorder.summary_abuse: Clorder is a restaurant online ordering SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves food businesses as a B2B tool for order management. No documented incidents of misuse or fraud associated with the platform exist.

Closing Boost.description: Closing Boost is a sales pipeline management and marketing automation platform designed to capture leads and nurture prospects.
Closing Boost.history: Closing Boost is a sales pipeline and marketing automation SaaS for lead capture and nurturing. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Closing Boost.summary_abuse: Closing Boost is a small CRM and marketing automation tool with no documented abuse incidents. Marketing automation tools can be misused for spam campaigns but no specific reports exist for this vendor.

Closure Library.description: Closure Library is a JavaScript library developed by Google for building robust web applications, offering utilities for DOM manipulation, event handling, data structures, and more.
Closure Library.history: Google Closure Library is a comprehensive JavaScript library originally developed by Google for internal use in products like Gmail, Google Docs, and Google Maps. It was open-sourced in 2009 under the Apache 2.0 license and provided cross-browser DOM, Ajax, and UI utilities optimized for use with the Closure Compiler. As TypeScript and modern JavaScript tooling matured, Closure Library became less relevant. Google officially sunset the project on August 1, 2024, citing that it no longer meets the needs of modern JavaScript development.
Closure Library.summary_abuse: Google Closure Library is a foundational JavaScript library with no abuse signal. As a backend browser utility library with no network calls or data collection of its own, it presents no meaningful abuse surface. The library has been deprecated by Google as of 2024.

Cloud Guard.description: CloudGuard is a cybersecurity solutions provider focused on cloud security.
Cloud Guard.history: Cloud Guard is the cloud security product line of Check Point Software Technologies, an Israeli cybersecurity company founded in 1993. Check Point developed CloudGuard to provide unified cloud-native security across multi-cloud environments including AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Oracle also independently uses the Cloud Guard name for its Oracle Cloud Infrastructure security posture management service. The Check Point CloudGuard product line has evolved from network security to full cloud workload protection and CSPM.
Cloud Guard.summary_abuse: Cloud Guard (Check Point) is a cloud security platform used to detect and prevent threats in enterprise cloud environments. Security tools of this type are used exclusively for defensive purposes by legitimate organizations. No abuse signal exists for the product itself.

Cloudbeds.description: Cloudbeds is a cloud-based hotel management platform which includes tools for managing reservations, availability, rates, distribution channels, payments, guests, housekeeping, and more.
Cloudbeds.history: Cloudbeds was founded in 2012 by Adam Harris and Richard Castle in San Diego after Castle was frustrated booking accommodation while traveling in Brazil. The company acquired Myallocator in June 2014, raised $82M Series C in 2020, and reached $253M in total funding by 2021 backed by SoftBank Vision Fund and Viking Global Investors. It serves hotels in 115+ countries.
Cloudbeds.summary_abuse: Cloudbeds is a hotel property management system used by hospitality businesses to manage reservations and operations. It handles guest personal and payment data within a regulated hospitality context. No notable abuse signal or documented misuse incidents exist.

CloudCannon.description: CloudCannon is a web development platform and content management system (CMS).
CloudCannon.history: CloudCannon was founded in 2013 by George Phillips and Mike Neumegen in New Zealand with the goal of making static site editing accessible to non-technical users. The platform became a leading visual CMS for static site generators including Jekyll, Hugo, Eleventy, and SvelteKit, and is recognized in the Jamstack ecosystem. The company remains independently owned.
CloudCannon.summary_abuse: CloudCannon is a CMS and hosting platform for static sites with no notable abuse signal. Its use case targets professional development teams and content editors. The static site architecture limits the attack surface compared to dynamic CMS platforms. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

CloudCart.history: CloudCart was founded in 2016 by brothers Nikolay and Petar Ilievi in Sofia, Bulgaria. It is an eCommerce-as-a-service platform serving over 1,500 businesses primarily in Bulgaria and Central and Eastern Europe. In 2023, it expanded to Romania with investment from Elevator Ventures and NV3. By 2022, merchants on the platform had recorded cumulative sales of EUR 300 million.
CloudCart.summary_abuse: CloudCart is a legitimate e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves SMBs in Bulgaria and the CEE region for building online stores. No documented incidents of the platform being used for fraudulent storefronts or misuse exist.

CloudEngage.description: CloudEngage is a platform enabling no-code personalization for enhanced revenue and customer satisfaction.
CloudEngage.history: CloudEngage was founded in 2013 by Paul Wagner in Portland, Oregon, originally as ForkFly before rebranding. The platform provides geo-responsive website personalization, allowing marketing teams to create dynamic content based on visitor location and behavioral triggers. It later added Chord Chat (live chat) and AnswerDash (AI-powered Q&A) modules.
CloudEngage.summary_abuse: CloudEngage is a website personalization platform that collects visitor location and behavioral data to deliver personalized content. This raises privacy concerns typical for behavioral personalization tools, but no specific documented incidents of data misuse or platform abuse exist.

Cloudera.description: Cloudera is a software platform for data engineering, data warehousing, machine learning and analytics that runs in the cloud or on-premises.
Cloudera.history: Cloudera was founded on June 27, 2008 in Burlingame, California by engineers from Google, Yahoo, and Facebook: Christophe Bisciglia, Amr Awadallah, Jeff Hammerbacher, and Mike Olson. Hadoop co-founder Doug Cutting joined in 2009. Cloudera went public on the NYSE in 2017, then merged with Hortonworks in January 2019 in a $5.2B deal. In October 2021, KKR and Clayton, Dubilier & Rice took Cloudera private in a $5.3B acquisition. In 2024, Cloudera acquired Verta for AI/ML model management.
Cloudera.summary_abuse: Cloudera is an enterprise big data and analytics platform used exclusively by large organizations for data processing and machine learning. It has no consumer-facing surface or anonymous access. No abuse signal exists for this product.

Cloudflare.description: Cloudflare is a web-infrastructure and website-security company, providing content-delivery-network services, DDoS mitigation, Internet security, and distributed domain-name-server services.
Cloudflare.history: Cloudflare was founded in 2009 by Matthew Prince, Lee Holloway, and Michelle Zatlyn as a web security and performance company. It launched its CDN, DDoS protection, and DNS services commercially. Cloudflare went public on NYSE in September 2019 (NET). The company has grown into a critical internet infrastructure provider powering millions of websites and offering services from DNS to Zero Trust networking, edge computing, and AI gateways.
Cloudflare.summary_abuse: Cloudflare is critical internet infrastructure used by millions of legitimate websites and enterprises. However, Cloudflare's free tier and abuse-resistant features have also been exploited by threat actors to protect phishing sites, malware distribution infrastructure, and DDoS-for-hire services from takedown. Cloudflare publishes detailed abuse policies and has improved its AUP enforcement over the years, but remains a popular choice for bad actors seeking both performance and resilience from reporting. Legitimate use overwhelmingly dominates but the dual-use nature of CDN/proxy services is well-documented.

Cloudflare Bot Management.description: Cloudflare bot management solution identifies and mitigates automated traffic to protect websites from bad bots.
Cloudflare Bot Management.history: Cloudflare Bot Management is a product within Cloudflare's enterprise security suite, launched to help organizations detect and block automated bot traffic. It uses machine learning and behavioral analysis to distinguish legitimate users from bots. As a product of Cloudflare (founded 2009, public 2019), it benefits from Cloudflare's global network and threat intelligence.
Cloudflare Bot Management.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Bot Management is a security product designed to block malicious bot traffic. It is used defensively by enterprises and has no consumer-facing surface or user-generated content. No abuse signal exists for this tool.

Cloudflare Browser Insights.description: Cloudflare Browser Insights is a tool that measures the performance of websites from the perspective of users.
Cloudflare Browser Insights.history: Cloudflare Browser Insights is Cloudflare's real-user monitoring (RUM) and web analytics product, launched as a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. It measures Core Web Vitals and page load performance without using cookies or fingerprinting. The feature is part of Cloudflare's broader analytics suite available to zone customers.
Cloudflare Browser Insights.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Browser Insights is a privacy-focused web performance analytics tool embedded by Cloudflare on websites using its CDN. It explicitly avoids tracking individuals or selling data, collecting only aggregated performance metrics. No abuse signal exists for this product.

Cloudflare Rocket Loader.description: Cloudflare Rocket Loader is responsible for prioritising over website's content by delaying the loading of Javascript until rendering.
Cloudflare Rocket Loader.history: Cloudflare Rocket Loader is a JavaScript optimization feature within Cloudflare's CDN, designed to defer non-critical JavaScript loading to improve page performance. It works by asynchronously loading JavaScript files and has been part of Cloudflare's speed optimization suite since approximately 2012. It is available on all Cloudflare plans including the free tier.
Cloudflare Rocket Loader.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Rocket Loader is a page performance optimization feature with no abuse signal. It is a passive JavaScript loader that defers scripts and has no data collection or tracking capabilities of its own. No misuse documentation exists.

Cloudflare Stream.description: Cloudflare Stream is a serverless live and on-demand video streaming platform.
Cloudflare Stream.history: Cloudflare Stream is a video streaming product launched by Cloudflare in 2018, enabling developers to upload, store, encode, and deliver video without managing encoding infrastructure. It is usage-based priced per minute of stored video and per minute of delivered video. The product targets developers and businesses needing scalable video delivery.
Cloudflare Stream.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Stream is a developer video streaming API with low abuse signal relative to consumer video platforms. It does not support anonymous public uploads or consumer-facing UGC. Cloudflare's content policies and AUP apply. No specific documented incidents of significant abuse exist for this product.

Cloudflare Turnstile.description: Turnstile is Cloudflare's smart CAPTCHA alternative.
Cloudflare Turnstile.history: Cloudflare Turnstile was launched in September 2022 as a privacy-preserving CAPTCHA alternative. It uses browser and device signals to verify users are human without visual puzzles or tracking, explicitly designed not to profile users. It is offered as a free replacement for Google reCAPTCHA and hCaptcha, and is available to any Cloudflare customer including free-tier users.
Cloudflare Turnstile.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Turnstile is a privacy-first CAPTCHA alternative with no abuse signal. Its purpose is to prevent automated bot access. It does not track users across sites or collect PII. No documented misuse exists for this product.

Cloudflare Workers.description: Cloudflare Workers is a serverless execution environment that allows you to create entirely new applications or augment existing ones without configuring or maintaining infrastructure.
Cloudflare Workers.history: Cloudflare Workers launched in 2017 as a serverless edge computing platform allowing developers to run JavaScript at Cloudflare's global network edge. It pioneered the V8 isolates approach to cold-start-free serverless execution. The platform has since expanded to support multiple languages and the broader Workers platform including D1, R2, KV, and Durable Objects.
Cloudflare Workers.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Workers is an edge computing platform that, like all serverless platforms, can be misused to host phishing pages, malware proxies, or command-and-control servers behind Cloudflare's trusted infrastructure. This has been documented in security research. However, legitimate use by developers overwhelmingly dominates, and Cloudflare actively enforces its AUP to take down abusive deployments.

Cloudflare Zaraz.description: Cloudflare Zaraz gives you complete control over third-party tools and services for your website, and allows you to offload them to Cloudflare’s edge, improving the speed and security of your website.
Cloudflare Zaraz.history: Cloudflare Zaraz is a server-side tag management solution acquired by Cloudflare in December 2021 (originally a startup called Zaraz). It moves third-party scripts like analytics and marketing pixels to Cloudflare's edge, improving page performance and privacy. The product became generally available in 2022 and integrated into Cloudflare's dashboard for all customers.
Cloudflare Zaraz.summary_abuse: Cloudflare Zaraz is a server-side tag manager that executes third-party scripts at the network edge rather than in the browser. This improves privacy by reducing browser-side data leakage. No abuse signal exists for this product.

Cloudfy.description: Cloudfy is a SaaS-based enterprise-level B2B ecommerce platform designed to streamline online business operations.
Cloudfy.history: Cloudfy is a UK-based B2B e-commerce SaaS platform serving manufacturers, distributors, and wholesalers. It focuses on ERP integration with pre-built connectors for SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, Oracle NetSuite, and Sage. The company released Enterprise v5 in 2026 with a modular API-first architecture. It operates as an independent company targeting complex B2B selling models.
Cloudfy.summary_abuse: Cloudfy is a B2B e-commerce platform used by manufacturers and distributors for trade customer portals. It operates exclusively in the enterprise B2B space with authenticated customers. No notable abuse signal or documented misuse incidents exist.

Cloudify.store.description: Cloudify.store is a subscription-based platform that allows anyone to set up a hyperlocal quick commerce business.
Cloudify.store.history: Cloudify.store is a subscription platform for setting up hyperlocal quick-commerce businesses. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Cloudify.store.summary_abuse: Cloudify.store is a quick-commerce platform for local businesses with no documented abuse incidents. The platform allows merchant storefronts and has standard e-commerce risk exposure but no specific abuse reports.

Cloudimage.description: Cloudimage automates the transformation and optimisation of images on the fly and accelerates their distribution via the Content Delivery Network (CDN).
Cloudimage.history: Cloudimage is an image CDN and optimization product from Scaleflex, a French media technology company. The service automates image resizing, compression, format conversion (WebP/AVIF), and delivery via global CDN. Customers include Rakuten, Michelin, and Hyundai. The product is developed under the Scaleflex umbrella alongside other digital asset management tools.
Cloudimage.summary_abuse: Cloudimage is a developer-focused image CDN with no notable abuse signal. Its role is to optimize and serve images for websites, with no user-generated content or anonymous hosting capabilities. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

Cloudinary.description: Cloudinary is an end-to-end image- and video-management solution for websites and mobile apps, covering everything from image and video uploads, storage, manipulations, optimisations to delivery.
Cloudinary.history: Cloudinary was founded in 2012 by Itai Lahan, Tal Lev-Ami, and Nadav Soferman in Israel. The company bootstrapped to profitability before receiving investment from Bessemer Venture Partners in 2015, when it also opened US headquarters and added video management. It has grown into the dominant cloud media management platform serving enterprises including media, e-commerce, and digital experience platforms. AI-powered features were added in 2024.
Cloudinary.summary_abuse: Cloudinary is the dominant cloud image and video management platform with minimal direct abuse signal for the platform itself. Media hosted via Cloudinary could theoretically include harmful content, but the platform targets enterprise customers and enforces content policies. No notable documented incidents of systematic abuse exist.

Cloudrexx.description: Cloudrexx is a proprietary content management system that provides customisable templates and built-in modules for managing content, ecommerce, events, newsletters, and more, along with tools for SEO, social media integration, and multilingual support.
Cloudrexx.history: Cloudrexx (originally released as Contrexx) is a Swiss CMS developed since 2005 and headquartered in Thun, Switzerland. The platform is offered as a managed SaaS CMS with hosting, updates, and support included. It is maintained by a very small team generating approximately CHF 220K in annual revenue. The product targets Swiss SMBs and web developers.
Cloudrexx.summary_abuse: Cloudrexx is a small Swiss CMS with no notable abuse signal. It serves professionally managed websites for Swiss SMBs and does not allow anonymous content hosting. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

CloudSponge.description: CloudSponge is a software-as-a-service tool for importing contacts from address book providers like Gmail, Yahoo Mail, Outlook.com, iCloud and AOL.
CloudSponge.history: CloudSponge is a SaaS API that allows websites to import contacts from major address book providers including Gmail, Yahoo Mail, Outlook, and 50+ others. The service provides a JavaScript widget and REST API for contact importing. The company operates independently and has served as a backend tool for referral programs and viral growth features.
CloudSponge.summary_abuse: CloudSponge accesses users' contact lists from email providers, which raises privacy concerns as this type of API has historically been used by applications that bulk-harvest contacts for spam and social engineering campaigns. The company operates legitimately and requires OAuth consent, but contact harvesting APIs inherently carry misuse risk when integrated into apps that use contacts for unsolicited outreach.

CloudSuite.history: Infor CloudSuite is the cloud ERP product line from Infor, a major enterprise software company. Infor began its cloud initiative around 2012-2013, investing $5 billion in rewriting its ERP products as industry-specific, multi-tenant SaaS running exclusively on AWS. Products like SyteLine were rebranded as CloudSuite Industrial. Infor itself was acquired by Koch Industries in 2020 in a deal valued at approximately $13 billion.
CloudSuite.summary_abuse: Infor CloudSuite is an enterprise ERP platform used exclusively by large organizations for business operations management. It has no consumer-facing surface or anonymous access. No abuse signal exists for this product.

Cloudways.description: Cloudways offers managed cloud-hosting services for WordPress sites on a cloud server where multiple copies of your content will be replicated throughout your chosen data center.
Cloudways.history: Cloudways was founded in 2012 by Aaqib Gadit and Pere Hospital in Malta to simplify cloud hosting via a managed platform layer on top of AWS, Google Cloud, DigitalOcean, and Vultr. In August 2022, DigitalOcean acquired Cloudways for $350 million in cash, integrating it into DigitalOcean's SMB hosting portfolio. The platform serves over 100,000 customers hosting 570,000+ websites.
Cloudways.summary_abuse: Cloudways is a managed cloud hosting platform with low abuse signal for a hosting provider. As an intermediary layer on top of major cloud providers, Cloudways benefits from their infrastructure-level abuse detection and complements it with its own AUP. Like all hosting platforms, malicious sites can be provisioned, but the managed nature and association with DigitalOcean provide reasonable accountability.

Cloverly.description: Cloverly is an API integration for ethical ecommerce brands to help their customers offset the carbon footprint of their online transactions.
Cloverly.history: Cloverly launched on Earth Day 2019 as a carbon offset API platform, describing itself as Sustainability as a Service. The company raised a $2.1M seed round in 2021 and subsequently raised a $19M Series A. The platform enables businesses to calculate, track, and purchase verified carbon offsets via API, integrating with e-commerce, logistics, and financial platforms.
Cloverly.summary_abuse: Cloverly is a carbon offset API platform with no notable abuse signal. Its core use case is sustainability accounting and offset purchasing for businesses. The carbon credit market has broader quality and greenwashing concerns industry-wide, but no specific documented incidents of Cloverly being misused for fraud exist.

Clubcast.description: Clubcast is a platform for managing and personalizing content for digital products, designed to enhance fan experiences and support revenue growth.
Clubcast.history: Clubcast is a UK-based digital content management and fan experience platform. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
Clubcast.summary_abuse: Clubcast is a UK content management SaaS for fan engagement with no notable abuse signal.

Cludo.description: Cludo is a cloud-based search solution.
Cludo.history: Cludo was founded in 2015 by Niels Ebbe Ebbesen in Copenhagen, Denmark. The company emerged to fill the gap left by Google's discontinuation of its Search Appliance product, offering intelligent site search and intranet search as SaaS. The name combines 'Clue' and 'Do'. The company serves organizations needing searchable website and intranet experiences.
Cludo.summary_abuse: Cludo is a site search SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It indexes website content to provide search capabilities, operating in a B2B context. No documented incidents of misuse exist.

Cluep.description: Cluep's artificially intelligent mobile ad platform targets people based on what they are sharing, how they are feeling and where they go in the physical world.
Cluep.history: Cluep was founded in 2012 and is headquartered in Toronto, Canada. The company built a patented AI-powered mobile advertising platform that targets users based on their social media posts, shared photos, emotions, and location history. The platform uses text analysis and image recognition to serve hyper-targeted mobile ads. Cluep raised early VC funding and serves brands and advertisers.
Cluep.summary_abuse: Cluep is an ad-tech platform that harvests detailed emotional and behavioral data from social media posts, photos, and location history to serve targeted mobile ads. This type of deep behavioral profiling raises significant privacy concerns and may conflict with data protection regulations. The platform collects data about users' emotional states, physical locations, and social content without necessarily making this clear to end users. No specific security incidents have been documented, but the depth of behavioral surveillance is well above typical ad-tech.

Cluster CIS.description: Cluster CIS is a provider of business automation solutions, including WooCommerce ERP, CRM, WMS, SFA, alongside website development and e-shop construction services.
Cluster CIS.history: Cluster CIS is a Greek business automation company providing WooCommerce ERP, CRM, and WMS solutions. No public founding year or ownership changes are documented.
Cluster CIS.summary_abuse: Cluster CIS is a Greek business automation SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves B2B merchants with backend tools.

ClustrMaps Widget.description: ClustrMaps widget is a visitor tracker, designed for general web and blog use.
ClustrMaps Widget.history: ClustrMaps is a visitor tracking widget service that displays a thumbnail world map showing the geographic locations of website visitors. It has been available since the mid-2000s, primarily targeting bloggers and personal websites. The service delivers over 7 million widget views per day and is used across various CMS platforms including WordPress and Drupal.
ClustrMaps Widget.summary_abuse: ClustrMaps collects visitor IP addresses to determine geographic location for map display purposes. It is a tracker by nature but limited in scope. No major data breach or abuse incidents are publicly reported. The service falls within standard web analytics use cases with low privacy risk beyond basic location tracking.

Clutch.description: Clutch review widgets are stand-alone applications that you can embed on your website to show your dynamic ratings and reviews.
Clutch.history: Clutch was founded in 2012 in Washington D.C. by Mike Beares as a B2B ratings and reviews platform for IT, marketing, and business services providers. The platform uses verified, interview-based reviews to vet agencies and service providers. It grew rapidly to cover over 350,000 providers globally and 1.4 million monthly buyers. Clutch received growth equity funding from Susquehanna Growth Equity (SGE) and has maintained strong year-over-year traffic growth.
Clutch.summary_abuse: Clutch is primarily used as a legitimate B2B marketplace for finding service providers. Some reputation manipulation is possible through fake reviews or incentivized testimonials, which is common in review platform ecosystems. No major data breach or malicious abuse incidents have been publicly documented. The company requires verified client accounts and uses phone or online interview verification to reduce fake reviews, making outright abuse less common than on self-service review sites.

Clym.description: Clym is a data privacy tool designed to support compliance with data protection obligations.
Clym.history: Clym is a digital compliance SaaS platform providing consent management, cookie compliance, and web accessibility tools. Founded around 2020, the company offers a unified interface covering GDPR, CPRA, LGPD, and 150+ global regulations. It includes a consent management platform (CMP), DSAR management, accessibility widget, and age gating. Deployed as a WordPress plugin or script on Shopify, Wix, and other CMS platforms.
Clym.summary_abuse: Clym is a compliance-focused consent management platform with no notable abuse signal. The product is designed specifically for privacy law compliance, making it unlikely to be leveraged in abusive contexts. No reported incidents of misuse, data breaches, or malicious activity are publicly documented.

CMoffer.description: CMoffer is a drop shipping app focused on necklaces, rings, bracelets, earrings, and other creative gifts.
CMoffer.history: CMoffer is a Chinese dropshipping app focused on jewelry products. It provides product sourcing and fulfillment for e-commerce merchants.
CMoffer.summary_abuse: CMoffer is a dropshipping app for jewelry products originating from China. Dropshipping platforms in this category are frequently associated with consumer fraud where storefronts sell counterfeit or low-quality goods with misleading claims. CMoffer enables such storefronts through its fulfillment service. Consumer complaints about dropshipping-based jewelry scams are common.

CmonSite.description: CmonSite is a French-based platform for building websites and ecommerce stores.
CmonSite.history: CmonSite is a French website builder platform that has operated for over 15 years, providing small businesses and individuals with tools to create websites and online stores without coding. Based in France with French-speaking support teams, it holds NF525 certification. The platform has steadily added features including e-commerce, abandoned cart emails, inventory management, and SEO tools over the years.
CmonSite.summary_abuse: CmonSite is a French website builder with no notable abuse signal documented publicly. As a general-purpose site builder, it could theoretically host phishing or fraudulent storefronts, but no specific documented incidents or patterns of abuse are recorded. The French regulatory context and NF525 certification suggest reasonable compliance practices.

CMS Caddy.description: CMS Caddy is a headless content management system that provides a flexible structure for publishing and managing content across projects and devices in a single platform.
CMS Caddy.history: CMS Caddy is a headless content management system for flexible content publishing across devices and projects. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
CMS Caddy.summary_abuse: CMS Caddy is a niche headless CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a content management tool for developers with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

CMS Made Simple.history: CMS Made Simple was created in 2004 by Ted Kulp as a free, open-source PHP content management system. It targets small-to-medium websites wanting a simpler alternative to Drupal or WordPress. The project is community maintained.
CMS Made Simple.summary_abuse: CMS Made Simple is a self-hosted open-source PHP CMS. Older versions have had multiple known SQL injection and remote code execution vulnerabilities. Sites running unpatched versions have been compromised and used to host malware or phishing pages. Keeping the software updated significantly mitigates this risk.

CMSimple.history: CMSimple is one of the oldest flat-file PHP CMS systems, originally released in 1999 by Peter Andreas Harteg. It operates without a database by storing content in flat files. The project spawned several forks including CMSimple_XH. Activity has slowed significantly as of the early 2020s.
CMSimple.summary_abuse: CMSimple is a legacy flat-file CMS with a very small user base. Unpatched installations can be vulnerable to code injection. No recent major public exploits documented.

Cnvert.description: Cnvert is a self-hosted lead and marketing automation system, formerly known as Sonician.
Cnvert.history: Originally established in 2008 as Sonician UK Limited, the company rebranded to Cnvert Sweden AB in 2018. It provides a suite of B2B marketing automation, CRM, and onboarding tools designed to help businesses with client acquisition and retention, primarily serving the European market.
Cnvert.summary_abuse: Cnvert is a B2B marketing automation and CRM platform. While marketing tools with email capabilities can theoretically be abused for spam, its focus on legitimate SMBs and sales-gated onboarding make it an unattractive target for dedicated scammers, resulting in a low abuse signal.

CNZZ.history: CNZZ was founded in 2007 as one of the first major Chinese web analytics platforms, similar to Google Analytics but targeting China. It was acquired by Alibaba and merged into the Umeng analytics suite. It tracks hundreds of millions of Chinese web users.
CNZZ.summary_abuse: CNZZ is a Chinese web analytics tracker operated by Alibaba. Data is collected under Chinese jurisdiction with no GDPR coverage. Being embedded on Western sites routes Chinese user analytics data to Alibaba infrastructure. Privacy risks are significant for non-Chinese deployments. No specific malicious use cases documented but it is a data-collection tracker with minimal transparency for Western users.

Coachy.description: Coachy is a platform for creating and selling online courses with integrated payment systems and GDPR-compliant data storage​.
Coachy.history: Founded in 2017 by Dennis Koray, Coachy is a German-language Learning Management System (LMS). It was developed to provide coaches and course creators with an easy-to-use, GDPR-compliant platform for hosting video courses and membership areas, primarily targeting the DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland) market.
Coachy.summary_abuse: Coachy is a B2B platform for creating and selling online courses. While online course platforms are occasionally used by aggressive marketers to sell low-value or deceptive content, the platform's paid subscription model and integration with strict payment processors like Digistore24 keep its abuse signal low.

Coaster CMS.history: Released in 2016 by the UK-based digital agency Web-Feet, Coaster CMS is an open-source content management system built on the Laravel framework. It was designed to offer a developer-friendly, block-based alternative to WordPress with unique features like physical web (beacon) integration, though development has largely ceased since 2021.
Coaster CMS.summary_abuse: Coaster CMS is an open-source, self-hosted content management system. It runs entirely on the user's own infrastructure and has no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Coax.description: Coax is a communication platform that consolidates chat histories from multiple channels to improve business communication and customer service.
Coax.history: Founded in 2023 and based in New South Wales, Australia, Coax is a B2B SaaS communication platform. It was developed to provide small and medium-sized businesses with a unified inbox, consolidating messages from Meta, WhatsApp, SMS, and email into a single customer conversation thread.
Coax.summary_abuse: Coax is a B2B omnichannel communication platform and unified inbox. While messaging tools can theoretically be abused for spam or aggressive outreach, its high subscription cost and focus on legitimate SMBs make it an unattractive target for dedicated scammers, resulting in a low abuse signal.

CoCo AI.description: CoCo AI is a WhatsApp marketing assistant that uses artificial intelligence to automate campaigns, manage customer interactions, and improve engagement.
CoCo AI.history: Founded in 2023 by a team of French entrepreneurs, CoCo AI was developed as a Shopify application to automate WhatsApp marketing and abandoned cart recovery. The bootstrapped startup quickly gained traction among e-commerce brands before being acquired by Because Ventures in 2024.
CoCo AI.summary_abuse: CoCo AI is a B2B marketing automation app for Shopify stores. While automated WhatsApp messaging and cart recovery tools can be perceived as intrusive or used for spam, the platform requires integration with a legitimate Shopify store and adherence to WhatsApp Business API policies, keeping its abuse signal low.

Cococart.history: Founded in 2020 by Zhicong Lim and Derek Low in San Francisco, Cococart is an e-commerce platform designed for local businesses and creators. Backed by Y Combinator and Sequoia Capital, it was built to provide a simple, no-code storefront solution for merchants who previously relied on social media DMs to take orders.
Cococart.summary_abuse: Cococart is a B2B e-commerce platform that allows local businesses and creators to build online storefronts. Like all e-commerce builders, it is occasionally abused by fraudsters to host fake stores or scam sites, but the vendor maintains an active anti-abuse posture and responds to takedown requests.

COCON.description: COCON is an email marketing strategy designed to enhance the performance of a Shopify store.
COCON.history: COCON is an email marketing strategy app for Shopify, helping merchants improve their email campaigns through automated sending strategies.
COCON.summary_abuse: COCON is a Shopify email marketing app. Email marketing tools carry inherent spam risk if misconfigured or misused. No major documented abuse incidents specific to COCON.

CoConstruct.history: Founded in 2005 by Donny Wyatt in Charlottesville, Virginia, CoConstruct is a cloud-based project management platform for custom home builders and remodelers. It grew to serve over 100,000 building professionals before being acquired by and unified with its main competitor, Buildertrend, in 2021.
CoConstruct.summary_abuse: CoConstruct is a B2B project management platform for home builders and remodelers. It is used to manage construction projects, client communications, and financials, and has no consumer-facing hosting features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

CoconutSoftware.description: Coconut is a cloud-based appointment scheduling solution designed for enterprise financial services organisations such as credit unions, retail banks and more.
CoconutSoftware.history: Founded in 2007 by Katherine Regnier in Saskatoon, Canada, Coconut Software is a cloud-based appointment scheduling and lobby management platform. It evolved to focus specifically on the financial services sector, providing banks and credit unions with enterprise-grade tools for managing customer appointments, video banking, and branch queues.
CoconutSoftware.summary_abuse: Coconut Software is an enterprise appointment scheduling and branch management platform for financial institutions. It is used by banks and credit unions to manage customer interactions and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Cocos2d.description: Cocos2d is a mature open source cross-platform game development framework.
Cocos2d.history: Originally created in 2008 as a Python framework, Cocos2d evolved into the highly popular C++ port Cocos2d-x in 2011, backed by Chinese company Chukong Technologies (Xiamen Yaji). It became one of the world's most widely used open-source 2D game engines, powering thousands of mobile games before the company shifted focus to its commercial successor, Cocos Creator.
Cocos2d.summary_abuse: Cocos2d is an open-source game development framework. It runs locally on developers' machines and compiles into client-side applications, possessing no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Code Climate.description: Code Climate is a platform that enhances code quality and efficiency.
Code Climate.history: Founded in 2011 by Bryan Helmkamp and Noah Davis in New York, Code Climate began as an automated code review and static analysis tool for Ruby on Rails. It has since expanded into a comprehensive software engineering intelligence platform, raising over $68 million to help engineering teams track code quality, technical debt, and team velocity.
Code Climate.summary_abuse: Code Climate is a B2B software engineering intelligence and automated code review platform. It integrates with version control systems to analyze code quality and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

CodeIgniter.history: Created in 2006 by Rick Ellis (EllisLab), CodeIgniter was one of the first widely adopted PHP MVC frameworks. Known for its small footprint and speed, it dominated the PHP ecosystem for years before being transferred to the British Columbia Institute of Technology (BCIT) in 2014, and later to the CodeIgniter Foundation, which continues to maintain version 4.
CodeIgniter.summary_abuse: CodeIgniter is an open-source PHP web framework. It runs locally on developers' machines or on self-hosted servers to build web applications, possessing no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

CodeMirror.description: CodeMirror is a JavaScript component that provides a code editor in the browser.
CodeMirror.history: Created by Marijn Haverbeke in 2007, CodeMirror began as an interactive console for the Eloquent JavaScript website. It evolved into one of the most widely used open-source code editor components for the web, powering the developer tools in major browsers and numerous online IDEs like CodePen and JSFiddle.
CodeMirror.summary_abuse: CodeMirror is an open-source JavaScript library used to embed code editors in web applications. It runs entirely within the user's browser and has no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

CodeSandbox.description: CodeSandbox is an online code editor and prototyping tool that makes creating and sharing web apps faster.
CodeSandbox.history: Founded in 2017 by Ives van Hoorne and Bas Buursma in Amsterdam, CodeSandbox began as an online code editor for React. It evolved into a comprehensive cloud development environment (CDE) with high-performance VM sandboxes, becoming a staple in the frontend community before being acquired by Together AI in 2024.
CodeSandbox.summary_abuse: CodeSandbox is a cloud development environment and online code editor. Because it offers a free tier that allows users to quickly deploy web applications and host static files, it is frequently abused by threat actors to host phishing landing pages, malware, or run unauthorized crypto-mining scripts, though the vendor actively monitors for abuse.

Codetipi.description: Codetipi is a provider of WordPress themes and plugins designed for premium users.
Codetipi.history: Founded in 2012 by Chris Bell in London, UK, Codetipi is an independent development studio specializing in premium WordPress themes and plugins. The studio gained significant popularity on the Envato marketplace with flagship magazine themes like Valenti and Zeen, serving over 27,000 clients globally.
Codetipi.summary_abuse: Codetipi develops premium WordPress themes and plugins. The software is self-hosted by customers on their own infrastructure and has no centralized hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Coffee Script.description: CoffeeScript is a language that compiles into JavaScript.
Coffee Script.history: Created in 2009 by Jeremy Ashkenas, CoffeeScript is a programming language that compiles to JavaScript. It introduced syntactic sugar inspired by Ruby and Python, significantly influencing the evolution of modern JavaScript (ES6) before its popularity waned as native JavaScript adopted many of its features.
Coffee Script.summary_abuse: CoffeeScript is an open-source programming language and compiler. It runs locally on developers' machines to generate JavaScript and has no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

CoffeeCup.description: CoffeeCup is a suite of web development tools for building sites, emails, and forms, with optional coding for added flexibility.
CoffeeCup.history: Founded in 1996 in an internet cafe in Texas, CoffeeCup Software is one of the oldest web development software companies. It is best known for its HTML Editor and Direct FTP tools, which were highly popular in the early days of the web.
CoffeeCup.summary_abuse: CoffeeCup Software provides desktop web design tools and basic hosting services. As a traditional software vendor, it has no notable consumer-facing abuse signal.

Cognigy.description: Cognigy is a provider of generative and conversational AI-powered customer service agents for businesses.
Cognigy.history: Founded in 2016 in Düsseldorf, Germany, Cognigy developed an enterprise conversational AI platform for automating customer service. It grew to become a market leader in AI agents for contact centers, raising $165 million before being acquired by customer experience software giant NICE in 2025.
Cognigy.summary_abuse: Cognigy is an enterprise conversational AI and customer service platform. It is used by large organizations to deploy AI agents and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in a low abuse signal.

Coin Currency Converter.description: Coin Currency Converter is an automatic currency conversion app for Shopify.
Coin Currency Converter.history: Developed by ShopPad Inc. (founded in 2012 in Oakland, California), Coin Currency Converter is a Shopify application designed to help e-commerce merchants sell internationally. It automatically detects a visitor's location and converts product prices into their local currency in real-time.
Coin Currency Converter.summary_abuse: Coin Currency Converter is a B2B application for Shopify merchants. It provides automated currency conversion for e-commerce storefronts and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Coinbase Commerce.description: Coinbase Commerce is a platform that enables merchants to accept cryptocurrency payments.
Coinbase Commerce.history: Launched in 2018 by Coinbase, Coinbase Commerce is a cryptocurrency payment gateway for merchants. It was developed to allow businesses to accept payments in Bitcoin, Ethereum, USDC, and other cryptocurrencies directly into their own wallets, growing to process billions of dollars in on-chain transactions globally.
Coinbase Commerce.summary_abuse: Coinbase Commerce is a cryptocurrency payment processor for merchants. Because it facilitates decentralized and semi-anonymous payments, it is frequently targeted by scammers, illicit storefronts, and high-risk merchants. However, Coinbase enforces strict KYC/AML policies and actively bans accounts associated with fraud or illegal activities, mitigating widespread abuse.

Coinhave.description: CoinHave is a cryptocurrency mining service.
Coinhave.history: Launched in 2018 as a Monero JavaScript mining service, allowing website owners to embed a JS miner that used visitor CPU to mine XMR. Operations were frozen in early 2019 due to low cryptocurrency exchange rates; withdrawals were honored for accounts with over 0.25 XMR. The domain later became defunct and now serves no meaningful content.
Coinhave.summary_abuse: Coinhave was a browser-based JavaScript cryptominer service, similar to CoinHive. It was widely deployed on websites without meaningful user consent, consuming visitors' CPU resources for Monero mining. The service explicitly offered Adblock bypass and acknowledged abuse in third-party contexts such as unauthorized YouTube miners. The service froze in early 2019 due to low XMR prices and never resumed operations. An abuse contact was provided but enforcement over already-deployed scripts was limited. The defunct domain now returns no real content.

CoinHive.description: Coinhive is a cryptocurrency mining service.
CoinHive.history: Launched in 2017, Coinhive was a cryptocurrency mining service that provided a JavaScript API to mine Monero using website visitors' CPU power. While initially marketed as an alternative to web advertising, it was overwhelmingly abused by hackers for 'cryptojacking' compromised websites. The service officially shut down in March 2019 following a drop in Monero's value and widespread blocking by antivirus software.
CoinHive.summary_abuse: Coinhive was the primary enabler of the 'cryptojacking' epidemic. Its JavaScript miner was overwhelmingly injected into compromised websites, browser extensions, and apps by malicious actors to silently steal visitors' CPU power to mine Monero. Due to this rampant abuse, it was universally blacklisted by ad blockers and antivirus vendors before shutting down in 2019.

CoinHive Captcha.description: Coinhive Captcha provides captcha service that is simple to integrate, where your users’ devices need to solve a number of hashes, adjustable by you, in order to login or post a comment to your site.
CoinHive Captcha.history: Launched in 2017, Coinhive Captcha was a spin-off product of the Coinhive cryptocurrency mining service. It replaced traditional image-based CAPTCHAs with a Proof-of-Work system, requiring visitors' devices to mine a set amount of Monero to prove they were human. The service officially shut down in March 2019 alongside the main Coinhive platform.
CoinHive Captcha.summary_abuse: Coinhive Captcha was a Proof-of-Work CAPTCHA service that forced users to mine cryptocurrency to access content. Like the main Coinhive miner, it was widely considered a form of 'cryptojacking' and was universally blacklisted by ad blockers and antivirus vendors before shutting down in 2019.

Coinimp.description: CoinImp is a cryptocurrency mining service.
Coinimp.history: Founded in 2017 and based in Warsaw, Poland, CoinImp emerged as a major competitor to Coinhive in the browser-based cryptocurrency mining space. It provided a JavaScript API that allowed website owners to monetize their traffic by mining Webchain (now MintMe) and Monero using visitors' CPU power.
Coinimp.summary_abuse: CoinImp is a browser-based cryptocurrency mining service. Like Coinhive, its JavaScript miner is overwhelmingly abused by malicious actors who inject it into compromised websites, browser extensions, and apps to silently steal visitors' CPU power for 'cryptojacking.' Due to this rampant abuse, it is universally blacklisted by ad blockers and antivirus vendors.

Colbass.description: Colbass is AI System that transforms text into real-human voiceovers.
Colbass.history: Colbass is an AI-powered text-to-voiceover widget service offered on websites as an embedded audio player. Limited public corporate history is available; the product appears to be a small, recently launched AI audio tool.
Colbass.summary_abuse: Colbass is a small AI voice synthesis widget with no documented abuse signal. AI text-to-speech tools are theoretically misusable for generating fraudulent audio content, but Colbass specifically is not documented as a known tool in abuse campaigns.

Colibri WP.description: Colibri WP is a drag-and-drop WordPress website builder.
Colibri WP.history: Colibri WP is a drag-and-drop WordPress theme and page builder developed by Extend Themes, a Romanian WordPress theme shop. The free theme was published on WordPress.org around 2019, with a paid pro version offering advanced features. It remains a niche WordPress ecosystem product with no notable acquisitions or major ownership changes.
Colibri WP.summary_abuse: Colibri WP has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted WordPress page builder theme distributed primarily through WordPress.org. Like all WordPress themes, it inherits whatever security posture the site owner maintains; the theme itself introduces no known vulnerabilities or abuse patterns.

Colis Privé.description: Colis Privé is a private parcel delivery service provider specialised in last-mile delivery.
Colis Privé.history: Founded in 1993, Colis Privé is a French private parcel delivery service specializing in last-mile delivery for B2C e-commerce. It grew to become the largest private postal operator in France, delivering tens of millions of parcels annually, before being acquired by the global shipping group CMA CGM in 2022.
Colis Privé.summary_abuse: Colis Privé is a physical shipping and logistics carrier operating primarily in France. It provides parcel delivery and tracking services and has no digital hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Colissimo.description: Colissimo is a 'drop off' parcel delivery service.
Colissimo.history: Created in 1989, Colissimo is the parcel delivery service of La Poste, the French national postal service. It provides domestic and international shipping solutions for both consumers and e-commerce businesses.
Colissimo.summary_abuse: Colissimo is a major French parcel delivery service. While shipping carriers can be tangentially involved in e-commerce fraud (such as triangulation scams), the service itself is a legitimate logistics provider with no direct digital abuse surface.

Collect.chat.description: Collect.chat is an interactive chatbot that collects data from website visitors.
Collect.chat.history: Founded in 2017 and based in Kozhikode, India, Collect.chat is a no-code conversational chatbot platform. It was developed to replace static web forms with interactive chat interfaces, allowing businesses to automate lead generation, appointment booking, and customer feedback collection.
Collect.chat.summary_abuse: Collect.chat is a B2B conversational chatbot platform embedded on customer websites. It is used by businesses to automate lead capture and customer service, and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in a low abuse signal.

Collective Audience.description: Collective Audience is a cloud ecosystem for media and brands on the open web, formerly known as BeOp.
Collective Audience.history: Originally founded as BeOp, a French AdTech startup specializing in conversational advertising formats. The company was later acquired and rebranded as Collective Audience, which now trades publicly on the OTCQB market.
Collective Audience.summary_abuse: Collective Audience (formerly BeOp) is an advertising and audience data platform. Like most AdTech networks, its tracking scripts are heavily embedded across publisher sites to collect user data, though it primarily serves premium brands and publishers rather than low-quality affiliates.

CollectiveAccess.description: CollectiveAccess is a free, open-source software for cataloguing and publishing museum and archival collections.
CollectiveAccess.history: Started in 2003 by the New York-based software company Whirl-i-Gig, CollectiveAccess (formerly OpenCollection) was developed to provide an affordable, open-source collections management system for museums and archives. It was officially released under the GPL in 2007 and has since been adopted by cultural heritage institutions worldwide.
CollectiveAccess.summary_abuse: CollectiveAccess is an open-source collections management system designed for museums and archives. It runs on the user's own infrastructure and has no hosted components or consumer-facing surface, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Colorlib Activello.description: Colorlib Activello is a clean, minimal multipurpose WordPress blog theme developer using the Bootstrap frontend framework making it fully responsive and mobile-friendly.
Colorlib Activello.history: Founded in 2013 by Aigars Silkalns in Riga, Latvia, Colorlib is a prominent developer of free and premium WordPress themes. Activello was released as a clean, minimal, multipurpose blog theme built on the Bootstrap framework, becoming one of the company's most popular offerings in the WordPress repository.
Colorlib Activello.summary_abuse: Colorlib Activello is a self-hosted WordPress theme. It runs entirely on the user's own infrastructure and has no centralized hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

Colorlib Illdy.description: Colorlib Illdy is a stunning multipurpose WordPress theme built based on Bootstrap frontend framework making it fully responsive and mobile friendly.
Colorlib Illdy.history: Colorlib was founded in 2013 by Aigars Silkalns and has become a major provider of free and premium WordPress themes. Illdy is one of their popular multipurpose themes built on the Bootstrap framework.
Colorlib Illdy.summary_abuse: Colorlib Illdy is a WordPress theme. As a self-hosted software component, it has no hosted consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Colorlib Shapely.description: Colorlib Shapely is considered as a powerful, clean and beautiful full-width free WordPress theme.
Colorlib Shapely.history: Colorlib was founded in 2013 and released the Shapely theme around 2016 as a one-page WordPress theme with Bootstrap-based design. Colorlib has grown to become one of the most prolific free WordPress theme developers, with themes downloaded millions of times from the WordPress.org repository. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Colorlib Shapely.summary_abuse: Shapely is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted WordPress theme, it does not provide hosting or content distribution infrastructure that could be abused independently. Any abuse of sites using Shapely would be a WordPress-level issue rather than theme-specific.

Colorlib Sparkling.description: Colorlib Sparkling is a clean, modern, flat design WordPress theme developed using Bootstrap.
Colorlib Sparkling.history: Colorlib released the Sparkling WordPress theme around 2014 as a clean, modern flat-design theme built on Bootstrap. It is distributed free on WordPress.org and has been one of Colorlib's most popular themes. Colorlib was founded in 2013 and remains an independent WordPress theme developer. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Colorlib Sparkling.summary_abuse: Sparkling is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted WordPress theme, it does not provide hosting or content distribution infrastructure that could be abused independently. Any abuse of sites using Sparkling would be a WordPress-level concern rather than theme-specific.

Colorlib Travelify.description: Colorlib Travelify is a responsive, free, travel WordPress theme.
Colorlib Travelify.history: Colorlib released the Travelify theme in 2013, making it one of their earliest and most popular free WordPress themes. It became the most popular travel-themed WordPress theme on WordPress.org, downloaded several million times. Colorlib is an independent WordPress theme developer founded in 2013. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Colorlib Travelify.summary_abuse: Travelify is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted WordPress theme, it does not provide hosting or content distribution infrastructure that could be abused independently. The travel theme niche sees some use for fake travel booking scams at the WordPress deployment level, but this is not specific to the theme itself.

ColorMag.description: ColorMag theme is for creating news, magazine, newspaper and other kinds of publishing sites. Compatible with Elementor.
ColorMag.history: Launched in 2014 by ThemeGrill, a WordPress development company based in Kathmandu, Nepal, ColorMag is a highly popular magazine and news theme for WordPress. It grew to become one of the most widely used free magazine themes in the WordPress repository, powering tens of thousands of publishing sites globally.
ColorMag.summary_abuse: ColorMag is a self-hosted WordPress theme for news and magazine websites. It runs entirely on the user's own infrastructure and has no centralized hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in no notable abuse signal.

ColorMeShop.description: ColorMeShop is an ecommerce platform from Japan.
ColorMeShop.history: Launched in 2005 by GMO Pepabo, ColorMeShop is one of Japan's most popular e-commerce platforms. It was developed to provide small and medium-sized businesses with an accessible, feature-rich online store builder, growing to support tens of thousands of merchants across the Japanese market.
ColorMeShop.summary_abuse: ColorMeShop is a B2B e-commerce platform that allows merchants to build and manage online stores. Like all major e-commerce builders, it is occasionally abused by fraudsters to host fake stores or scam sites, but the vendor maintains an active anti-abuse posture and responds to takedown requests.

Comarch e-Sklep.description: Comarch e-Sklep is an ecommerce platform for creating and managing an online store.
Comarch e-Sklep.history: Comarch is a Polish multinational IT company founded in 1993 by Janusz Filipiak in Krakow, Poland and listed on the Warsaw Stock Exchange. Comarch e-Sklep is their SaaS e-commerce platform product, primarily serving Polish and Central European businesses with B2C and B2B online store capabilities. Over 2,200 firms have used the platform. No major rebrands or controversies.
Comarch e-Sklep.summary_abuse: Comarch e-Sklep is a legitimate enterprise SaaS ecommerce platform from a publicly listed Polish IT company with no notable abuse signal. It is used by verified businesses requiring ERP integration, and the platform's KYC and business verification requirements limit anonymous or illicit use.

Combahton FlowShield.description: Combahton FlowShield is a network security solution designed to protect networks and servers from various cyber threats, including DDoS attacks, malware, and other types of malicious traffic.
Combahton FlowShield.history: Combahton GmbH is a German hosting and network infrastructure company offering custom server solutions, IaaS, managed hosting, and DDoS protection. Their FlowShield product is a self-developed DDoS appliance based on the netmap framework. The company operates from Germany and focuses on the European hosting market. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Combahton FlowShield.summary_abuse: Combahton FlowShield is a DDoS protection infrastructure product with no notable abuse signal. As a security product used to filter malicious traffic, it is on the defensive side of the abuse equation. Combahton's hosting services have standard acceptable use policies typical of German hosting providers.

Combeenation.description: Combeenation is a powerful cloud-based configurator platform.
Combeenation.history: Combeenation was founded in 2009 in Upper Austria by Klaus Pilsl and colleagues from FH Hagenberg. The company specializes in product configurator and CPQ (configure-price-quote) software, offering 2D and 3D product visualization tools for e-commerce. It remains an independent Austrian SaaS company with no notable ownership changes.
Combeenation.summary_abuse: Combeenation is a niche B2B SaaS product configurator platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves manufacturing and e-commerce companies for product customization workflows. The enterprise-oriented nature of the service with business verification limits any meaningful abuse surface.

Combodo iTop.description: Combodo iTop is an open-source IT service management (ITSM) and IT operations management (ITOM) platform developed by Combodo, a software company based in France.
Combodo iTop.history: Combodo was founded in 2009 by the creators of iTop (IT Operations Portal), an open-source ITSM and CMDB platform. Originally developed as an internal tool and released as open source, iTop has grown to over 700,000 downloads and 2,800+ organizations worldwide. Combodo celebrated its 15th anniversary in 2024. The project remains under the AGPL license with a commercial support and hosting offering alongside the open-source core.
Combodo iTop.summary_abuse: iTop is an open-source ITSM and CMDB platform with no notable abuse signal. As an IT service management tool deployed internally by organizations, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or scam abuse. The self-hosted deployment model further limits any centralized abuse vector.

Comeet.description: Comeet is an Collaborative Recruiting, and Applicant Tracking System.
