Comeet.history: Comeet was founded in 2013 as a collaborative applicant tracking system (ATS), launching in Israel in 2015 before expanding globally. Early customers included Fiverr and Playtika. The company later merged with or was acquired by Spark Hire, with the product rebranded as Spark Hire Recruit. It is headquartered in the US and was backed by investors including former Yahoo executives.
Comeet.summary_abuse: Comeet is a B2B applicant tracking SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform handles recruiter and candidate data, so privacy handling of job applicant PII is the primary concern, but there are no documented incidents of data misuse or malicious exploitation specific to the platform.

CometChat.description: CometChat is a SaaS solution that provides apps and websites with scalable text, voice, and video chat functionality.
CometChat.history: CometChat was founded in 2009 by brothers Anuj and Anant Garg and provides in-app messaging, voice, and video calling SDKs and APIs for developers. The company transitioned from a licensed model to a SaaS subscription model around 2018. It raised a $1.6M seed round in 2020 and a subsequent $10M Series A, totaling over $21M in funding from investors including Silicon Badia and Signal Peak. Headquartered in Denver, Colorado.
CometChat.summary_abuse: CometChat is a chat SDK and API platform used by developers to add messaging to apps. As an infrastructure tool embedded in customer applications, its abuse risk depends on how those applications are used. There are no documented large-scale abuse incidents specific to CometChat. The platform is used in telehealth and social communities where appropriate moderation policies are expected at the app level.

CometD.description: CometD is a scalable clustered web messaging platform that provides a HTTP-based event routing bus using the Ajax Push technology pattern known as Comet.
CometD.history: CometD is an open-source implementation of the Bayeux protocol for scalable web messaging, originating from the Comet programming model coined by Alex Russell in 2006. The first implementation was included in Jetty 6.1. CometD provides publish/subscribe messaging over HTTP and WebSocket using the Bayeux protocol. It is widely used in Salesforce Streaming API integrations. The project has been maintained by Simone Bordet and the community for over 15 years.
CometD.summary_abuse: CometD is a server-side messaging library implementing the Bayeux protocol with no notable abuse signal. As a backend messaging framework with no hosted consumer surface, it has no meaningful abuse exposure of its own.

Comgem.description: Comgen is a B2B ecommerce platform that facilitates transactions and interactions between businesses through a digital marketplace.
Comgem.history: Comgem was founded in 2004 in the UK as a B2B ecommerce and ERP platform targeting wholesalers, distributors, and manufacturers. The platform connects customer websites with ERP and order management systems and is focused on the UK market. It has served UK businesses for over two decades with no notable ownership changes or controversies.
Comgem.summary_abuse: Comgem is a UK B2B ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. The business-to-business nature of the platform, with verified trade accounts, significantly limits anonymous or illicit use. No documented abuse incidents.

Comm100.description: Comm100 is a provider of customer service and communication products.
Comm100.history: Comm100 was founded on July 3, 2009, in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. The company launched its first product, Comm100 Live Chat, on August 5, 2009. By April 2011 it had reached 100,000 registered business users. It has grown into an AI-powered omnichannel customer support platform serving over 200,000 businesses, including Rackspace, Stanford University, and the State of California. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Comm100.summary_abuse: Comm100 is a legitimate live chat and customer support platform with no notable abuse signal. The platform handles customer communication data for over 200,000 businesses. As a customer engagement tool, it collects visitor behavioral data, which is the primary privacy consideration. No documented phishing or scam abuse incidents specific to the platform.

Commanders Act TagCommander.description: Commanders Act TagCommander is a European company providing a tag management product designed to handle website tags.
Commanders Act TagCommander.history: Founded in 2010 in France under the name TagCommander, Commanders Act rebranded to its current name to reflect its expansion into a full Customer Data Platform (CDP). The company is a significant player in European tag management and consent management, with TrustCommander being the only CMP exempted by the French data protection authority CNIL. The company was acquired by Piano, a US analytics and content monetization company.
Commanders Act TagCommander.summary_abuse: TagCommander is a tag management system used by enterprises primarily in Europe. As a marketing data orchestration tool, it handles user tracking and behavioral data, which is the primary privacy concern rather than external abuse. No documented phishing or scam incidents specific to the platform.

Commanders Act TrustCommander.description: Commanders Act TrustCommander is a consent management platform (CMP) which allows you to comply with the general data protection regulation (GDPR) regulation in terms of collecting consent.
Commanders Act TrustCommander.history: TrustCommander is the consent management platform (CMP) product of Commanders Act, a French company founded in 2010. It gained regulatory recognition as the only CMP exempted by France's CNIL for producing anonymous audience statistics. Commanders Act (formerly TagCommander) was acquired by Piano, a US analytics company, and TrustCommander continues to serve European enterprises requiring GDPR and ePrivacy compliance.
Commanders Act TrustCommander.summary_abuse: TrustCommander is a consent management platform (CMP) used to implement GDPR cookie consent banners and consent records. As a compliance tool with no hosting or content distribution features, it has essentially no abuse signal. There are no documented instances of TrustCommander being exploited for phishing or fraud.

Commento.description: Commento is a tool for fostering discussion and improving engagement on websites.
Commento.history: Commento was created as a privacy-respecting, open-source alternative to Disqus for blog commenting. It gained attention for its no-tracking, no-ads approach and was distributed as both a self-hosted tool and a paid cloud service. The project has largely been abandoned by its original maintainer, and the Comentario fork has emerged as the actively maintained successor. The cloud service commento.io is no longer accepting new users.
Commento.summary_abuse: Commento is a privacy-focused comment system with no notable abuse signal. Its small adoption footprint and self-hosted deployment model make it an unlikely target for abuse. The project has been abandoned and the cloud service is no longer active, so current abuse risk is negligible.

CommentSold.description: CommentSold is a platform that converts social media comments into sales by enabling automated purchase processing.
CommentSold.history: CommentSold was founded around 2017 as a live and video commerce platform enabling retailers to sell through Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and their own webstores. The company pioneered comment-based selling and has processed over $5 billion in GMV for 7,000+ small and mid-sized retailers. In 2022, CommentSold launched Videeo as an enterprise-focused live video commerce offering. Named Live Video Commerce Platform of the Year by Retail Tech Insights in 2025.
CommentSold.summary_abuse: CommentSold is a live selling platform with no major documented abuse incidents specific to the platform. Social commerce platforms can occasionally be used to sell counterfeit goods or facilitate buyer scams, but CommentSold's focus on established small retailers with storefronts and payment processing reduces this risk compared to open marketplaces.

Commerce Engine.description: Commerce Engine is a headless SaaS platform that provides API-based ecommerce infrastructure including product search, order management, and fulfillment.
Commerce Engine.history: Commerce Engine is a headless SaaS ecommerce infrastructure provider offering API-based product search, order management, and fulfillment services for growing businesses. The company operates as a relatively new entrant in the headless commerce space. No notable ownership changes or controversies documented.
Commerce Engine.summary_abuse: Commerce Engine is a headless ecommerce API platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B infrastructure service for online retailers, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or scam abuse.

Commerce Server.history: Microsoft Commerce Server originated from e-Shop software acquired by Microsoft from Pierre Omidyar in 1996. Microsoft released it as Merchant Server in 1996, later renamed Site Server Commerce Edition, then Microsoft Commerce Server 2000. The product had multiple versions through 2009. Microsoft outsourced the business to Cactus Commerce in 2007, which was acquired by Ascentium in 2011 and rebranded as Ascentium Commerce Server. In 2013, Sitecore acquired CommerceServer.net. The product is now effectively deprecated as a standalone offering.
Commerce Server.summary_abuse: Commerce Server is a legacy enterprise ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. As an on-premises enterprise product now deprecated, it has no consumer-facing surface relevant to current abuse patterns.

Commerce Vision.description: Commerce Vision is a provider of enterprise-level B2B and B2C ecommerce solutions integrated with ERP applications.
Commerce Vision.history: Commerce Vision was founded in 2001 in Brisbane, Australia, specializing in B2B ecommerce solutions for manufacturers, wholesalers, and distributors. The company also developed Opmetrix (mobile merchandising/field CRM) and Lucy (order processing automation). In March 2025, Commerce Vision was acquired by Advantive, LLC, a US-based software holding company, to strengthen its international B2B ecommerce presence.
Commerce Vision.summary_abuse: Commerce Vision is an Australian B2B ecommerce platform with no notable abuse signal. Its enterprise B2B focus with verified business accounts and ERP integration requirements limits any meaningful abuse surface.

Commerce.js.description: Commerce.js is an API-first ecommerce platform for developers and businesses.
Commerce.js.history: Commerce.js (formerly Chec) is a headless ecommerce API platform founded around 2016. It provides developer-focused APIs and SDKs for building custom storefront experiences with React, Next.js, and other frameworks. The company operates as an independent startup with a developer-centric approach to ecommerce infrastructure.
Commerce.js.summary_abuse: Commerce.js is a developer-focused headless ecommerce API with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B developer tool with standard payment processing integrations, it has no significant consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or scam abuse.

Commerce7.description: Commerce7 is an ecommerce platform for wineries.
Commerce7.history: Commerce7 was founded in 2018 in Vancouver, British Columbia by Andrew Kamphuis and Jason Andres, specifically targeting the winery direct-to-consumer market. The platform grew to serve 3,500+ wineries worldwide with ecommerce, CRM, POS, and wine club management. Commerce7's parent company Fullsteam acquired the DTC SaaS division of WineDirect, consolidating Commerce7's position in the wine ecommerce space to potentially control nearly half the wine DTC market.
Commerce7.summary_abuse: Commerce7 is a niche ecommerce platform for wineries with no notable abuse signal. The platform processes wine sales for verified wineries with built-in age verification and compliance requirements, making it an unlikely target for fraudulent storefronts.

CommerceHQ.description: CommerceHQ is a high-conversion ecommerce store system engineered for online retail operations.
CommerceHQ.history: CommerceHQ was founded around 2015 in Canada as an all-in-one ecommerce platform with drag-and-drop store building. The platform remained bootstrapped with no external funding. In September 2025, CommerceHQ was acquired by NameSilo, a domain registrar. The platform has seen significant decline in adoption, with store count dropping 61% year-over-year in 2025.
CommerceHQ.summary_abuse: CommerceHQ is a general-purpose ecommerce platform with some documented use for dropshipping and potentially low-quality storefronts. Its easy store creation and declining active monitoring following acquisition may modestly increase risk of fraudulent or low-quality merchant storefronts compared to larger platforms with stricter merchant vetting.

Commercelayer.description: Commercelayer is a headless ecommerce platform that permits businesses to create customisable and scalable online shopping experiences via an API-first architecture that allows developers to use any programming language or framework for building ecommerce sites and applications.
Commercelayer.history: Commercelayer was founded in 2017 near Florence, Italy by Filippo Conforti. The company raised a $6M Series A led by Benchmark Capital in 2020 and a $16M Series B led by Coatue in 2021, totaling $22M. Customers include Chilly's, Brioni, SumUp, Paradox Interactive, and Coca-Cola Embonor. The platform is a leading headless commerce API and order management system enabling multi-currency, multi-market selling.
Commercelayer.summary_abuse: Commercelayer is a headless commerce API platform with no notable abuse signal. As a developer-focused B2B infrastructure tool for enterprise merchants, it has strong merchant vetting and no anonymous public-facing consumer surface that would attract phishing or fraud.

commercetools.description: commercetools is a headless commerce platform.
commercetools.history: commercetools was founded in 2006 by Dirk Hoerig and Denis Werner in Munich, Germany. It pioneered the headless commerce model. In 2014, it was acquired by REWE Digital. In 2019, it was spun out following a $145 million investment from Insight Partners. In 2021, it raised a $140 million Series C at a $1.9B valuation from Accel. In February 2025, it laid off dozens of staff amid market changes.
commercetools.summary_abuse: commercetools is an enterprise headless commerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves large retailers and brands with enterprise contracts and rigorous onboarding, making it an unlikely vector for fraud.

Commercio.description: Commercio is a platform that allows promotional product distributors to set up fully branded company stores.
Commercio.history: Commercio.network is an Italian blockchain platform launched in 2019 by Commerc.io s.r.l. from Schio, Italy. It is designed specifically for B2B document exchange, combining EDI with blockchain for document encryption, signing, and non-repudiation using Tendermint Proof-of-Stake consensus. The network is a federated blockchain with over 100 validator nodes coordinated by the Commercio Consortium.
Commercio.summary_abuse: Commercio.network is a permissioned blockchain for B2B document exchange with no notable abuse signal. Its consortium-based validator model and enterprise B2B focus significantly limit anonymous abuse. No documented incidents of exploitation.

Common Ground.description: Common Ground is an online solution for independent record sellers.
Common Ground.history: Common Ground (common-ground.io) is an online platform for independent record sellers to manage and sell vinyl and music records. It provides marketplace tools and catalog management for music collectors and small independent shops. No notable ownership changes or controversies documented.
Common Ground.summary_abuse: Common Ground is a niche online marketplace for independent record sellers with no notable documented abuse signal. As a specialized marketplace for music collectors, the primary concern would be counterfeit or misrepresented items, but the platform serves a small, specialized community.

Comms.description: Comms is an all-in-one ecommerce platform.
Comms.history: Comms is a small all-in-one e-commerce platform. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Comms.summary_abuse: Comms is a small e-commerce platform with no documented abuse incidents.

Community.description: Community is a platform enabling public figures, small to midsize businesses, and enterprise brands to connect instantly with their audience through large-scale conversational text messaging.
Community.history: Community (community.com) was founded as a pivot from Shimmur, a mobile social app that went through Techstars in 2017. It launched in July 2019 as a two-way SMS messaging platform for celebrities, brands, and public figures to connect with fans. Co-founded by Josh Rosenheck, Ashton Kutcher, and Guy Oseary, the platform gained early celebrity adoption and raised nearly $90M from investors including Salesforce Ventures, Morgan Stanley, HubSpot, and Verizon Ventures.
Community.summary_abuse: Community.com is an SMS messaging platform for celebrities and brands that handles direct consumer phone numbers. The collection of fan phone numbers at scale creates a meaningful privacy surface. Mass SMS platforms can be used for spam if compromised, though Community focuses on opted-in audiences. The aggregation of phone numbers for high-profile accounts creates a target for social engineering attacks.

Community Box.description: Community Box is a tool that allows users to create directories within a website without writing any code.
Community Box.history: Community Box was launched in 2018 as a no-code directory builder tool that integrates with website builders, particularly Squarespace. It allows users to create searchable member or business directories without coding. The product is a small independent SaaS with no notable ownership changes.
Community Box.summary_abuse: Community Box is a simple no-code directory builder with no notable abuse signal. Its niche use case for member directories has minimal abuse surface.

Community Funded.description: Community Funded is a digital fundraising and engagement platform.
Community Funded.history: Community Funded is a digital fundraising and community engagement platform targeting nonprofits, universities, and community organizations for crowdfunding campaigns. The platform serves the community fundraising niche with tools for peer-to-peer fundraising and donor engagement. No notable ownership changes documented.
Community Funded.summary_abuse: Community Funded is a nonprofit and community fundraising platform with low documented abuse risk. Crowdfunding platforms can be used for fraudulent campaigns, but Community Funded targets verified nonprofits and institutions which significantly reduces anonymous fraud risk.

Complianz.description: Complianz is a GDPR/CCPA Cookie Consent plugin that supports GDPR, DSGVO, CCPA and PIPEDA with a conditional Cookie Notice and customized Cookie Policy based on the results of the built-in Cookie Scan.
Complianz.history: Complianz is a GDPR/CCPA WordPress plugin developed by Complianz BV in Groningen, Netherlands, under the Really Simple Security brand. Launched around 2018, the plugin has grown to serve over 1 million WordPress websites with cookie consent management and privacy policy generation. It supports GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, POPIA and other privacy regulations. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Complianz.summary_abuse: Complianz is a WordPress privacy compliance plugin with no notable abuse signal. As a compliance tool helping websites implement cookie consent banners, it has no content hosting or distribution surface that would attract abuse.

ComplyAuto.description: ComplyAuto is a dealership compliance automation platform that helps automotive businesses manage regulatory requirements, track documentation, and maintain adherence to industry standards through automated processes.
ComplyAuto.history: ComplyAuto was founded as an automotive dealership compliance platform, built by and for dealers. It became the NADA affinity partner for compliance solutions and is endorsed by 42 out of 50 US state dealer associations. The platform serves over 10,000 dealerships with regulatory compliance covering GLBA, FTC Safeguards Rule, UDAP, OSHA, and state privacy laws.
ComplyAuto.summary_abuse: ComplyAuto is an automotive dealership compliance software platform with no notable abuse signal. As a specialized B2B compliance tool for licensed auto dealerships, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud.

Composite Products.description: Composite Products is a service that provides customizable product kits allowing selection and assembly of components to suit specific needs.
Composite Products.history: Composite Products is a WooCommerce extension plugin developed by Automattic (owner of WooCommerce/WordPress.com). It allows store owners to create customizable product kit builders where customers select and assemble components. The plugin is maintained alongside the broader WooCommerce ecosystem under Automattic ownership.
Composite Products.summary_abuse: Composite Products is a WooCommerce plugin with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted ecommerce extension, any abuse would manifest at the WooCommerce store level rather than through the plugin itself.

comScore.description: comScore is an American media measurement and analytics company providing marketing data and analytics to enterprises; media and advertising agencies; and publishers.
comScore.history: comScore was founded in 1999 by Gian Fulgoni and Magid Abraham and went public in 2007. It became a leading internet and media measurement company. In 2016, a major accounting scandal led to financial restatements and SEC investigations. The company later merged with Rentrak in 2016 to expand its TV and movie measurement capabilities.
comScore.summary_abuse: comScore is a media analytics company that embeds tracking scripts on publishers' websites to measure audience. Its tracking pixels collect browsing data across millions of sites, which is the nature of its business model. It complies with industry privacy frameworks but is inherently an extensive tracker by design.

Concert.description: Concert is a publisher-led marketplace for ads by Vox Media.
Concert.history: Concert is a publisher-led programmatic advertising marketplace built and owned by Vox Media. It was created to enable premium publishers to sell targeted advertising inventory to brands. As part of Vox Media's advertising business, Concert operates as an ad-tech marketplace connecting publishers with advertisers in a brand-safe environment.
Concert.summary_abuse: Concert is a publisher ad marketplace operated by Vox Media. As an ad-tech platform with curated publisher inventory, it operates with editorial oversight typical of premium media companies. The advertising nature of the platform means user tracking is inherent, but the premium publisher model reduces the risk of malvertising compared to open programmatic exchanges.

Concrete CMS.history: Concrete CMS development began in 2003 for the Lewis & Clark Bicentennial website, built by Franz Maruna and Andy Holzschuh at PortlandLabs. The platform was released as open source (originally named concrete5) in June 2008 and was SourceForge project of the month in October 2008. It was later rebranded to Concrete CMS, dropping the numeral. As of 2021, over 62,000 live websites use Concrete CMS, with 258+ GitHub contributors. PortlandLabs continues to lead development.
Concrete CMS.summary_abuse: Concrete CMS is an open-source content management system with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted CMS, any abuse would manifest at the individual deployment level. Like all CMS platforms, outdated installations can be targeted for exploitation if not patched, but the platform itself has no centralized abuse surface.

Conditional Fields for Contact Form 7.description: Adds conditional logic to Contact Form 7.
Conditional Fields for Contact Form 7.history: Conditional Fields for Contact Form 7 is a WordPress plugin that adds conditional show/hide logic to Contact Form 7 forms. It is developed by bdwm.be, a Belgian developer. The plugin has a pro version with advanced features including repeatable fields, multi-step forms, and calculated fields. No notable ownership changes.
Conditional Fields for Contact Form 7.summary_abuse: Conditional Fields for Contact Form 7 is a WordPress form enhancement plugin with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted plugin that adds form logic, it has no centralized service surface for abuse.

Conduit.description: Conduit is a data integration tool designed to synchronize production systems through an extensible, event-first approach with minimal dependencies for integration into existing workflows.
Conduit.history: Conduit Ltd. was founded in 2005 in Israel and became infamous for its browser toolbar product that was widely classified as adware and malware. The toolbar would hijack browser settings, change homepages, and install itself via bundled software without clear consent. In 2013, Conduit spun off its toolbar division into Perion Network via reverse merger and pivoted to mobile app development. At peak, over 100 million Conduit toolbars were active monthly. The toolbar product is now deprecated.
Conduit.summary_abuse: The Conduit toolbar was widely classified as adware and malware by security researchers and antivirus vendors. It was distributed via software bundling, often installed without clear user consent, and would hijack browser homepages and default search settings. The toolbar collected user data for advertising purposes. Security vendors including Avast and Fortiguard flagged it as potentially unwanted software. The toolbar product has since been deprecated following Conduit's 2013 corporate restructuring.

Conduze.description: Conduze is a call tracking and pop-up solution designed to help businesses capture and analyze inbound leads.
Conduze.history: Conduze is a Danish consumer intelligence and lead generation platform founded around 2010 in Risskov, Denmark. It provides call tracking, pop-up form lead capture, and marketing analytics tools. The company operates as a small independent SaaS with no notable ownership changes.
Conduze.summary_abuse: Conduze is a small Danish call tracking and lead generation platform with no notable abuse signal. Its niche B2B marketing analytics focus limits any meaningful abuse surface.

Conekta.description: Conekta is a Mexican payment platform.
Conekta.history: Conekta was founded in 2012 by Hector Cardenas, Leo Fischer, and Cristina Randall, engineering graduates from the University of Waterloo. The company pivoted from an online business directory to become Mexico's leading payment gateway, processing credit cards, cash (OXXO), and bank transfers for businesses. Customers include Google, Uber, and Netflix for their Mexican market operations. The company raised over $21M from investors including FEMSA, Propel Venture Partners, and 500 Global.
Conekta.summary_abuse: Conekta is a legitimate Mexican payment gateway used by major companies including Google and Netflix. As a regulated financial technology company processing payments in Mexico, it has standard fraud prevention and KYC controls. No significant documented abuse incidents specific to the platform.

Conexa.description: Conexa is a business scaling technology partner platform designed to support growth through strategic solutions and automation.
Conexa.history: Conexa Saude was founded in 2017 in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil as a digital health and telemedicine platform. The company democratizes access to healthcare in Brazil through virtual consultations, electronic medical records, and digital prescriptions. It raised $50.9M over 4 rounds, with investors including General Atlantic, Goldman Sachs, and e.Bricks Ventures. A Telefonica fund also backed the company.
Conexa.summary_abuse: Conexa is a Brazilian telemedicine platform with no notable abuse signal. As a regulated digital health service, it handles sensitive medical records and patient data under Brazilian healthcare regulations (LGPD). No documented phishing or fraud incidents specific to the platform.

Confer With.description: Confer With triggers live streaming video calls between shoppers and instore experts from a website, or outside a store.
Confer With.history: Confer With is a UK-based live video shopping platform that enables retailers to provide one-to-one video advice with direct checkout integration. Named a Gartner 2024 Cool Vendor in Digital Commerce, it focuses on high-value retail categories such as electronics, furniture, and luxury. The company operates as an independent startup.
Confer With.summary_abuse: Confer With is a live video shopping platform for retailers with no notable abuse signal. The platform facilitates direct retailer-customer video interactions for commerce and has no public consumer-facing account registration that would attract scam abuse.

Conferbot.description: Conferbot is a platform designed to improve website engagement through customisable chatbots, catering to various business requirements.
Conferbot.history: Conferbot was founded in 2019 in Bengaluru, India by Deepak Kumar as a no-code AI chatbot platform. The platform serves over 50,000 businesses with lead generation and customer support chatbots deployable across WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, Telegram, and websites. It integrates with OpenAI for AI capabilities.
Conferbot.summary_abuse: Conferbot is a chatbot platform with a moderate abuse risk profile. No-code chatbot platforms can be used to create phishing bots or social engineering tools, though legitimate use clearly dominates. The free tier with multi-channel deployment (WhatsApp, Messenger) could be exploited for spam. No specific documented incidents of abuse were found.

Confiant.description: Confiant is a cybersecurity company specialising in ad security and ad quality assurance for digital publishers, programmatic platforms, and advertisers.
Confiant.history: Confiant was founded in September 2013 by Louis-David Mangin and Jerome Dangu to combat malvertising and low-quality digital advertising. In 2017, they launched the industry's first real-time ad verification and blocking solution. They have published numerous security research reports linking malvertising campaigns to specific actors, including a 2023 investigation linking ScamClub to WayTop International Advertising in Hong Kong. Partners include MSN, eBay, Magnite, and Gannett.
Confiant.summary_abuse: Confiant is an ad security platform that actively fights malvertising and has no abuse signal of its own. The company publishes security research exposing malicious advertising actors and provides real-time blocking technology to publishers. It is on the defensive side of the abuse equation.

Congressus.description: Congressus is a Dutch-language online application for member administration, financial management, communication and a linked website with webshop.
Congressus.history: Congressus is a Dutch SaaS platform for association and membership management, focused on the Netherlands market. It provides CRM, CMS, financial administration, event management, and communication tools for small and large Dutch associations. The platform integrates with iDeal via Mollie for Dutch online payments. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Congressus.summary_abuse: Congressus is a Dutch association management SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves verified associations with membership databases and financial tools. The B2B nature and Dutch regulatory context limit any meaningful abuse surface.

Conjured.description: Conjured provides Shopify brands with Shopify apps and custom development.
Conjured.history: Conjured Referrals is a Shopify app for referral marketing originally developed as an independent product and later acquired by Shop Circle, a Shopify app holding company. The app helps Shopify merchants create referral programs with automated rewards. It includes built-in anti-fraud features to prevent self-referral abuse.
Conjured.summary_abuse: Conjured is a Shopify referral program app with built-in anti-fraud measures including email and IP banning for referral abuse. The platform is designed to prevent self-referral fraud. As a merchant-facing Shopify app, its direct consumer abuse surface is limited.

Connectally.description: Connectally is a lead generation and automation platform designed to help marketing professionals streamline client acquisition.
Connectally.history: Connectally is a lead generation and marketing automation platform. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Connectally.summary_abuse: Connectally is a lead generation and automation tool with no documented abuse incidents. Marketing automation in this category carries spam risk but no specific reports exist.

Connectif.description: Connectif is a marketing automation and personalisation data-first action platform, powered by AI.
Connectif.history: Connectif was founded in 2018 in Murcia, Spain as an AI-powered marketing automation and Customer Data Platform for ecommerce. It is recognized as the leading marketing automation platform in Spain, serving over 1,271 businesses including PcComponentes and MiFarma. The company has offices in Spain and Latin America.
Connectif.summary_abuse: Connectif is a marketing automation CDP platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B tool for ecommerce marketing, its primary privacy concern is behavioral tracking of end consumers for marketing personalization, which is typical for marketing automation platforms operating under GDPR.

Conneqto.description: Conneqto is an all-in-one platform offering email, automation, high-converting carts, and sales pipeline tools for streamlined marketing and sales management.
Conneqto.history: Conneqto is an AI-powered marketing automation platform with email, cart, and pipeline tools. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Conneqto.summary_abuse: Conneqto is a small marketing automation SaaS with no documented abuse incidents. Email automation tools carry spam risk but no specific reports exist for this vendor.

Connexity.description: Connexity is a platform that focuses on audience targeting, data management, optimization, attribution, and insight generation for enhanced marketing strategies.
Connexity.history: Connexity traces its roots to Bizrate.com, founded in 1996. The company rebranded to Shopzilla in 2004, then to Connexity in 2014. Through acquisitions, the company absorbed Become.com (2014), PriceGrabber (2015), and the affiliate network Skimlinks. These acquisitions created one of the largest retail marketing networks with 50 million+ monthly users across price comparison and affiliate sites including Bizrate, Shopzilla, and Skimlinks.
Connexity.summary_abuse: Connexity operates retail marketing and affiliate networks including Skimlinks and Shopzilla. As an affiliate marketing and price comparison network, it handles significant consumer behavioral tracking. Affiliate networks have documented histories of link stuffing, cookie dropping, and affiliate fraud, though Connexity as an established player has standard compliance measures in place.

Consent Manager.description: Consent Manager is a provider ensuring GDPR and CCPA compliance for websites.
Consent Manager.history: Consent Manager (consentmanager.net) is a German consent management platform (CMP) for GDPR compliance, supporting over 30 languages with customizable cookie consent banners. It is an IAB TCF-certified CMP. The platform is operated as an independent German SaaS company. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Consent Manager.summary_abuse: Consent Manager is a GDPR cookie consent platform with no notable abuse signal. As a compliance tool, its purpose is to help websites respect user privacy. There are no documented instances of the platform being exploited for malicious purposes.

Constant Contact.description: Constant Contact is a marketing automation and email marketing solution.
Constant Contact.history: Constant Contact was founded in 1995 by Randy Parker as Roving Software in Waltham, Massachusetts. The company launched email marketing tools in 2000 and rebranded to Constant Contact in 2004. It went public on NASDAQ in October 2007 raising $107M. Acquisitions included e2M Systems (2008), NutshellMail (2010), CardStar and SinglePlatform (2012). In 2015, the company was sold to Endurance International Group, then acquired by Clearlake Capital in 2021.
Constant Contact.summary_abuse: Constant Contact is a major email marketing platform with documented use for spam campaigns. As a large email marketing service with millions of small business users, it is occasionally exploited for spam, phishing campaigns disguised as legitimate marketing, and unsolicited bulk email. The platform has anti-spam policies and abuse detection, but email marketing platforms at scale invariably see some misuse. Constant Contact has been listed in spam databases but actively enforces its terms of service.

Construct 3.description: Construct 3 is a browser-based game development platform that enables the creation of 2D games using a visual, drag-and-drop interface without requiring programming knowledge.
Construct 3.history: Construct is an HTML5 2D game engine developed by Scirra Ltd. in the UK. The original Construct was released in 2007 as a free GPL-licensed DirectX 9 engine. Construct 2 launched in 2011 as a commercial product with HTML5 runtime. Construct 3 launched in public beta in March 2017 as a fully browser-based editor and runtime, changing the pricing from one-time to subscription. Scirra remains an independent company.
Construct 3.summary_abuse: Construct 3 is a browser-based 2D game development tool with no notable abuse signal. As a game creation platform, games made with it are self-hosted by developers. There are no documented incidents of the platform being exploited for malicious purposes.

Constructor.description: Constructor is an ecommerce tool optimizing site search and product discovery, leveraging AI, NLP, data, and personalization to enhance user experiences and meet KPIs.
Constructor.history: Constructor (constructor.com) was founded in 2015 by Eli Finkelshteyn and Dan McCormick. The company builds AI-powered product discovery and search solutions for ecommerce retailers, using machine learning and collaborative personalization. Enterprise customers include Sephora, Petco, Bonobos, Target Australia, and Birkenstock. Constructor is recognized as a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Search and Product Discovery in 2025.
Constructor.summary_abuse: Constructor is an enterprise ecommerce search and product discovery platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B infrastructure tool serving verified enterprise retailers, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud.

Constructor.io.description: Constructor.io is a site search and merchandising platform designed to help ecommerce.
Constructor.io.history: Constructor.io is the same product as constructor.com - an AI-powered ecommerce search and product discovery platform founded in 2015 by Eli Finkelshteyn and Dan McCormick. The company serves enterprise retailers including Sephora, Petco, Bonobos, and Birkenstock. It is a Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for Search and Product Discovery 2025.
Constructor.io.summary_abuse: Constructor.io is an enterprise ecommerce search and product discovery platform with no notable abuse signal. As a B2B infrastructure tool for verified enterprise retailers, it has no consumer-facing surface that would attract phishing or fraud.

Contabo.description: Contabo is a German hosting provider, previously known by the name Giga-International.
Contabo.history: Contabo was founded in 2003 in Munich, Germany as Giga-International, offering web hosting and dedicated servers. The company rebranded to Contabo in 2013 and expanded internationally. It launched VPS services in 2010 which became its core product. In 2014 it opened a second German data center in Nuremberg. In 2020 it expanded to the US. By 2023, Contabo manages over 400,000 servers in 11 locations across 4 continents with ~300 employees.
Contabo.summary_abuse: Contabo is a German budget VPS hosting provider with a notable abuse profile common to affordable unmanaged hosting. Contabo's low pricing and permissive initial KYC make it attractive for hosting malware, phishing pages, spam bots, and other abusive content. Security researchers have documented Contabo IP ranges being used for botnet C2 infrastructure. While Contabo has abuse reporting mechanisms and German legal obligations, its response speed is considered slower than premium providers.

Contact Form 7.description: Contact Form 7 is an WordPress plugin which can manage multiple contact forms. The form supports Ajax-powered submitting, CAPTCHA, Akismet spam filtering.
Contact Form 7.history: Contact Form 7 was first developed in 2009 by Japanese developer Takayuki Miyoshi and is maintained by Rock Lobster Inc. It has become the most popular WordPress contact form plugin with over 5 million active installations. The plugin is free and open source under GPL and has been consistently maintained with regular updates for over 15 years.
Contact Form 7.summary_abuse: Contact Form 7 is one of the most widely-used WordPress plugins and is frequently targeted by spam bots submitting form data. The plugin itself has had several documented security vulnerabilities over the years, some allowing file upload exploitation or cross-site scripting. Spam and phishing form submissions are common in sites using unprotected Contact Form 7 instances. The developer regularly releases security patches and recommends reCAPTCHA integration.

ContactUs.description: ContactUs is a custom ecommerce development and online marketing platform offering customer acquisition and prospect management solutions.
ContactUs.history: ContactUs is an e-commerce development and digital marketing platform offering customer acquisition tools. No notable ownership changes are documented.
ContactUs.summary_abuse: ContactUs is a small marketing and customer acquisition SaaS with no documented abuse incidents.

Container Media Group.description: Container Media Group offers technical solutions for agencies and brands to plan, execute, optimise, and report on advertising campaigns. They specialise in creating engaging custom creatives and utilise advanced tracking pixels to monitor user advertising activity across media channels.
Container Media Group.history: Container Media Group is a UK-based adtech company providing custom ad creatives and advanced tracking pixels for agencies and brands. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Container Media Group.summary_abuse: Container Media Group operates advanced advertising tracking pixels across third-party websites. Adtech companies with cross-site tracking have elevated privacy risk and can be vectors for ad fraud. No specific large-scale abuse incidents are documented but the nature of cross-site pixel tracking is inherently privacy-invasive.

Contao.description: Contao is an open source CMS that allows you to create websites and scalable web applications.
Contao.history: Contao is an open-source CMS first released in 2006 by Leo Feyer under the name TYPOLight. In 2010, it was renamed Contao to avoid confusion with TYPO3. Licensed under LGPL-3.0, it is one of the top 5 CMS platforms in the German-speaking (DACH) region, popular with German-speaking agencies. It remains community-maintained with Leo Feyer as the lead developer.
Contao.summary_abuse: Contao is a self-hosted open-source CMS with no notable abuse signal. As a download-and-install CMS with no centralized hosting, any abuse would manifest at individual deployment level. It is primarily used in the DACH region for business websites.

Contenido.description: Contenido is a robust content management system (CMS) tailored for efficient creation and administration of digital content across websites and online projects.
Contenido.history: Contenido CMS is a German open source web content management system developed by 4fb GmbH in Offenbach, Germany, in operation since 2001. It is described as one of the leading open source CMS solutions in the DACH (Germany, Austria, Switzerland) region and has been deployed thousands of times with national and international SMEs. The project has maintained a stable community-driven development model for over two decades.
Contenido.summary_abuse: Contenido CMS is a legitimate open source CMS primarily used in the DACH region with no known abuse patterns or malicious functionality. It is self-hosted, has been actively maintained since 2001, and poses minimal privacy or security risk by design.

Contensis.history: Contensis is a headless CMS developed by Zengenti, a company founded in 2001 in Ludlow, United Kingdom. It is primarily used by UK higher education institutions and public sector organisations. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Contensis.summary_abuse: Contensis is an enterprise headless CMS used by universities and public sector bodies in the UK with no notable abuse signal.

ContentBot.description: ContentBot is an AI-driven platform for creating custom content workflows, processing imports, and generating blog posts with an AI blog writer.
ContentBot.history: ContentBot was founded in 2021 by Nick Duncan in Miami, Florida as an AI-powered content creation and workflow automation platform.
ContentBot.summary_abuse: ContentBot is an AI content generation SaaS with no documented abuse incidents. AI writing tools can be misused to generate spam or SEO manipulation content but no specific reports exist for ContentBot.

ContentBox.history: ContentBox is an open source modular CMS built on the ColdBox MVC framework and developed by Ortus Solutions, Corp since 2012. Licensed under the Apache 2 License, it was committed as open source and free forever. It targets developers building websites, blogs, wikis, and complex web applications on ColdFusion (CFML) and Java. Ortus Solutions has maintained the project through multiple major versions including v5.x.
ContentBox.summary_abuse: ContentBox is a clean open source CMS maintained by Ortus Solutions with no known abuse patterns. As a self-hosted ColdFusion/CFML CMS with an Apache 2 license, it poses minimal privacy risk and has no tracker or dark pattern concerns.

Contentder.description: Contentder is a website development platform that integrates inbound marketing analytics to optimize site performance.
Contentder.history: Contentder is a website development and inbound marketing analytics platform. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Contentder.summary_abuse: Contentder is a small website development SaaS with no documented abuse incidents.

Contentful.description: Contentful is an API-first content management platform to create, manage and publish content on any digital channel.
Contentful.history: Contentful was founded in 2013 in Berlin, Germany by Sascha Konietzke and Paolo Negri, initially conceived in 2011 as a CMS for mobile apps. The company pioneered the headless CMS space, raising $800K in seed funding in 2013, and grew rapidly with Series D-F funding including a $175M Series F in July 2021 at a $3B valuation. The company moved its headquarters to San Francisco. Notable clients include IKEA, Spotify, Red Bull, Twilio, and Kraft Heinz.
Contentful.summary_abuse: Contentful is a reputable, well-funded enterprise headless CMS with strong security certifications (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR) and a clear responsible disclosure policy. No abuse patterns or dark patterns documented. It is a mainstream platform widely adopted by large enterprises.

ContentGems.description: ContentGems is a no-code content discovery tool designed to help users find and curate relevant content.
ContentGems.history: ContentGems is a no-code content discovery and curation tool. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
ContentGems.summary_abuse: ContentGems is a content curation tool with no notable abuse signal.

Contently.description: Contently is a SaaS content marketing platform from the company of the same name headquartered in New York.
Contently.history: Contently was founded in 2011 by Joe Coleman, Dave Goldberg, and Shane Snow in New York City. The enterprise content marketing platform raised $19.2M from investors including ff Venture Capital, Techstars, and Lightbank. By 2017 Contently had reached $20M ARR with 300 enterprise clients, growing to $53.8M in revenue by 2024. It serves regulated industries with an end-to-end CMP combining technology, content strategy, and a freelance creator marketplace.
Contently.summary_abuse: Contently is a legitimate enterprise content marketing platform with no abuse incidents. It focuses on regulated industries and brand content, positioning itself as privacy-respecting. Moderate tracker density consistent with SaaS marketing platforms.

Contento.description: Contento is a headless CMS tailored to prioritize the needs of marketers.
Contento.history: Contento was co-founded in 2022 in Ireland by Josh Angell (CEO/tech lead) and Alan Gleeson (CMO), with seed investment from Peter Coppinger, co-founder of Teamwork. The founders built Contento after being unable to find a headless CMS tailored to B2B SaaS marketing websites. The platform focuses on giving marketing teams autonomy without sacrificing developer needs.
Contento.summary_abuse: Contento is a new, clean headless CMS startup from Ireland with no abuse or dark pattern concerns. Seed-funded and focused on B2B SaaS websites, it has a minimal footprint and no privacy concerns documented.

Contents.description: Contents is a generative AI platform that enables businesses to produce high-performing, original, and SEO-optimized content.
Contents.history: Contents is a generative AI platform for producing SEO-optimized marketing content, likely founded around 2018-2020 in Italy. No notable ownership changes are documented.
Contents.summary_abuse: Contents is an AI content generation SaaS with no notable abuse signal. It serves marketing teams with no consumer-facing anonymous hosting features.

Contentsquare.description: Contentsquare is an enterprise-level UX optimisation platform.
Contentsquare.history: Contentsquare was founded in 2012 in Paris, France by Jonathan Cherki while attending ESSEC Business School. It started as a consultancy and pivoted to SaaS in 2016. The company grew to become a unicorn, raising over $1.4 billion including a $500M Series E led by SoftBank Vision Fund 2 and a $400M Series F in 2022 at a $5.6B valuation. The platform provides digital experience analytics to large enterprises and has made 8 significant acquisitions.
Contentsquare.summary_abuse: Contentsquare is a well-funded enterprise digital experience analytics company with strong security certifications (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR). The product captures user behavior data for analytics, which involves session recording and behavioral tracking. No abuse incidents documented; its privacy practices are enterprise-grade but inherently data-intensive.

Contentstack.description: Contentstack is a headless CMS software designed to help businesses deliver personalised content experiences to audiences via multiple channels.
Contentstack.history: Contentstack was founded in 2018 by Neha Sampat, Nishant Patel, and Matthew Baier, emerging from their previous company Built.io (acquired by Software AG) and Raw Engineering, a 15-year-old consulting firm. The company raised $31.5M Series A in 2019 (Insight Partners), $57.5M Series B in 2021, and $80M Series C in 2022. Clients include Walmart and Shell, with 4x revenue growth in H1 2019.
Contentstack.summary_abuse: Contentstack is a reputable enterprise headless CMS with strong security posture (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR). No abuse or dark pattern incidents documented. It serves large enterprise customers including Walmart and Shell with enterprise-grade security.

ContentStudio.description: ContentStudio is an integrated cloud-based social media management and content marketing solution.
ContentStudio.history: ContentStudio was founded in 2016 by Muhammad Azhar and Waqar Azeem in North Kansas City, Missouri. The bootstrapped social media management and content marketing SaaS grew to approximately $2.9M in annual revenue. It provides an integrated cloud-based platform for content planning, publishing, social media scheduling, and analytics. The company expanded operations with teams in the US and Pakistan.
ContentStudio.summary_abuse: ContentStudio is a legitimate social media management and content marketing SaaS with no known abuse incidents. It is bootstrapped and independently owned, with moderate tracker density typical of marketing tools. No significant dark pattern or privacy concerns have been documented.

ContentViews.description: ContentViews is a lightweight and user-friendly WordPress Plugin, crafted specifically for efficient content management and display. This plugin offers seamless integration with Widgets and enables users to showcase their posts, pages, or custom post types in a highly responsive grid, list, or timeline layout.
ContentViews.history: Content Views is a WordPress plugin for displaying posts in grid, list, and timeline layouts using shortcodes, Gutenberg blocks, and Elementor widgets. It was developed by the contributor known as 'pt-guy' and has been available since at least WordPress 3.3. The plugin has evolved significantly over the years with a professional version (Content Views Pro) available at contentviewspro.com for advanced filtering and grid functionality.
ContentViews.summary_abuse: Content Views is a legitimate WordPress plugin with no known abuse patterns or malicious functionality. It is open source (GPL), actively maintained, and distributed through the official WordPress plugin repository with no tracking or dark pattern concerns.

ContextBar.description: ContextBar is a tool designed to attract consistent, thematically relevant visitors to a website.
ContextBar.history: ContextBar is a Russian web traffic and SEO tool designed to attract targeted visitors. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
ContextBar.summary_abuse: ContextBar is a Russian SEO and traffic tool with no documented specific abuse incidents, though tools designed to artificially drive web traffic can be used for click fraud or SEO spam.

Contextual Related Posts.description: Add related posts to your WordPress site with inbuilt caching.
Contextual Related Posts.history: Contextual Related Posts is a WordPress plugin first released in January 2009, created and maintained by Ajay D'Souza via WebberZone. It shows relevant related posts based on content analysis. Over 16 years of active development, the plugin has gained a strong reputation for quality and responsive support.
Contextual Related Posts.summary_abuse: Contextual Related Posts is a clean, long-running WordPress plugin distributed through the official repository. Open source (GPL), no tracking or dark patterns. No abuse concerns documented in over 16 years of operation.

Contlo.description: Contlo is an AI powered marketing software.
Contlo.history: Contlo was founded in 2021 by Mukunda N S and Ishaan Bhola in Newark, US. The AI-powered customer data and marketing platform targets eCommerce and D2C brands. It raised $6.79M from Kae Capital and Better Capital, growing revenue from $1.1M (2022) to $7.1M (2024) with a 71-person team. The platform has evolved toward an AI super-app branding as SuperAGI Marketing.
Contlo.summary_abuse: Contlo is a legitimate AI marketing automation platform for eCommerce brands with no documented abuse. It handles customer data for segmentation and personalization, which involves collecting behavioral data. No dark patterns or significant privacy incidents documented.

Control.description: Control is a freehand website builder that allows users to create beautiful fully-custom responsive sites.
Control.history: Control (cntrl.site) is a freehand website builder allowing fully custom responsive sites. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Control.summary_abuse: Control is a website builder with no documented abuse incidents. Website builders with free tiers can host scam pages but Control is a minor platform with no specific abuse reports.

Convead.description: Convead is a CRM marketing platform for ecommerce stores that helps collect data, segment customers, and automate marketing efforts.
Convead.history: Convead is a Russian CRM and marketing automation platform for e-commerce stores. No public founding year or notable ownership changes are documented.
Convead.summary_abuse: Convead is a CRM and email marketing platform for e-commerce with no documented abuse incidents. Email marketing tools can be misused for spam but no specific reports exist for Convead.

Converdiant.description: Converdiant is a conversion optimization system that analyzes user behavior and website performance data to improve engagement, streamline customer journeys, and increase conversion rates through data-driven insights.
Converdiant.history: Converdiant is a conversion optimization SaaS. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Converdiant.summary_abuse: Converdiant is a small conversion optimization tool with no documented abuse incidents.

Converge.description: Converge is an all-in-one marketing measurement platform that consolidates performance data to provide insights across multiple channels.
Converge.history: Converge was founded in 2022 by Jerome Billiet, Thomas Holvoet, and Jan-Henrik Lambrechts in Belgium. The company is part of Y Combinator's S23 cohort and raised $5.6M from Uncorrelated Ventures and YC. It builds a marketing attribution and conversion tracking platform specifically for eCommerce, processing conversions server-side to achieve 100% tracking accuracy and forwarding data to ad platforms.
Converge.summary_abuse: Converge is a clean, YC-backed marketing attribution startup with no abuse incidents. It is a server-side tracking solution that improves data accuracy for eCommerce businesses. No dark patterns or privacy concerns documented; GDPR-compliant as an EU company.

Convermax.description: Convermax is a search solution that enhances the search experience for ecommerce website visitors by providing more accurate and relevant results.
Convermax.history: Convermax is an e-commerce site search solution providing relevant search results for online stores. No public founding year or notable ownership changes are documented.
Convermax.summary_abuse: Convermax is an e-commerce site search tool with no notable abuse signal.

Conversant Consent Tool.description: Conversant Consent Tool is a free tool to gain GDPR and ePD compliant consent for digital advertising.
Conversant Consent Tool.history: Conversant (formerly ValueClick) was acquired by Alliance Data Systems for $2.3B in December 2014 and subsequently became part of Epsilon. In 2019, Publicis Groupe acquired Epsilon for $4.4B. The Conversant Consent Tool is a free GDPR/ePD compliance tool offered by Conversant Media EU for publishers and advertisers to collect legally compliant consent for digital advertising activities.
Conversant Consent Tool.summary_abuse: The Conversant Consent Tool is offered by Conversant, an ad-tech company that is part of Epsilon and Publicis Groupe. While the consent tool itself is designed for GDPR compliance, Conversant's core business involves extensive behavioral data collection and targeted advertising. The tool may be used to facilitate consent for Conversant's own ad tracking infrastructure, representing a conflict of interest. Found on ~8,836 websites.

Conversio.description: Conversio is an optimisation and analytics agency.
Conversio.history: Conversio was founded in 2014 by Adii Pienaar (co-founder of WooThemes) in South Africa, originally focused on helping eCommerce brands monetize transactional emails like receipts. By 2019 the company had $2M ARR and 14 employees and was acquired by Campaign Monitor. It was rebranded as CM Commerce and later joined Marigold, Campaign Monitor's parent organization.
Conversio.summary_abuse: Conversio (now CM Commerce) is a legitimate eCommerce email marketing platform with no documented abuse. Founded by a credible entrepreneur and acquired by Campaign Monitor, it has a clean track record. Moderate tracker density typical for email marketing tools.

Conversio App.description: Conversio App is an optimisation and analytics app for Shopify stores.
Conversio App.history: Conversio App is the Shopify app version of Conversio, an eCommerce email marketing optimization platform founded in 2014 by Adii Pienaar. It was acquired by Campaign Monitor in 2019, rebranded as CM Commerce, and operates under the Marigold group. The Shopify app focuses on optimization and analytics for Shopify stores, providing email marketing and analytics tools.
Conversio App.summary_abuse: Conversio App is the Shopify extension of a legitimate email marketing platform now owned by Marigold. No abuse incidents documented. Moderate tracker density typical for email analytics tools. Clean track record.

Conversion Bear.description: Conversion Bear is a Shopify app providing trust badges and sticky add-to-cart features to enhance store credibility and streamline the purchasing process.
Conversion Bear.history: Conversion Bear is a Shopify app providing trust badges and sticky add-to-cart features. No public founding year or notable ownership changes are documented.
Conversion Bear.summary_abuse: Conversion Bear is a Shopify trust badge and cart optimization app with no notable abuse signal. It operates within Shopify's merchant ecosystem.

Convert.description: Convert Experiences is an enterprise A/B testing and personalisation solution for conversion optimisation and data-driven decisions in high-traffic websites.
Convert.history: Convert.com (formerly Convert Insights) was founded around 2009 by Dennis van der Heijden and Claudiu Rogoveanu. The bootstrapped company is headquartered in Walnut, California (with remote teams) and focuses on enterprise A/B testing and personalization for high-traffic websites. It has positioned itself as a privacy-first alternative to tools like Optimizely, emphasizing GDPR compliance and minimal data collection.
Convert.summary_abuse: Convert.com is a reputable bootstrapped enterprise A/B testing tool with a strong privacy-first reputation. It is GDPR compliant and emphasizes minimal data collection. No abuse incidents or dark patterns documented. Widely used by CRO agencies and enterprise marketers.

Convert and Flow.description: Convert and Flow is a customer relationship management tool designed to manage customer relationships and enhance customer satisfaction.
Convert and Flow.history: Convert and Flow is a small CRM tool for managing customer relationships. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
Convert and Flow.summary_abuse: Convert and Flow is a small CRM tool with no documented abuse incidents.

ConvertBox.description: ConvertBox is a tool that delivers targeted on-site messages based on visitor behavior to generate more leads, sales, and customers.
ConvertBox.history: ConvertBox is a targeted on-site messaging and lead capture tool for websites. It is a bootstrapped indie SaaS product. No public founding year or notable ownership changes are documented.
ConvertBox.summary_abuse: ConvertBox is an on-site pop-up and lead capture tool. Pop-up and overlay tools can be configured for dark patterns or aggressive prompts, but no documented abuse incidents exist for ConvertBox specifically.

Convertcart.description: ConvertCart helps online businesses deliver outstanding experiences to customers throughout their journey.
Convertcart.history: ConvertCart was founded in 2014 in Bengaluru, India, by Venkat Rajendran and Arun Kumar as a conversion rate optimization and marketing automation platform for e-commerce. The company reached $11M in annual revenue by 2024 with approximately 100 customers and has remained independently operated.
Convertcart.summary_abuse: ConvertCart is a B2B e-commerce optimization and marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It embeds behavioral tracking on merchant websites to optimize the customer journey, which raises standard privacy considerations but no documented fraud or phishing abuse patterns.

ConvertFlow.description: ConvertFlow is the all-in-one conversion marketing platform.
ConvertFlow.history: ConvertFlow is a conversion marketing platform for building funnels, pop-ups, and landing pages. Founded around 2016, the company is US-based and independently operated. No notable ownership changes are documented.
ConvertFlow.summary_abuse: ConvertFlow is a conversion funnel builder with no documented abuse incidents. Pop-up and funnel builders can be used for aggressive or misleading conversion flows, but legitimate use dominates and no specific reports exist for ConvertFlow.

Convertful.description: Convertful is a tool that automates conversions by turning website traffic into leads and sales in real time.
Convertful.history: Convertful is a small lead generation and conversion SaaS offering popup and opt-in widgets starting at $9/month. Founding year and corporate ownership are not publicly disclosed; the platform appears to be an independent micro-SaaS.
Convertful.summary_abuse: Convertful is a popup and opt-in tool for lead generation. Its features could be misused to create deceptive overlays or fake urgency prompts, but there are no documented incidents of widespread abuse. Abuse risk is minimal relative to the broader SaaS category.

Convertim.description: Convertim is an ecommerce checkout platform.
Convertim.history: Convertim is an e-commerce checkout platform providing payment processing and checkout optimization tools. The company appears to be a small independent startup with limited public corporate history.
Convertim.summary_abuse: Convertim is a checkout platform that processes payment data on e-commerce sites. Payment processors can be misused to facilitate fraudulent transactions on fake storefronts, though Convertim is not documented as a known tool in fraud campaigns. The platform's KYC and merchant onboarding standards are not publicly documented.

Convertiser.description: Convertiser is a website monetization platform from Poland that helps publishers and advertisers optimize revenue through performance-based marketing solutions.
Convertiser.history: Convertiser was established in 2011 in Wroclaw, Poland as a performance-based affiliate marketing and website monetization platform. It works with over 3000 publishers and major Polish ecommerce retailers in fashion, electronics, and finance verticals.
Convertiser.summary_abuse: Convertiser is an affiliate marketing network and publisher monetization platform. Affiliate networks can facilitate adware or cookie stuffing abuse by unvetted publishers, giving it a modest abuse risk. There are no widely documented specific incidents, but performance marketing platforms generally carry some risk of low-quality traffic and attribution fraud.

ConvertKit.description: ConvertKit is an email marketing tool built for content creators.
ConvertKit.history: ConvertKit was founded in 2013 by Nathan Barry in Boise, Idaho. Starting from near-zero revenue with Nathan investing his life savings of $50K, the bootstrapped email marketing platform for creators grew to $35M+ ARR and 100,000+ creator users. Barry struggled through low revenue periods but committed fully to the product, reaching $8M ARR by 2017. The product was briefly rebranded as 'Kit' in 2024.
ConvertKit.summary_abuse: ConvertKit (now Kit) is a reputable email marketing platform for content creators with no significant abuse incidents. It is bootstrapped and independently owned, has been operationally stable for over 10 years, and maintains strong creator community trust. Light tracker density and transparent pricing.

ConvertoBot.description: ConvertoBot is a chatbot and conversational marketing tool.
ConvertoBot.history: ConvertoBot was founded around 2017 as a conversational marketing and chatbot SaaS. It offers subscription plans starting at $27/month for unlimited chatbots. Corporate ownership details are not publicly disclosed.
ConvertoBot.summary_abuse: ConvertoBot is a chatbot and conversational marketing platform. Chatbots can occasionally be deployed on scam landing pages, but ConvertoBot itself has no documented abuse incidents and is a niche B2B tool. Abuse risk is low.

Convertr.description: Convertr is a Brazilian ecommerce platform, fashion specialist.
Convertr.history: Convertr is a Brazilian ecommerce platform specializing in fashion wholesale and retail. The company claims approximately 18 years of experience derived from two pioneer companies in the sector, suggesting founding around 2006. It targets fashion merchants across wholesale, retail, and mixed models.
Convertr.summary_abuse: Convertr is a niche Brazilian ecommerce SaaS for fashion merchants. It has no notable abuse profile and does not offer features that fraudsters commonly exploit.

Convertri.description: Convertri is a sales funnel building solution.
Convertri.history: Convertri was founded in 2016 by Andy Fletcher in Brighton, UK, as a high-speed sales funnel builder explicitly positioned against ClickFunnels and Leadpages. The product's core differentiator has been page load speed through a progressive-rendering architecture. The company reached $1.5M in revenue by 2021 and remains independently operated.
Convertri.summary_abuse: Convertri is a sales funnel and landing page builder that, like similar platforms, can be used to create convincing phishing pages or fake offer funnels. The platform does not offer free hosting with custom domains, which limits its attractiveness for mass phishing campaigns. No specific abuse incidents are documented for this particular tool.

Converzee.description: Converzee is a digital platform created to help companies manage their human resource processes.
Converzee.history: Converzee was founded in 2021 and is based in Iceland. It is a small HR and people-management SaaS platform. No notable ownership changes or funding rounds are publicly documented.
Converzee.summary_abuse: Converzee is a small HR management SaaS. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B tool targeting internal company HR processes with no consumer-facing attack surface.

ConveyThis.description: ConveyThis is a website translation service.
ConveyThis.history: ConveyThis was founded in 2008 by Alex Buran in New York, originally as a simple JavaScript translation button aggregating free translation APIs. The product evolved into a full-featured multilingual website plugin for WordPress, Shopify, and other CMS platforms, combining AI translation with human oversight. By 2024 the service powered over 10,000 multilingual websites and generated $321.8K in annual revenue.
ConveyThis.summary_abuse: ConveyThis has no notable abuse signal. It is a website translation service that embeds a language switcher on client sites. The platform does not host user content or provide file storage, making it unattractive for phishing or malware campaigns.

Convincely.description: Convincely is a platform designed to rapidly launch and optimize personalized sales funnels for businesses.
Convincely.history: Convincely is an Australian behavioral intelligence platform for enterprise teams in finance, retail, insurance, and healthcare. It combines journey orchestration, behavioral measurement, and structured experimentation across three product pillars: Acquisition Intelligence, Conversion Intelligence, and Retention Intelligence. Notable clients include AIA Health, Alinta Energy, JudoBank, RAC Roadside Assist, and RMIT Online. The platform deploys via a single script tag and is rated 4.9/5 on G2.
Convincely.summary_abuse: Convincely is a legitimate enterprise behavioral intelligence and CRO platform. No abuse incidents documented. Serves regulated industries including finance and insurance with enterprise-grade personalization. Moderate tracker density from behavioral analytics. No dark pattern concerns.

Convious.description: Convious is an attraction management platform supporting operations before, during, and after visits, including exit-intent functionality.
Convious.history: Convious was founded in 2017 in Amsterdam, Netherlands, formerly known as Ticketoo. The company raised €10.5M in December 2021 to grow its AI-driven ecommerce platform for the experience economy (theme parks, zoos, museums, wellness venues). Convious has been recognized by Deloitte twice as one of the top 20 fastest-growing companies in the Netherlands. Over 125 companies from 16 countries use the platform.
Convious.summary_abuse: Convious is a reputable Dutch attraction management and ticketing platform for the leisure industry. No abuse incidents documented. GDPR compliant as an EU company. The platform handles visitor data for personalization and dynamic pricing, but within appropriate enterprise data handling practices.

Conviva.description: Conviva is a census, continuous measurement and engagement platform for streaming media.
Conviva.history: Founded in 2006 as Rinera Networks by Carnegie Mellon and UC Berkeley professors Hui Zhang, Ion Stoica, Aditya Ganjam, and Jibin Zhan; rebranded to Conviva in 2008. Raised over $100M in venture funding including $44M in 2013 from NEA, Foundation Capital, GGV, and Time Warner Investments, and $40M more in 2017 valuing the company at around $300M. Pivoted from adaptive bitrate streaming optimization to a broader streaming analytics and audience engagement platform.
Conviva.summary_abuse: Conviva is a backend streaming analytics platform used by large media companies to monitor and optimize video delivery. There is no notable abuse signal associated with Conviva; it is an enterprise B2B tool with no consumer-facing attack surface relevant to phishing, fraud, or scam infrastructure.

Convrrt.description: Convrrt is a platform that offers tools for creating responsive landing pages.
Convrrt.history: Convrrt is a white-label landing page builder platform founded by Kavin Patel and Dhruv Patel, reportedly started in 2006. It is designed primarily for SaaS companies to embed landing page creation capabilities into their products. The platform has crossed $1M ARR and published over 1.7 million landing pages, remaining an independent startup.
Convrrt.summary_abuse: Convrrt is a SaaS landing page builder used by marketing teams and SaaS vendors. Landing page builders carry a moderate abuse risk since they can be used to create phishing or scam pages, but Convrrt targets B2B SaaS customers rather than anonymous users, which limits abuse exposure. No notable public incidents or reports of widespread abuse have been documented.

Conword.description: Conword is a platform that provides automatic translations for websites, enabling multilingual content delivery without manual intervention.
Conword.history: Conword is a small SaaS platform providing automatic website translation services. Corporate ownership, founding year, and funding history are not publicly disclosed.
Conword.summary_abuse: Conword is a website translation automation tool. It has no notable abuse profile; automated translation services are not commonly exploited by fraudsters.

Cookie Assistant.description: Cookie Assistant is a system designed to manage user consent for cookies while providing analytics.
Cookie Assistant.history: Cookie Assistant is a small SaaS product providing cookie consent management and analytics, aimed at GDPR compliance for websites. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Cookie Assistant.summary_abuse: Cookie Assistant is a cookie consent management tool with no documented abuse signal. It serves a compliance function and does not expose features commonly exploited for fraud.

Cookie Control.history: Cookie Control is a consent management platform developed by CIVIC UK, a British digital agency. It is a certified IAB TCF v2.0 Consent Management Platform and is available as a standalone JavaScript widget as well as a plugin for WordPress, Drupal, and Joomla. It is available on the UK Government Digital Marketplace.
Cookie Control.summary_abuse: Cookie Control is a consent management tool and privacy compliance solution. It has no notable abuse signal; it is specifically designed to prevent unauthorized tracking and enhance user privacy.

Cookie Information.description: Cookie Information is a privacy tech company that develops software that helps making company websites and mobile apps GDPR and ePrivacy compliant.
Cookie Information.history: Cookie Information launched its Consent Management Platform in 2017 and grew to be Northern Europe's leading cookie consent solution, serving 5,000+ organizations. In 2023, the company merged with Piwik PRO, a Polish analytics platform, expanding its product suite to integrate consent management with privacy-first analytics.
Cookie Information.summary_abuse: Cookie Information is a GDPR-focused consent management platform. As a privacy compliance tool, it has no notable abuse signal; the service is specifically designed to protect user data and facilitate regulatory compliance.

Cookie Information plugin.description: Cookie Information plugin helps your website stay compliant with GDPR using a free cookie pop-up, consent log, and more.
Cookie Information plugin.history: The Cookie Information WordPress plugin is the official integration of the Cookie Information CMP for WordPress sites. It is maintained by the Cookie Information team, which merged with Piwik PRO in 2023. The plugin supports Google Consent Mode v2 and IAB TCF.
Cookie Information plugin.summary_abuse: The Cookie Information plugin is a self-hosted WordPress integration of a GDPR consent management platform. It has no notable abuse signal and is specifically designed to protect user privacy.

Cookie Notice.description: Cookie Notice provides a simple, customizable website banner that can be used to help your website comply with certain cookie consent requirements under the EU GDPR cookie law and CCPA regulations and includes seamless integration with Cookie Compliance to help your site comply with the latest updates to existing consent laws.
Cookie Notice.history: Cookie Notice is a WordPress plugin by hu-manity.co for displaying cookie consent banners. It has over 1 million active installations on WordPress and is one of the earliest and most widely deployed cookie consent plugins, predating GDPR which came into force in 2018.
Cookie Notice.summary_abuse: Cookie Notice is a widely used self-hosted WordPress plugin for cookie consent banners. It has no notable abuse signal and is purely a compliance tool that runs within a website's own installation.

Cookie Script.description: Cookie-Script automatically scans, categorizes and adds description to all cookies found on your website.
