Cookie Script.history: CookieScript (cookie-script.com) is a Lithuanian consent management platform that launched around 2018. The WordPress plugin was first published on August 28, 2018. It is certified by Google for Consent Mode v2 and is IAB TCF integrated. It serves primarily SMB websites and is rated as a top CMP for small businesses on G2.
Cookie Script.summary_abuse: CookieScript is a GDPR-focused consent management platform. There is no notable abuse signal; its purpose is privacy compliance and it is used by legitimate website operators. Some WordPress user reviews noted difficulty deactivating the plugin, but no fraud or scam-related issues have been documented.

Cookie Seal.description: Cookie Seal is a tool or system that helps you manage and configure the use of cookies on your website in accordance with data protection laws, ensuring compliance with relevant regulations.
Cookie Seal.history: Cookie Seal is a small GDPR cookie consent management tool for websites. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
Cookie Seal.summary_abuse: Cookie Seal is a cookie consent and compliance management tool. It is specifically designed to help websites comply with GDPR cookie regulations and has no meaningful abuse profile.

CookieBAR.description: CookieBAR is a tool that helps websites comply with the EU cookie law by managing user consent for cookies in accordance with regulatory requirements.
CookieBAR.history: CookieBAR is a free open-source vanilla JavaScript plugin for EU cookie law compliance, hosted on GitHub under the MIT license. It was created to help websites display a compliant cookie consent notice without any framework dependencies.
CookieBAR.summary_abuse: CookieBAR is a lightweight open-source cookie consent library. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Cookiebot.description: Cookiebot is a cloud-driven solution that automatically controls cookies and trackers, enabling full GDPR/ePrivacy and CCPA compliance for websites.
Cookiebot.history: Cookiebot was developed by the Danish company Cybot, founded in 2012. It became one of the most widely used cookie consent management platforms globally. In September 2021, Usercentrics (a German CMP founded in 2017) acquired Cybot, merging the two companies to form a global consent management leader. Cookiebot continues to operate as a distinct product line focused on SMBs while Usercentrics targets enterprise.
Cookiebot.summary_abuse: Cookiebot is one of the most widely used GDPR consent management platforms. It has no notable abuse signal; it is specifically designed to enforce privacy compliance. The platform has 100,000+ WordPress installations and serves millions of websites.

CookieFirst.description: CookieFirst is an GDPR and CCPA compliant consent management platform.
CookieFirst.history: CookieFirst was founded in 2019 in Amsterdam, Netherlands by Kees Van Den Bos and Tom Alexander van den Bos. It provides an all-in-one cookie consent management platform compliant with GDPR, LGPD, CCPA, and ePrivacy regulations.
CookieFirst.summary_abuse: CookieFirst is a GDPR-focused consent management platform. It has no notable abuse signal and is specifically designed to protect user privacy and assist websites with regulatory compliance.

CookieHub.description: CookieHub is a platform that helps website owners comply with data privacy regulations such as GDPR and CCPA by providing tools for managing cookie consent, tracking consent preferences, and ensuring legal compliance related to online tracking and data collection practices.
CookieHub.history: CookieHub was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in Reykjavik, Iceland. It serves 40,000+ websites with cookie consent management and GDPR/CCPA compliance. In 2026 the company launched a new DSAR (Data Subject Access Request) management platform for SMEs.
CookieHub.summary_abuse: CookieHub is an Icelandic consent management platform. It has no notable abuse signal and is focused entirely on helping websites achieve GDPR and privacy regulation compliance.

CookieYes.history: CookieYes was founded in 2018 in Milton Keynes, United Kingdom as a WordPress plugin for cookie compliance following GDPR adoption. It grew from a simple banner plugin into a full consent management platform supporting GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, and Google Consent Mode v2. It is used by over 1.5 million websites.
CookieYes.summary_abuse: CookieYes is one of the most widely deployed GDPR consent management platforms. It has no notable abuse signal; the service exists specifically to protect user privacy and facilitate regulatory compliance.

Cool Popup.description: Cool Popup is a tool that helps convert website visitors into customers, subscribers, or leads by displaying visually designed popups that require no coding to set up.
Cool Popup.history: Cool Popup is a small no-code popup builder SaaS for lead capture and conversion optimization. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Cool Popup.summary_abuse: Cool Popup is a marketing popup tool with minimal documented abuse signal. Like all popup and overlay tools, it could be used for deceptive consent overlays, but no specific incidents are documented.

Cool Tag Cloud.description: A simple tag cloud system for WordPress.
Cool Tag Cloud.history: Cool Tag Cloud is a WordPress plugin by Cool Plugins (CoolHappy) for displaying customizable tag clouds on WordPress sites. It is part of a larger portfolio of WordPress plugins by the same Indian developer team.
Cool Tag Cloud.summary_abuse: Cool Tag Cloud is a self-hosted WordPress plugin for displaying tag clouds. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

Cool Timeline.description: Creates vertical and horizontal history timeline blocks.
Cool Timeline.history: Cool Timeline is a WordPress plugin by Cool Plugins (CoolHappy / Narinder Singh) first published on November 23, 2015. It allows website owners to create vertical and horizontal timeline sliders. It has 20,000+ active installations, 325 reviews averaging 4.7 stars, and is actively maintained with regular updates in 2026.
Cool Timeline.summary_abuse: Cool Timeline is a self-hosted WordPress plugin for displaying timeline content. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

Cooladata.description: Cooladata is a data warehouse and behavioral analytics platform designed for gaming, elearning, ecommerce, SaaS, and media companies.
Cooladata.history: CoolaData was founded in 2012 in Israel by Guy Greenberg and Tomer Ben Moshe as an advanced BI and behavioral analytics platform for cloud-stored user data. The company raised $13M from 83North, Salesforce Ventures, and Carmel Ventures. On June 3, 2019, CoolaData was acquired by Medallia for $7.6M to enhance Medallia's customer experience analytics capabilities.
Cooladata.summary_abuse: CoolaData is a backend behavioral analytics platform used by enterprises. Following its 2019 acquisition by Medallia, it operates as part of an enterprise customer experience platform. No notable abuse signal is associated with this technology.

Cooltix.description: Cooltix is an event and ticketing system designed to manage and sell tickets for concerts.
Cooltix.history: Cooltix was founded in 2018 and is headquartered in Budapest, Hungary. It is an event ticketing and management SaaS primarily serving the Hungarian and Central European market for concerts and events.
Cooltix.summary_abuse: Cooltix is an event ticketing platform. Ticketing platforms occasionally attract fake ticket scams on third-party resale sites, but the platform itself has no documented pattern of abuse and primarily serves legitimate event organizers.

Coopt.description: Coopt is a tool designed to boost sales by transforming customers into social media advertisers, leveraging their networks to promote products and services, thus enhancing brand visibility and engagement.
Coopt.history: Coopt is a small SaaS tool that turns customers into social media promoters by offering incentives for sharing products. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
Coopt.summary_abuse: Coopt is a referral and social sharing tool for ecommerce. It could be used to create spam-like social promotions, but there are no documented abuse incidents and its user base is small. Abuse risk is minimal.

Copernica.description: Copernica is a platform for managing email campaigns and automating customer communication using structured data and rule-based workflows.
Copernica.history: Copernica was founded in 2001 in Amsterdam, Netherlands by Michael Linthorst and Emiel Bruintjes as an email marketing and campaign management platform. It provides tools for email, web, mobile messaging, and PDF marketing campaigns, with a focus on the European market.
Copernica.summary_abuse: Copernica is an email marketing and marketing automation platform. As with all mass-email platforms, it carries moderate abuse risk from spam campaigns, though it focuses on a European B2B audience and is subject to GDPR-compliant use. No major public abuse incidents are documented.

Copilot AI.description: CoPilot AI is a LinkedIn automation software designed to identify and generate leads.
Copilot AI.history: Microsoft Copilot launched in 2023 as Microsoft's primary generative AI assistant, replacing Cortana and built on OpenAI's GPT-4 models through Microsoft's multi-billion dollar partnership with OpenAI. It is integrated into Microsoft 365, Windows, Edge, and Bing. The paid tier (Copilot Pro) launched alongside the consumer assistant.
Copilot AI.summary_abuse: Microsoft Copilot is a mainstream generative AI assistant with a large user base. Like other AI tools, it can be prompted to generate harmful content, and Microsoft has deployed content safety filters accordingly. No specific large-scale abuse infrastructure role has been documented; abuse risk is typical for major AI chatbots.

Copilot.Live.description: Copilot.Live is an AI agent with human-level capabilities, enabling the creation of AI agents for customer support.
Copilot.Live.history: Copilot.live is a conversational AI agent platform that automates customer support, sales, and marketing tasks. It is associated with Fynd (rebranded as Kaily) and was built as a no-code AI assistant builder. The platform is SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliant.
Copilot.Live.summary_abuse: Copilot.live is a no-code AI chatbot builder. No documented large-scale abuse incidents are associated with the platform. As with most AI automation tools, there is potential for misuse in creating deceptive chatbots, but no specific incidents are documented.

Copiny.description: Copiny is a Russian-based community support system.
Copiny.history: Copiny was founded in 2010 in Moscow, Russia by Dmitry Chistov and Andrey Petrenko as an online customer support community platform. The company raised $500K in funding and reported ~$440K annual revenue with a 4-person team. It provides peer-driven community, feedback widget, and knowledge base functionality.
Copiny.summary_abuse: Copiny is a Russian-based online community and customer support platform. It has no significant documented abuse or fraud incidents; it targets B2B customers needing peer support forums.

Coppermine.description: Coppermine is an open-source image gallery application.
Coppermine.history: Coppermine Photo Gallery was initiated by Grégory Demar in the early 2000s and released under the GPL license. It became one of the more popular self-hosted PHP/MySQL image gallery scripts of its era, with integrations for phpBB, Joomla, and other platforms. Development continues at a slower pace maintained by a community team.
Coppermine.summary_abuse: Coppermine is a self-hosted open source photo gallery. It has no notable abuse signal as a platform; however, outdated installations have historically been targeted by attackers exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities to host malicious content. The project's self-hosted nature means abuse depends on the deploying site operator's maintenance practices.

CopyPoison.description: Copypoison is a plagarism protection tool that protects content by replacing text with symbols that are visually similar.
CopyPoison.history: CopyPoison is a very small plagiarism protection tool that replaces copied text with visually-similar symbols to deter scraping. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
CopyPoison.summary_abuse: CopyPoison is a content protection tool that obfuscates text on copy. The technique of substituting similar-looking characters could theoretically be repurposed for homoglyph phishing, though the tool itself targets legitimate content protection. There are no documented abuse incidents.

Cordial.description: Cordial is a cross-channel marketing and customer engagement platform for enterprise brands to send personalized email, SMS, mobile push notifications.
Cordial.history: Cordial was founded in 2014 in San Diego, CA by David Baker, Chris McGreal, Jeremy Swift, and Adam Gillespie. It provides a cross-channel marketing automation platform for enterprise brands, focusing on personalized email, SMS, and push campaigns. In July 2022, the company raised $50M in funding.
Cordial.summary_abuse: Cordial is an enterprise cross-channel marketing platform for email, SMS, and push campaigns. As with any mass-messaging platform, there is potential for abuse through spam, though the platform targets enterprise customers with compliance requirements. No documented large-scale abuse incidents are associated with Cordial.

Core Framework.description: Core Framework is a visual CSS framework for WordPress and other platforms.
Core Framework.history: Core Framework is a visual CSS framework and page-building toolkit primarily designed for WordPress and other web platforms. It offers a freemium model with a one-time premium option. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Core Framework.summary_abuse: Core Framework is a WordPress CSS/page-building plugin with no documented abuse signal. It does not expose features typically targeted by fraudsters.

core-js.description: core-js is a modular standard library for JavaScript, with polyfills for cutting-edge ECMAScript features.
core-js.history: core-js was created by Denis Pushkarev (zloirock) around 2014 as a modular polyfill library for modern JavaScript. It is one of the most downloaded npm packages in the world. In 2023, the maintainer published a controversial blog post about the project's sustainability, sparking broad discussion about open-source funding. The project has remained independently maintained.
core-js.summary_abuse: core-js is a foundational JavaScript polyfill library with no notable abuse signal. It is a backend/bundle-time developer dependency with no consumer-facing surface.

Corebine.description: Corebine is a content management system designed for Sports
Corebine.history: Corebine is a sports CMS and fan engagement platform launched in 2017 by OMNIGON, an Infront Sports & Media group company. Infront is a Swiss-based global sports marketing and media company. Corebine was designed as a mobile-first platform for sports clubs and media rights holders.
Corebine.summary_abuse: Corebine is an enterprise sports CMS deployed by professional clubs and media organizations. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B enterprise product with controlled access and no consumer-facing surfaces that fraudsters target.

Corebook.description: Corebook is a platform for brand guidelines, designed to assist creators of brilliant brands in managing their brand.
Corebook.history: Corebook is a Latvian SaaS platform for digital brand guidelines, launched publicly in 2020 with a $1.2M seed valuation. It was co-founded by Raitis Velps and targets brand designers and agencies. Notable clients include Virgin Atlantic, TomTom, Chelsea F.C., Tommy Hilfiger, and Miro.
Corebook.summary_abuse: Corebook is a digital brand guidelines platform used by design teams and agencies. It has no notable abuse signal and no consumer-facing features that could be easily exploited.

CoreMedia.description: CoreMedia is an enterprise-grade, Java-based content management system used to manage, publish, and deliver digital content across websites and channels.
CoreMedia.history: CoreMedia was founded in 1996 at the University of Hamburg by Soren Stamer and colleagues. Its first major customer was DPA (German Press Agency), and it built expertise in high-speed content management for news publishers. The company grew into an enterprise digital experience platform used by large media and retail brands. It was acquired by private equity firm OpenGate Capital.
CoreMedia.summary_abuse: CoreMedia is an enterprise CMS used by large media and retail organizations. It is a backend B2B product with no consumer-facing abuse surface and no documented abuse signal.

CoreMedia Content Cloud.description: CoreMedia Content Cloud is an agile content management and digital asset management platform.
CoreMedia Content Cloud.history: CoreMedia Content Cloud is the cloud-hosted SaaS variant of the CoreMedia enterprise digital experience platform. CoreMedia was founded in 1996 in Hamburg, Germany and acquired by OpenGate Capital. Content Cloud provides cloud-native deployment of the CoreMedia DXP with managed hosting, content management, and headless delivery capabilities.
CoreMedia Content Cloud.summary_abuse: CoreMedia Content Cloud is an enterprise SaaS CMS platform. It is a B2B product with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse signal.

CoreUI.description: CoreUI provides cloud hosting, web and mobile design, animations, wireframes, and UX testing services.
CoreUI.history: CoreUI is an open-source UI component library and admin template suite built on Bootstrap 5, providing native implementations for React, Angular, Vue, and plain JavaScript. It was created to offer a consistent cross-framework UI library without jQuery dependencies. The MIT-licensed free version is maintained alongside a commercial PRO tier.
CoreUI.summary_abuse: CoreUI is an open-source frontend UI library. It is a developer tool with no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

Corksy.description: Corksy is a solution for wineries, offering technology and services aimed at enhancing direct-to-consumer sales.
Corksy.history: Corksy launched in 2022 as an all-in-one direct-to-consumer winery platform built by the team at BrandClick, a digital marketing agency based in Irvine, California. The platform integrates wine club management, ecommerce, POS, reservations, CRM, and marketing. In 2022 Corksy acquired Vinebase to expand its capabilities.
Corksy.summary_abuse: Corksy is a specialized winery DTC platform for wine sales and club management. It operates in a regulated industry (alcohol sales) which involves age verification and compliance. No notable abuse or fraud incidents have been documented.

Cornerstone.description: Cornerstone is a content management system that includes modules for CRM and membership management.
Cornerstone.history: Cornerstone is the visual website builder component of Themeco's WordPress product suite. It was introduced in May 2015 with the release of X theme version 4.0. Themeco also produces the Pro theme. Together, X and Pro with Cornerstone have been among the top-selling WordPress theme products on ThemeForest for over a decade.
Cornerstone.summary_abuse: Cornerstone is a WordPress page builder plugin for web designers. It is a self-hosted tool with no notable abuse signal.

CoRover.description: CoRover is a conversational AI chatbot platform with proprietary cognitive AI technology.
CoRover.history: Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Bengaluru, India, CoRover is a conversational AI platform. It developed proprietary cognitive AI technology and the BharatGPT large language model to build multi-lingual virtual assistants, gaining significant traction by powering chatbots for major Indian enterprises and public sector organizations like IRCTC.
CoRover.summary_abuse: CoRover is an enterprise conversational AI and chatbot platform. It is used by large organizations to automate customer service and has no consumer-facing hosting or communication features that fraudsters typically target, resulting in a low abuse signal.

Correos.description: Correos is a state-owned company responsible for providing postal service in Spain.
Correos.history: Correos (Sociedad Estatal Correos y Telegrafos) is Spain's national postal service, with origins dating to 1716. It is state-owned and provides universal postal services across Spain including domestic and international shipping. In recent decades it expanded into logistics, ecommerce fulfillment, and digital services for online retailers.
Correos.summary_abuse: Correos is Spain's national postal service. Parcel delivery services are routinely impersonated in phishing and smishing attacks, with fraudsters sending fake Correos delivery notifications to steal credentials or payment data. Correos actively warns customers about these impersonation scams. The actual Correos platform is not directly implicated in abuse.

Correos Ecommerce.description: Correos Ecommerce is an ecommerce platfrom from Spain.
Correos Ecommerce.history: Correos Ecommerce is the dedicated logistics and fulfillment platform of Spain's state-owned postal service Correos. It provides warehousing, order management, fulfillment, and returns management for online retailers, along with API integrations for ecommerce platforms.
Correos Ecommerce.summary_abuse: Correos Ecommerce is Spain's state postal service ecommerce logistics platform. Like all parcel delivery brands, Correos is frequently impersonated in phishing scams, but the actual platform itself is not a source of abuse.

Corso.description: Corso is an all-in-one platform for Shopify that provides shipping protection, returns, exchanges, and warranty management to streamline post-purchase processes.
Corso.history: Corso is a post-purchase platform for Shopify brands that centralizes returns, exchanges, warranties, and shipping protection. It operates on a usage-based model funded by customer opt-ins to delivery protection at checkout, with no monthly fees. The company targets scaling DTC Shopify merchants.
Corso.summary_abuse: Corso is a post-purchase ecommerce platform for Shopify merchants. No notable fraud or abuse signal is associated with the platform.

Cosmedcloud.description: Cosmedcloud is cloud-based practice management software tailored for cosmetic and aesthetic medicine clinics.
Cosmedcloud.history: Cosmedcloud was founded approximately in 2013 by practitioners who needed an integrated platform to run their cosmetic medicine clinics. It provides cloud-based practice management tailored to cosmetic and aesthetic medicine, including medical records and patient management.
Cosmedcloud.summary_abuse: Cosmedcloud is a healthcare practice management SaaS for cosmetic and aesthetic medicine clinics. It handles sensitive patient data but has no notable abuse profile; it is a closed B2B product with no consumer-facing attack surface.

Cosmic.description: Cosmic is a cloud-based CMS that provides developers with an API and web interface for content management, enabling the creation of custom content types, relationship definitions, and webhooks for actions based on content changes.
Cosmic.history: Cosmic JS was founded in 2014 by Tony Spiro and Carson Gibbons as a cloud-hosted headless CMS providing API-first content management for any website or app. The company went through Y Combinator (W19) and raised a $150K seed round in 2019. It has since expanded from headless CMS into an AI-powered content and app platform.
Cosmic.summary_abuse: Cosmic is a headless CMS platform. It has no notable abuse signal; it is a developer tool used by teams building web applications.

Cosmoshop.history: CosmoShop GmbH was founded in 1997 near Munich, Germany as a B2B ecommerce shop software vendor. The company has been developing modular shop systems for 28 years and serves primarily the German B2B market. It was recognized as the best shop software in Germany in the techconsult Professional User Rating 2026.
Cosmoshop.summary_abuse: CosmoShop is a German B2B ecommerce platform. It serves primarily business clients in Germany and has no notable abuse or fraud signal.

Cotonti.description: Cotonti is an open-source content management system and development framework offering flexibility and modularity for building and managing websites and web applications.
Cotonti.history: Cotonti is an open-source PHP content management framework (CMF) with origins in the late 2000s. It is licensed under the 3-clause BSD license and developed by a community of 51 contributors. The project is hosted on GitHub and has had 4,279 commits. It is primarily used in Russian-speaking markets.
Cotonti.summary_abuse: Cotonti is a self-hosted open-source CMS framework. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

CouchDB.history: Apache CouchDB was created in April 2005 by Damien Katz, a former IBM Lotus Notes developer, initially in C++ then rewritten in Erlang in 2006. It was released as open source under the GNU GPL, entered the Apache Software Foundation Incubator in February 2008, and graduated to an Apache top-level project in November 2008. It is notable as one of the pioneering NoSQL document databases and uses HTTP/REST for its API with JSON document storage.
CouchDB.summary_abuse: Apache CouchDB is a foundational NoSQL database used as backend infrastructure. It has no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse signal related to its use as a database engine.

Counseling Kit.description: Counseling Kit is a HIPAA-compliant platform offering professional online tools designed specifically for therapists to support virtual therapy sessions.
Counseling Kit.history: Counseling Kit is a small HIPAA-compliant SaaS platform offering virtual therapy tools for therapists and mental health practitioners. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
Counseling Kit.summary_abuse: Counseling Kit is a HIPAA-compliant telehealth tool for therapists. It handles sensitive mental health data but is a closed professional platform with no consumer-facing attack surface and no documented abuse incidents.

Countable.description: Countable is a community platform designed to support discussion, collaboration, and engagement among members through shared content, forums, and interactive features.
Countable.history: Countable is a community engagement platform that supports discussion, collaboration, and content sharing among members. It is designed for organizations seeking to build interactive online communities.
Countable.summary_abuse: Countable is a community platform for online discussions. Community platforms carry some abuse risk from spam and coordinated inauthentic behavior, though no specific incidents are documented for this platform.

Countdown Timer Ultimate.description: Add and display responsive Countdown timer on your website.
Countdown Timer Ultimate.history: Countdown Timer Ultimate is a WordPress plugin by WP OnlineSupport (Essential Plugin) providing customizable countdown timers for websites. It supports Gutenberg, Elementor, Beaver Builder, Divi, and WooCommerce coupon integration. The free version is available on WordPress.org and a Pro version is sold at $99/year.
Countdown Timer Ultimate.summary_abuse: Countdown Timer Ultimate is a self-hosted WordPress plugin for displaying countdown timers. It has no consumer-facing hosted service and no notable abuse signal.

Countly.description: Countly is an open-source analytics platform that provides real-time insights into mobile, web, and desktop applications, offering features such as user segmentation, performance monitoring, and crash analytics to help businesses optimize their digital experiences and user engagement strategies.
Countly.history: Countly was founded in 2012 in London as an open-source mobile analytics platform offering a privacy-first alternative to Google Analytics. Starting with the Community Edition for self-hosting, it expanded to support web, desktop, and IoT analytics with features including push notifications, A/B testing, and crash monitoring. The company bootstrapped its growth and serves over 5000 apps in 93 countries.
Countly.summary_abuse: Countly is a privacy-focused open-source analytics platform. It is specifically designed as a GDPR-compliant alternative to behavioral tracking tools. No notable abuse signal is associated with Countly itself.

coUrbanize.description: coUrbanize is a platform that assists real estate development and planning teams in conducting community engagement online.
coUrbanize.history: coUrbanize is a SaaS platform for online community engagement in real estate development and urban planning. Limited founding details are publicly available.
coUrbanize.summary_abuse: coUrbanize is a community engagement platform for urban planning with no notable abuse signal. It targets municipal and real estate stakeholders and has no consumer-facing content-hosting features that fraudsters would exploit.

Coureon.description: Coureon is a digital logistics carrier for international shipping.
Coureon.history: Coureon Logistics GmbH was a Berlin-based logistics startup founded in 2014 that built a 100% digital parcel delivery network aggregating third-party carriers for e-commerce merchants. The company is no longer active and is listed as deadpooled.
Coureon.summary_abuse: Coureon was a logistics SaaS platform that is now defunct. No notable abuse signal was associated with the platform during its operation.

Cova.description: Cova is a cannabis software platform that supports dispensary operations with point-of-sale, payments, inventory management, and regulatory compliance.
Cova.history: Cova Software was founded to serve the cannabis retail industry, growing to become the number-one cannabis POS in North America with a 98% customer retention rate. Headquarters are in Denver, Colorado and Vancouver, British Columbia. The platform includes dispensary POS, payments, inventory management, compliance reporting, and ecommerce.
Cova.summary_abuse: Cova is a cannabis dispensary POS platform operating in a regulated industry. It is designed for compliance with state cannabis regulations and seed-to-sale tracking. No notable abuse signal is associated with the platform.

Cove.description: Cove is a commenting and community platform that integrates with Ghost sites to enable reader discussions and user engagement.
Cove.history: Cove Data Protection is a cloud-first backup and disaster recovery service developed by N-able (formerly SolarWinds MSP) for managed service providers. It provides backup for servers, workstations, and Microsoft 365 with AES 256-bit encryption and a multi-tenant dashboard. N-able separated from SolarWinds as an independent public company in 2021.
Cove.summary_abuse: Cove Data Protection is an enterprise backup and disaster recovery platform for managed service providers. It is a B2B security product with no notable abuse signal.

Coveo.description: Coveo is an enterprise search and relevance platform that uses AI to enhance search capabilities and deliver personalized content across digital touchpoints.
Coveo.history: Coveo was founded in 2005 in Quebec City as a spin-off from Copernic Technologies by Laurent Simoneau, Richard Tessier, and Marc Sanfacon. Louis Tetu joined as CEO in 2008. The company raised $100M in 2018 led by Evergreen Coast Capital, and closed a C$227M ($172M) funding round in 2019 at a $1B+ valuation. In October 2021, Coveo acquired Qubit, an AI personalization company. The company is publicly traded on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
Coveo.summary_abuse: Coveo is an enterprise AI-powered search and personalization platform. It is a backend B2B SaaS used by Fortune-500 companies and has no notable abuse signal.

CoverManager.description: CoverManager is a restaurant table booking widget.
CoverManager.history: CoverManager was founded around 2014 in Seville, Spain, as a restaurant reservation and guest management platform. It grew to serve over 6,000 restaurants across 25 countries, competing with platforms like TheFork and OpenTable in the European market. In 2022 the company raised €35M to accelerate expansion. In 2025 CoverManager was acquired by French hospitality tech company Zenchef, with the merger completing in Q3 2025 forming a major pan-European restaurant technology platform.
CoverManager.summary_abuse: CoverManager collects guest dining preferences and reservation data. As a restaurant SaaS with EU headquarters, it operates under GDPR. The platform is focused on operational reservation management rather than advertising analytics, so tracker density is low. No significant abuse patterns have been documented publicly.

Covery.description: Covery is a risk management platform that provides KYC and device fingerprinting fraud detection tools to prevent identity theft and fraudulent transactions.
Covery.history: Covery was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Birkirkara, Malta. The company provides a fraud prevention and risk management platform offering KYC/AML identity verification, device fingerprinting, and behavioral analytics. It has achieved Dow Jones certification for AML compliance data quality. Covery primarily serves online businesses in fintech, e-commerce, and iGaming sectors that need real-time fraud signals and chargeback protection.
Covery.summary_abuse: Covery processes sensitive personal data including identity documents and behavioral biometrics for fraud detection. Its services are regulated under EU AML directives and GDPR. The platform operates in a compliance-intensive space and is not known for any abusive data practices beyond what is inherent to fraud prevention operations.

Covet.pics.description: Covet.pics is a customizable Shopify app for Instagram and Lookbook shoppable galleries.
Covet.pics.history: Covet.pics is a Shopify app developed by Space Squirrel Ltd, a small Canadian software company. The app enables Shopify merchants to embed shoppable Instagram and UGC galleries on their storefronts. It integrates with Instagram's API to pull images and tag products, allowing shoppers to buy directly from social content. Plans start at $29.99/month for up to 5 galleries, targeting small to mid-size Shopify merchants seeking social proof and increased conversion.
Covet.pics.summary_abuse: Covet.pics processes Instagram OAuth tokens and social media images on behalf of merchants. It embeds gallery widgets into Shopify storefronts which may load external scripts from its CDN. No significant privacy abuse documented. Data handling is governed by Shopify's app partner policies and GDPR where applicable.

Cowboy.description: Cowboy is a small, fast, modular HTTP server written in Erlang.
Cowboy.history: Cowboy is a small, fast HTTP server for Erlang/OTP created by Loïc Hoguin and first released in 2011 under the Nine Nines project. It supports HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, WebSocket, and REST via Webmachine-style handlers. Cowboy is widely used as the foundation for Phoenix framework connections in Elixir and for production Erlang services requiring low-latency concurrency. It uses Ranch for connection management and is the de-facto HTTP server in the Erlang ecosystem.
Cowboy.summary_abuse: Cowboy is a pure server-side Erlang/OTP library with no client-side JavaScript, no telemetry collection, and no licensing restrictions beyond its ISC open-source license. It is widely reviewed and has no known supply chain abuse or malicious code issues.

Cozy AntiTheft.description: Cozy AntiTheft helps you to protect your store content, images and texts from being stolen with a few simple clicks.
Cozy AntiTheft.history: Cozy AntiTheft is a Shopify app that protects store images and text from being copied. It is a micro-SaaS product available on the Shopify App Store. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
Cozy AntiTheft.summary_abuse: Cozy AntiTheft is a content protection app for Shopify stores. It has no notable abuse profile; it exists to protect content rather than enable any harm.

CPABuild.description: CPABuild is a next generation CPA network with integrated template building and sharing functionality.
CPABuild.history: CPABuild is a CPA (cost-per-action) affiliate network with an integrated landing page builder, allowing publishers to create content locking and survey-type monetization pages.
CPABuild.summary_abuse: CPABuild is a CPA affiliate network combining content locking, surveys, and template building. CPA content locker platforms are strongly associated with scam offers and deceptive monetization tactics: users are trapped behind fake surveys, prize claims, or software installation prompts. CPABuild has been cited in consumer protection forums as a platform hosting misleading CPA campaigns. The content locking model and permissive offer review make it a significant abuse vector.

cPanel.description: cPanel is a web hosting control panel. The software provides a graphical interface and automation tools designed to simplify the process of hosting a website.
cPanel.history: cPanel was originally developed in 1996 by J. Nick Koston for Speed Hosting. It grew to become the dominant web hosting control panel. In 2018, cPanel LLC was acquired by Oakley Capital, becoming part of the WebPros group alongside Plesk. A controversial 2019 pricing change from per-server to per-account billing caused significant backlash in the hosting industry.
cPanel.summary_abuse: cPanel is widely used in the hosting industry and occasionally appears in abuse contexts because it is used by many low-cost shared hosting providers that may attract abusive tenants. Compromised cPanel accounts are a common target for phishers who upload malicious scripts. The platform does not itself facilitate abuse but its broad adoption across lower-tier hosting means it frequently appears in phishing infrastructure.

CPEx.description: CPEx is a publisher exchange system facilitating content distribution and collaboration among publishers.
CPEx.history: CPEx is a Czech publisher exchange system for programmatic advertising, facilitating content distribution and advertising among Czech media companies.
CPEx.summary_abuse: CPEx is a Czech programmatic advertising exchange. Like all ad networks it can carry malvertising risk. No major documented abuse incidents specific to CPEx.

CPG Dragonfly.history: CPG Dragonfly CMS is an open-source PHP-based CMS derived from PHP-Nuke 6.5, developed by the Dragonfly CMS community. Active in the early-to-mid 2000s, it is largely abandoned with no active development in recent years.
CPG Dragonfly.summary_abuse: CPG Dragonfly CMS is an abandoned open-source PHP CMS. Old, unmaintained CMS installations can be exploited to host phishing pages or malware if left unpatched, but this is a general risk of abandoned software rather than specific to Dragonfly's design.

CppCMS.history: CppCMS is a high-performance C++ web development framework created by Artyom Beilis, first appearing around 2008. Despite its name, it is not a CMS but a framework for building web applications in C++. It targets use cases requiring extreme performance, supporting FastCGI and various database backends. It is dual-licensed under LGPLv3 for open-source use and a commercial license for proprietary use. Activity has significantly slowed in recent years, with the project now largely dormant.
CppCMS.summary_abuse: CppCMS is a server-side C++ library with no client-side components. There are no reported supply chain, telemetry, or licensing abuse issues. The dual LGPLv3/commercial licensing is standard for this type of framework and creates no inherent user-facing privacy concerns.

Craft CMS.description: Craft CMS is a content management system for building bespoke websites.
Craft CMS.history: Craft CMS was created by Brandon Kelly at Pixel & Tonic and launched in 2013 as a content-first CMS for developers and content managers. The company is headquartered in Bend, Oregon. Craft CMS has been used by over 40,000 websites including those for major brands like Oakley, IKEA, and Netflix. It initially used a one-time license model but transitioned to subscription-based pricing with Craft 4. The Solo edition remains free; the Pro and Team tiers carry an annual fee for updates.
Craft CMS.summary_abuse: Craft CMS is self-hosted software with no telemetry by default. The control panel does communicate with Craft's licensing server for license validation. No significant privacy or abuse issues are documented. The project maintains responsible disclosure channels and has a healthy security track record.

Craft Commerce.history: Craft Commerce is the official e-commerce plugin for Craft CMS, built and maintained by Pixel & Tonic. It was first released around 2015 and provides a full-featured commerce layer including product management, cart and checkout flows, order management, taxes, shipping, and discounts. Craft Commerce 5 introduced multi-store support for up to five stores per install, inventory location management, and improved Stripe integration. It requires a paid license and is available in Lite and Pro editions.
Craft Commerce.summary_abuse: Craft Commerce is self-hosted software integrated tightly with Craft CMS. License validation communicates with Pixel & Tonic servers. No telemetry, tracker injection, or data harvesting beyond standard e-commerce operations is documented. The plugin is well-regarded in the Craft developer community.

Craftum.description: Craftum is an Russian website builder.
Craftum.history: Craftum is a cloud-based website and landing page builder founded in 2021 and headquartered in Saint Petersburg, Russia. The platform enables users to build websites without coding using pre-made templates. It is included in the Russian software registry, which indicates it is certified for use within Russia. The service offers a 10-day free trial period. Craftum targets small businesses and entrepreneurs primarily in the Russian-speaking market.
Craftum.summary_abuse: Craftum is a Russian-origin website builder. As a Russian-registered software product, it is subject to Russian data residency and surveillance law requirements rather than GDPR. The platform embeds analytics and marketing trackers on hosted sites. Enterprise or international deployments raise data sovereignty concerns given the regulatory environment.

Crall.description: Crall is a personalized shopping experience system.
Crall.history: Crall AS is an e-commerce personalization SaaS platform founded around 2016 and headquartered in Fredrikstad, Ostfold, Norway. The company offers a system that provides merchants with a real-time view of online store visitors, uses AI to display personalized product recommendations, and optimizes the shopping experience. Crall targets small to mid-size Nordic e-commerce businesses. The platform integrates with major e-commerce systems and provides behavioral analytics for conversion optimization.
Crall.summary_abuse: Crall embeds tracking scripts into e-commerce storefronts to monitor visitor behavior and serve personalized recommendations. This involves session tracking and behavioral profiling typical of personalization tools. GDPR consent management falls to the merchant, not Crall directly. No documented abuse beyond standard personalization data collection.

Cratejoy.description: Cratejoy is a brand new ecommerce platform with a focus on subscription payments.
Cratejoy.history: Cratejoy was founded in 2014 by Amir Elaguizy and Alex Morse and is headquartered in Austin, Texas. It emerged from Y Combinator and raised $6.2M from investors including Charles River Ventures, Maverick Capital, and SV Angel. The platform provides an all-in-one marketplace and SaaS solution for subscription box businesses, supporting over 2,500 companies and 1 million subscribers. Founder Amir Elaguizy previously founded MarketZero, which was acquired by Zynga in 2011.
Cratejoy.summary_abuse: Cratejoy processes subscriber payment and shipping data for subscription box merchants. Being a marketplace it holds customer PII across many merchants. It uses standard analytics trackers on its marketplace pages. No documented data abuse or security incidents of significance have been reported.

Crayon Syntax Highlighter.description: Syntax Highlighter supporting multiple languages, themes, fonts, highlighting from a URL, local file or post text.
Crayon Syntax Highlighter.history: Crayon Syntax Highlighter is a WordPress plugin created by Aram Kocharyan (akarmenia) and first published on August 10, 2011. At its peak it was widely used to display formatted code blocks in WordPress posts. The plugin was last updated in May 2016 and was tested only up to WordPress 4.2. It was closed and removed from the WordPress.org plugin repository on December 13, 2022 due to a security issue. A community fork called Urvanov Syntax Highlighter has been maintained as a replacement.
Crayon Syntax Highlighter.summary_abuse: Crayon Syntax Highlighter was removed from the WordPress plugin directory due to a security vulnerability. The plugin had not received updates since 2016 and no longer works correctly on modern WordPress. Active installations on existing sites pose a security risk. No evidence of intentional malicious behavior by the author, but the abandoned and vulnerable state elevates its risk profile.

Crazy Egg.history: Crazy Egg was founded in 2006 by Neil Patel and Hiten Shah as a website optimization platform. It pioneered heatmap analytics for web pages, allowing marketers to visually see where visitors click and scroll. The company is bootstrapped and has acquired Hello Bar and Stride CRM. Over 449,000 websites use Crazy Egg. Plans start at $29/month based on tracked pageviews, with tiers up to $499/month. The tool also includes session recordings, A/B testing, and traffic analytics.
Crazy Egg.summary_abuse: Crazy Egg deploys a tracking script on client websites that records visitor mouse movements, clicks, and scrolls. This behavioral tracking is privacy-sensitive and requires proper GDPR/CCPA consent disclosure by website owners. The company provides GDPR-compliant data processing agreements but the script itself is a significant tracking vector. Crazy Egg has been cited in privacy-focused discussions as a tool that requires explicit consent under EU law.

Creaitor.description: Creaitor is an AI-driven platform that offers tools for content creation and SEO optimization to help content producers enhance their online presence.
Creaitor.history: Creaitor.ai is an AI-powered content creation and SEO optimization platform founded in 2022 and headquartered in Lucerne, Switzerland. The platform provides over 80 AI writing assistants, supports 40+ languages, and offers SERP analysis and keyword research features. It targets content marketers, bloggers, and SEO professionals. Plans start at $19/month with a 7-day free trial, and include unlimited character generation at higher tiers.
Creaitor.summary_abuse: Creaitor.ai is an AI writing tool that processes user-submitted prompts and content briefs to generate text. User data may be used to improve the AI model per the privacy policy. No significant documented abuse patterns, though AI writing tools inherently raise concerns about content authenticity and potential misuse for spam or misleading content generation.

Crealive.description: Crealive is a boutique digital agency.
Crealive.history: Crealive is a boutique digital agency and e-commerce solutions provider founded in 2005 and based in Sisli, Istanbul, Turkey. The company builds custom websites and mobile applications using PHP, Laravel, and MySQL. It serves corporate clients across Turkey offering web development, content management systems, and e-commerce solutions. Crealive has grown from a small development team to a trusted technology partner for leading Turkish brands.
Crealive.summary_abuse: Crealive is a professional services agency that builds custom software for clients. Limited public information is available on its data handling practices. As a Turkey-based company, EU GDPR may not apply unless they serve EU clients. No documented abuse patterns, though public transparency on privacy practices is limited.

Create Demand.description: Create Demand is an all-in-one sales and marketing platform designed to support business owners in managing and growing their sales and marketing operations.
Create Demand.history: Create Demand is an all-in-one sales and marketing platform targeting small business owners. It provides funnel building, CRM, email automation, appointment scheduling, and membership area tools integrated in one platform, similar to GoHighLevel. The platform integrates with over 100 third-party apps via webhook/Zap connectors and is hosted on AWS infrastructure backed by Cloudflare. It emphasizes customer data ownership, claiming not to contact a user's customers.
Create Demand.summary_abuse: Create Demand is a white-label marketing platform that holds customer CRM and contact data. Its all-in-one marketing suite style makes it similar to GoHighLevel reseller platforms. These tools are sometimes misused for mass email campaigns or lead scraping. No specific documented abuse incidents found, but the platform category has elevated risk for spam and dark-pattern marketing practices.

CreateJS.description: CreateJS is a suite of modular libraries and tools which work together or independently to enable interactive content on open web technologies via HTML5.
CreateJS.history: CreateJS is a suite of modular JavaScript libraries for HTML5 canvas and interactive content created by Grant Skinner (gskinner) and first released around 2011. It is officially sponsored by Adobe, Microsoft, and Mozilla. Adobe integrated CreateJS into Flash Professional (later Adobe Animate) for HTML5 Canvas export, making it the standard library for publishing Flash-based animations as web content. The suite includes EaselJS for canvas graphics, TweenJS for animation tweens, SoundJS for audio, and PreloadJS for asset loading.
CreateJS.summary_abuse: CreateJS is a client-side JavaScript library with no telemetry or tracking behavior. It is distributed via CDN at code.createjs.com. No supply chain, license abuse, or privacy concerns have been documented. Adobe's sponsorship and integration into Animate gives it strong institutional backing.

Creatium.description: Creatium is a website builder developed in Russia that provides a user-friendly drag-and-drop interface and a variety of customisation options for creating websites without coding knowledge.
Creatium.history: Creatium is a Russian website builder founded around 2017 and headquartered in Moscow, Russia. It provides a drag-and-drop interface for building websites and landing pages without coding knowledge. The editor is available only in Russian, making it primarily a domestic Russian-market product. It offers a 14-day free trial but no permanent free plan. Features include pop-up builders, A/B testing, SEO tools, built-in calculators, and SSL certificates.
Creatium.summary_abuse: Creatium is a Russian-developed website builder subject to Russian federal law, including data localization requirements under Federal Law No. 242-FZ and FSB access provisions. As a hosted platform it holds content and user data for sites built on it. International users face data sovereignty risk. The interface is Russian-only, limiting external scrutiny.

Creativ.eMail.description: Creativ.eMail is a email editor WordPress plugin which simplifies email marketing campaign creation and pulls your WordPress blog posts, website images and WooCommerce products right into your email content.
Creativ.eMail.history: Creativ.eMail was launched around 2020 as a WordPress email marketing plugin backed by Constant Contact, part of Endurance International Group. It integrated with Jetpack and WooCommerce to simplify email campaign creation directly from the WordPress admin. The plugin allowed users to pull blog posts, images, and product listings into email templates. Creative Mail is now closed and no longer available, with the service discontinued and no migration path offered.
Creativ.eMail.summary_abuse: Creativ.eMail was a Constant Contact-backed plugin that connected WordPress sites to email marketing infrastructure. It collected subscriber email lists and behavioral data. The service has been shut down without a formal migration path, which raises concerns for users who had active subscriber lists on the platform. No intentional abuse documented, but abandonment without data export assistance is a vendor reliability concern.

CREMA.description: CREMA is a platform offering review management, marketing personalisation, and size recommendations to enhance and streamline the consumer journey.
CREMA.history: CREMA is a review management and marketing personalization platform for e-commerce brands, providing product reviews, Q&A, and visual UGC tools.
CREMA.summary_abuse: CREMA is a product review and UGC platform for e-commerce. Review platforms can be used to post fake reviews; CREMA includes fraud detection to mitigate this. No major documented abuse incidents.

Creoline.description: Creoline is a provider of scalable cloud solutions and application domain services.
Creoline.history: Creoline GmbH is a German cloud hosting and managed services provider offering virtual servers, application hosting, CDN, and enterprise cloud solutions. The company is certified to ISO-27001 and PCI-DSS standards and operates data centers in Germany, emphasizing GDPR-compliant data sovereignty. Creoline also offers a reseller marketplace for Plesk hosting products and a Sitejet-based website builder integration. It has over 280 Trustpilot reviews rated as Excellent.
Creoline.summary_abuse: Creoline is a German ISO-27001 and PCI-DSS certified hosting provider operating under strict GDPR data protection requirements. Data is stored in German data centers, providing strong sovereignty guarantees. No abuse or privacy violations have been documented. The company has positive customer reviews and transparent certification status.

Crexi.description: Crexi is a commercial real estate platform that provides property listings with photos, maps, and detailed descriptions.
Crexi.history: Crexi was founded in 2015 by Mike DeGiorgio in Los Angeles, California, with the goal of modernizing the commercial real estate industry. The platform provides digital property listings, transaction management, and market data intelligence. Since launch, it has facilitated over $330 billion in commercial real estate transactions and serves over 2 million buyers, brokers, and tenants monthly. Crexi competes with CoStar/LoopNet and has become a leading CRE marketplace.
Crexi.summary_abuse: Crexi collects detailed commercial property and transaction data as well as user behavior data on its marketplace platform. It uses standard analytics and advertising trackers on its website. No documented data misuse or security incidents. As a US-based platform primarily serving real estate professionals, GDPR compliance is limited for non-EU user bases.

Crikle.description: Crikle is a multichannel customer engagement software.
Crikle.history: Crikle was founded in 2019 and is headquartered in London, England. The platform provides multichannel sales engagement and video-based lead conversion tools, helping sales teams connect with prospects via video from social media, websites, or direct outreach. Crikle raised $699K and reached a $5.3M valuation by 2024, generating $1.6M in revenue with a team of five. Investors include Seedblink and GECAD Group.
Crikle.summary_abuse: Crikle handles video call recordings and sales conversation data, which is sensitive for both businesses and prospects. It is GDPR-compliant as a UK/EU-market tool. No significant documented privacy violations or dark patterns found. The small company size and limited public footprint means third-party security audits are unlikely.

Crisp Live Chat.description: Crisp Live Chat is a live chat solution with free and paid options.
Crisp Live Chat.history: Crisp was founded in 2015 in Nantes, France by Baptiste Jamin and Valerian Saliou. The company is 100% bootstrapped with no external funding and serves over 700,000 businesses. Crisp started as a simple live chat widget and has evolved into an all-in-one AI-powered customer messaging platform with chatbots, helpdesk, knowledge base, ticketing, and omnichannel inbox capabilities. Infrastructure is hosted entirely within the EU, making it strongly GDPR-aligned.
Crisp Live Chat.summary_abuse: Crisp is a bootstrapped, EU-hosted customer messaging platform with strong GDPR compliance built in. The chat widget collects visitor data but under a compliant framework. Being EU-domiciled and privacy-conscious, Crisp is one of the more trust-worthy live chat tools. No documented abuse, data breaches, or dark-pattern issues.

Criteo.description: Criteo provides personalised retargeting that works with Internet retailers to serve personalised online display advertisements to consumers who have previously visited the advertiser's website.
Criteo.history: Criteo was founded on November 1, 2005 in Paris, France by Jean-Baptiste Rudelle, Franck Le Ouay, and Romain Niccoli. Initially focused on R&D for machine-learning product recommendations, it launched its first product in April 2008 and pioneered dynamic retargeting ads. Criteo raised $17M in early funding from AGF Elaia Partners, Index Ventures, and Bessemer Venture Partners. In October 2013, Criteo completed a $251M IPO on NASDAQ under ticker CRTO, becoming one of the largest French tech IPOs. It has since evolved into a retail media platform.
Criteo.summary_abuse: Criteo is one of the most significant online advertising trackers, operating a retargeting network that places cookies and tracking pixels across millions of partner websites to follow users across the internet. It has been heavily scrutinized under GDPR, with the French DPA CNIL fining Criteo €40M in 2023 for GDPR violations including lack of valid consent and failure to honor opt-out requests. Criteo's tracking script is consistently flagged by privacy tools as a high-risk tracker.

Critical Mention.description: Critical Mention is a media monitoring technology that tracks, collects, and analyzes content from broadcast, online, and social media sources to provide insights into coverage and trends.
Critical Mention.history: Critical Mention was launched in 2002 as a US media monitoring SaaS platform, founded by Sean Morgan who had previously co-founded Screaming Media. The platform specialized in broadcast media monitoring with lightning-speed search, automated alerts, and clip management. Over 20 years it expanded channel and language coverage to become a leading media intelligence platform in North America. In 2022 Critical Mention was acquired by Onclusive, a global media intelligence company serving PR and communications professionals.
Critical Mention.summary_abuse: Critical Mention monitors public media broadcasts and aggregates press coverage data. As a B2B PR platform it handles client media tracking but does not collect end-user personal data in a consumer-facing context. No significant privacy violations or abuse patterns documented. Acquisition by Onclusive provides institutional stability.

CRM Done Better.description: CRM Done Better is a marketing automation software that streamlines campaign management, customer segmentation, and lead nurturing to improve efficiency and data-driven decision making.
CRM Done Better.history: CRM Done Better is a small US-based marketing automation and CRM SaaS. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
CRM Done Better.summary_abuse: CRM Done Better is a small marketing automation and CRM platform with no documented abuse incidents. Marketing automation tools can be misused for spam but no specific abuse reports exist for this vendor.

CRM+.description: CRM+ is a German CRM software product building on Vtiger with GDPR-compliant extensions and improvements.
CRM+.history: CRM+ is a German CRM product by Brainformatik, built on top of Vtiger CRM with GDPR-compliant extensions specifically tailored for the German market.
CRM+.summary_abuse: CRM+ is a German market CRM. It has no significant abuse surface and no documented abuse incidents.

CRMBOOST.description: CRMBOOST is a customer relationship management (CRM) platform that helps businesses organize, track, and manage interactions with clients and leads.
CRMBOOST.history: CRMBOOST is a small CRM platform for managing client and lead interactions. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
CRMBOOST.summary_abuse: CRMBOOST is a small CRM platform with no documented abuse incidents. Standard CRM tools have minimal direct abuse surface, though data handling practices in small CRM vendors can be opaque.

Crobox.history: Crobox was founded in 2014 by Leonard Wolters, Rodger Buyvoets, and Sjoerd Mulder in Amsterdam, Netherlands. The company describes itself as a persuasion-as-a-service platform that combines consumer psychology with machine learning to influence purchase decisions on e-commerce sites. It raised a $1.2M seed round led by Keadyn with participation from Ventech Paris. Crobox clients have included Rakuten, T-Mobile, and Under Armour. The platform uses 200+ psychological triggers to serve notifications, micro-copy, and product tags.
Crobox.summary_abuse: Crobox injects persuasion overlays and psychological nudges into e-commerce product pages using behavioral data to drive conversions. These overlay notifications can show false urgency signals such as fake low-stock or viewer counts, which is a documented dark-pattern practice in conversion optimization. The tool requires visitor tracking and behavioral profiling. Merchants using Crobox must ensure GDPR consent compliance.

Crocoblock JetElements.description: Crocoblock JetElements is an addon for Elementor that adds additional customisation options to the page builder.
Crocoblock JetElements.history: Crocoblock was founded in 2018 in Ukraine and develops the JetPlugins suite for WordPress page builders including Elementor, Gutenberg, and Bricks. JetElements is their flagship plugin providing 42+ interactive widgets for animations and content display. The full Crocoblock toolkit includes 20 JetPlugins and 200+ widgets covering dynamic content, custom post types, forms, woo commerce features, and visual layouts. The company serves web developers and designers building complex WordPress sites without custom code.
Crocoblock JetElements.summary_abuse: Crocoblock JetElements is a self-installed WordPress plugin sold via a subscription license. It is installed server-side and does not inject external tracking scripts by default. No documented supply chain issues or privacy violations. The Ukraine-based team has maintained the product actively despite challenging conditions. Plugin licensing is managed through a proprietary license server.

Croct.description: Croct is a headless CMS that incorporates A/B testing and personalization features for content management and digital delivery.
Croct.history: Croct is a headless CMS for personalization and optimization founded around 2018 in Sao Paulo, Brazil by Marcos Passos and Juliana Amorim. The platform uses a component-based approach enabling teams to run experiments and deliver personalized content without heavy developer involvement. Croct raised a $1.3M seed round in July 2021 led by Astella, with participation from multiple angel investors. The company also participated in the Techstars Boulder accelerator program in late 2023.
Croct.summary_abuse: Croct collects visitor behavioral data and runs A/B experiments to serve personalized content. This requires tracking user sessions and attributes. As a headless CMS with personalization, implementing GDPR-compliant consent management is the responsibility of the developer integrating Croct. No documented abuse patterns found.

Cronitor.description: Cronitor is a monitoring tool for developers that tracks status, performance, and uptime of cron jobs, websites, APIs, and other services.
Cronitor.history: Cronitor was founded in 2014 by August Flanagan and Shane Harter in Berkeley, California, after the founders identified a gap in cron job monitoring tools. The product launched on Hacker News and quickly found product-market fit. Cronitor is a developer-focused monitoring platform for scheduled jobs, cron tasks, and background workers. By 2023 it had reached $289.7K in revenue with 1,000 customers. The free plan includes 5 monitors; paid plans start at $7/month.
Cronitor.summary_abuse: Cronitor is a developer-facing monitoring tool that collects job execution pings and status data. It does not inject browser-side trackers and has no consumer-facing data collection. The platform is transparent about its pricing and data use. No documented abuse, dark patterns, or privacy violations.

Croogo.description: Croogo is an open-source CMS built on CakePHP, offering a platform for managing website content.
Croogo.history: Croogo is an open-source CMS built on the CakePHP MVC framework, released under the MIT License. Development was active through the mid-2010s with version 3.x; a 5.0.0 beta was released in October 2020 but the GitHub repository has seen no commits since July 2023, indicating the project is effectively dormant.
Croogo.summary_abuse: Croogo is an open-source CMS. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a self-hosted CMS with no consumer-facing hosted surface that fraudsters target.

Cross Pixel.description: Cross Pixel is an advertising platform through which advertisers can leverage the marriage of partner audience synergies with the power of retargeting.
Cross Pixel.history: Cross Pixel Media was founded in 2011 by Alan Pearlstein, who previously founded digital advertising agency Flying Point Media in 2002. The company is headquartered in Miami Beach, Florida, and provides audience data and contextual targeting solutions for the programmatic advertising industry. Its audiences are integrated into over 40 DSP partners worldwide. Cross Pixel raised $2M from KBS Ventures and was later acquired by Knower Tech.
Cross Pixel.summary_abuse: Cross Pixel operates a data marketplace that harvests and monetizes behavioral data from across the web to build audience segments for advertisers. This involves large-scale user tracking without direct consumer consent, which raises significant GDPR and CCPA concerns. The company aggregates third-party data from partner sites and sells it to programmatic advertising platforms. This type of data brokerage is among the highest-risk categories for user privacy.

Cross Sell.description: Cross Sell provide recommendations solution for Shopify based sites.
Cross Sell.history: Cross Sell is a small Shopify app that provides product recommendations for ecommerce stores. Corporate ownership and founding year are not publicly disclosed.
Cross Sell.summary_abuse: Cross Sell is a Shopify product recommendations app. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B ecommerce tool with no consumer-facing attack surface beyond standard retail recommendation widgets.

CrossBox.history: CrossBox is a self-hosted webmail and communication suite that has been in development since 2016, with production-ready status achieved in September 2019. The company behind CrossBox has been operating as a custom software development firm since 2010. CrossBox integrates with hosting control panels including Plesk, cPanel, and DirectAdmin as a modern alternative to Roundcube or Horde. It offers 40+ collaboration features including email, chat, audio/video calls, screen sharing, and file sharing all in a self-hosted package.
CrossBox.summary_abuse: CrossBox is a self-hosted email and communication platform, meaning all data remains on the operator's own server. It has no external telemetry or data reporting. This architecture gives hosting providers and enterprise clients strong data sovereignty. No abuse or privacy violations have been documented. Being self-hosted, it is inherently GDPR-friendly.

Crowdin.description: Crowdin is a localization management platform that streamlines the process of translating, organizing, and maintaining multilingual content for software, websites, and other digital products.
Crowdin.history: Crowdin was founded in 2009 in Ternopil, Ukraine by Serhiy Dmytryshyn as a hobby project for localizing small software projects. It launched officially in January 2009 and gained traction after being recommended by a prominent translation industry expert. Notable early customers included Minecraft, Plague Inc., and Khan Academy. The company has since expanded with offices in Tallinn, Estonia. By 2024 Crowdin surpassed 3 million registered accounts and has become one of the leading localization management platforms globally.
Crowdin.summary_abuse: Crowdin handles translation files and content strings from customers' software and websites. These may include sensitive UI text. Being a GDPR-compliant European company with SOC2 certification, data handling is governed by strong privacy practices. No documented abuse or data misuse. The platform has strong community trust and is widely used by open source projects.

Crowdsignal.description: Crowdsignal is an online tool formerly known as Polldaddy that enables the creation of free polls for embedding on websites.
Crowdsignal.history: Crowdsignal was originally known as Polldaddy, founded around 2007 as a polling and survey tool. It was acquired by Automattic, the company behind WordPress.com, and later rebranded as Crowdsignal. The service enables creation of polls, surveys, quizzes, and ratings. The Crowdsignal Dashboard WordPress plugin has over 100,000 active installations. Free accounts include the first 2,500 survey responses at no cost. The platform is deeply integrated with the WordPress and Jetpack ecosystems.
Crowdsignal.summary_abuse: Crowdsignal is owned by Automattic, a major and trusted internet company. The service collects survey and poll response data from visitors on behalf of website owners. It embeds a small JavaScript widget. Under Automattic's ownership, GDPR and privacy practices are well-maintained. No documented abuse or tracking violations.

CrownPeak.description: CrownPeak is a cloud-based Digital Experience Platform (DXP).
CrownPeak.history: CrownPeak was founded in January 2001 by Jim Howard and Carl Sutter in Los Angeles, California, as the first company to offer web content management as a SaaS solution. The company was headquartered in Denver, Colorado. It received Series D funding of $50M in April 2016. CrownPeak acquired e-Spirit (FirstSpirit) in 2021 to expand its headless CMS and digital experience platform capabilities. In December 2025 CrownPeak was acquired by Rezolve AI.
CrownPeak.summary_abuse: CrownPeak is an enterprise-grade cloud CMS and digital experience platform serving large organizations with strict compliance requirements. The company holds SOC2 and ISO 27001 certifications and provides GDPR-compliant data processing. No documented abuse or privacy violations. The company also operates the Universal Consent Platform for GDPR consent management.

CrownPeak Universal Consent Platform.description: CrownPeak Universal Consent Platform is a tool for managing user consent and ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations.
CrownPeak Universal Consent Platform.history: CrownPeak Universal Consent Platform (UCP) is a consent management platform (CMP) for enterprise websites, a product of CrownPeak Technology. It helps brands comply with GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations by deploying cookie consent banners powered by a database covering over 6,000 technology vendors. The UCP dynamically populates consent notices with real-time data about first and third-party vendors on a site. It supports Google Consent Mode v2 integration and over 50 languages.
CrownPeak Universal Consent Platform.summary_abuse: CrownPeak UCP is itself a privacy and compliance tool designed to enable GDPR consent. By definition it operates as a guardian of privacy rather than a threat to it. It injects a single consent tag rather than tracking scripts. No documented misuse or privacy violations associated with the UCP product itself.

Cryout Creations Bravada.description: Bravada is an unparalleled fullscreen WordPress theme created by Cryout Creations.
Cryout Creations Bravada.history: Cryout Creations is a small WordPress theme development studio that has been producing free and premium themes since approximately 2011. The studio is based in Romania and offers themes with extensive customization options. Their portfolio includes Bravada, Fluida, Mantra, and Parabola, all available for free on WordPress.org with optional premium Plus versions. The themes are GPL-licensed and self-hosted on WordPress installations.
Cryout Creations Bravada.summary_abuse: Cryout Creations themes are GPL-licensed WordPress themes with no external analytics scripts injected. They are distributed through the WordPress.org theme directory and the developer's own site. No supply chain issues, tracking, or abuse patterns have been documented. The themes are purely styling tools with no data collection.

Cryout Creations Fluida.description: Fluida is a modern, crystal clear and squeaky clean WordPress theme by Cryout Creations.
Cryout Creations Fluida.history: Cryout Creations is a small WordPress theme development studio that has been producing free and premium themes since approximately 2011. The studio is based in Romania and offers themes with extensive customization options. Their portfolio includes Bravada, Fluida, Mantra, and Parabola, all available for free on WordPress.org with optional premium Plus versions. The themes are GPL-licensed and self-hosted on WordPress installations.
Cryout Creations Fluida.summary_abuse: Cryout Creations themes are GPL-licensed WordPress themes with no external analytics scripts injected. They are distributed through the WordPress.org theme directory and the developer's own site. No supply chain issues, tracking, or abuse patterns have been documented. The themes are purely styling tools with no data collection.

Cryout Creations Mantra.description: Mantra is a do-it-yourself WordPress theme, featuring a pack of over 100 customization option created by Cryout Creations.
Cryout Creations Mantra.history: Cryout Creations is a small WordPress theme development studio that has been producing free and premium themes since approximately 2011. The studio is based in Romania and offers themes with extensive customization options. Their portfolio includes Bravada, Fluida, Mantra, and Parabola, all available for free on WordPress.org with optional premium Plus versions. The themes are GPL-licensed and self-hosted on WordPress installations.
Cryout Creations Mantra.summary_abuse: Cryout Creations themes are GPL-licensed WordPress themes with no external analytics scripts injected. They are distributed through the WordPress.org theme directory and the developer's own site. No supply chain issues, tracking, or abuse patterns have been documented. The themes are purely styling tools with no data collection.

Cryout Creations Parabola.description: Parabola is an fully responsive, clean and elegant design WordPress theme created by Cryout Creations.
Cryout Creations Parabola.history: Cryout Creations is a small WordPress theme development studio that has been producing free and premium themes since approximately 2011. The studio is based in Romania and offers themes with extensive customization options. Their portfolio includes Bravada, Fluida, Mantra, and Parabola, all available for free on WordPress.org with optional premium Plus versions. The themes are GPL-licensed and self-hosted on WordPress installations.
Cryout Creations Parabola.summary_abuse: Cryout Creations themes are GPL-licensed WordPress themes with no external analytics scripts injected. They are distributed through the WordPress.org theme directory and the developer's own site. No supply chain issues, tracking, or abuse patterns have been documented. The themes are purely styling tools with no data collection.

crypto-js.description: crypto-js is a JavaScript library of crypto standards.
crypto-js.history: crypto-js is a JavaScript cryptography library implementing standards like AES, SHA, HMAC. It has been available since around 2009 and became widely used. Activity on the main repository has significantly slowed, with security concerns raised about its continued maintenance. Security researchers have warned that unmaintained crypto libraries should be replaced.
crypto-js.summary_abuse: crypto-js has no notable direct abuse signal as a library. However, it is sometimes included in malware or phishing kits to perform client-side cryptographic operations. Its dormant maintenance state means security vulnerabilities may go unpatched, which is a risk for applications that depend on it.

Crypto-Loot.description: Crypto-Loot is a browser based web miner for the uPlexa Blockchain.
Crypto-Loot.history: Crypto-Loot emerged in 2017 as a competing browser-based cryptomining service to Coinhive, designed to mine uPlexa and Monero cryptocurrency using website visitors' CPU resources without explicit consent. It was widely used in cryptojacking campaigns and declined sharply after Coinhive shut down in 2019, effectively becoming dormant.
Crypto-Loot.summary_abuse: Crypto-Loot is a browser-based cryptomining script that was frequently injected into websites without owner or visitor consent, representing a textbook cryptojacking vector. It ranked among the top cryptomining threats in 2018 according to security researchers. Its primary use was to silently consume visitors' CPU cycles for cryptocurrency mining, constituting a form of resource theft. The service appeared to have no meaningful AUP enforcement and the operator's identity was opaque. It is now largely defunct following the Coinhive shutdown in 2019 that collapsed the browser-mining ecosystem.

Crystallize.description: Crystallize is an ecommerce platform that offers a headless ecommerce solution for businesses.
Crystallize.history: Crystallize is a Norwegian headless commerce platform incubated by Snowball, a digital agency. The founders created Crystallize after starting an artisan bicycle factory and finding no suitable commerce platform for rich product storytelling. It launched as a GraphQL-based PIM and headless commerce service combining product information management, a digital asset manager, CMS, payments, and subscription commerce in a single API. The company holds Norwegian organization number 919134216 and has been involved in supporting the React Day Norway conference.
Crystallize.summary_abuse: Crystallize is a developer-focused headless commerce platform with no consumer-facing tracking components. Merchants using Crystallize own their customer data. The platform is EU-based with Norwegian data governance. No documented privacy violations or abuse patterns. Its API-first architecture gives development teams complete control over data flows.

CS Cart.description: CS Cart is a turnkey ecommerce shopping cart software solution.
CS Cart.history: CS-Cart was founded in 2005 and is a self-hosted e-commerce platform developed by Simtech. It is licensed software for running online stores and multi-vendor marketplaces. The company is registered in Cyprus.
