CS Cart.summary_abuse: CS-Cart is a self-hosted e-commerce software platform. As with any e-commerce software, it can be used to run fraudulent storefronts, but the self-hosted nature means the store operator (not CS-Cart) controls the site. No significant documented abuse of the platform itself.

CSSIgniter Olsen Light.description: CSSIgniter Olsen Light is a clean, minimal, stylish and elegant WordPress blog theme, perfect for lifestyle, food, cooking, fashion, travel, wedding, health & fitness, photography and beauty blogging.
CSSIgniter Olsen Light.history: CSSIgniter Olsen Light is a free, minimal WordPress blog theme developed by CSSIgniter, a Greek WordPress theme company. It was distributed on the WordPress theme directory as a free tier of the premium Olsen theme.
CSSIgniter Olsen Light.summary_abuse: Olsen Light is a free WordPress blog theme. No meaningful abuse surface or documented incidents.

cState.description: cState is an open-source static (serverless) status page.
cState.history: cState is an open-source static status page built on Hugo and Go, created around 2017 by Mantas Vilcinskas. It is hosted on GitHub under the cstate organization and uses a static-site model requiring no server-side runtime. Civo maintains a fork as well.
cState.summary_abuse: cState is a self-hosted open-source static status page generator. It has no consumer-facing hosted surface and no documented abuse signal.

cStore.description: cStore is a B2B wholesale platform facilitating automatic order acceptance from regular customers.
cStore.history: cStore is a Polish B2B wholesale e-commerce platform developed by a team based in Gdynia, Poland, with roots going back to approximately 2006 and serving over 700 projects. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events are recorded.
cStore.summary_abuse: cStore is a B2B wholesale platform aimed at business clients placing regular orders; it has no notable abuse signal as a storefront or phishing vector. The platform is gated behind business contracts with no anonymous public signup, significantly limiting fraud exposure.

CTAwidget.description: CTAwidget is a tool designed to increase website conversion through customizable widgets aimed at enhancing user engagement and optimizing conversion rates.
CTAwidget.history: CTAwidget is a Russian-origin website conversion tool offering customizable call-to-action widgets to boost website engagement.
CTAwidget.summary_abuse: CTAwidget is a Russian-hosted website widget tool. Russian-jurisdiction SaaS products carry elevated risk for GDPR non-compliance and potentially increased abuse tolerance. No specific documented incidents.

CTRWOW.description: CTRWOW is a system designed for building template funnels, offering tools for streamlining the creation and management of marketing funnels.
CTRWOW.history: CTRWOW is a marketing funnel template builder tool offered under the WowSuite brand. No public founding year or ownership history is available.
CTRWOW.summary_abuse: CTRWOW is a funnel builder tool with limited public footprint. Marketing funnel tools can be misused to create phishing or scam pages, though no specific documented abuse incidents are on record for CTRWOW.

CTT.description: CTT operates as the national postal service of Portugal.
CTT.history: CTT (Correios de Portugal) is the national postal service of Portugal with roots tracing to 1520 under the Portuguese Royal Post. In its modern form as CTT it dates from the early 20th century. The company was privatized and listed on the Lisbon Stock Exchange in 2013. CTT provides postal, logistics, and financial services.
CTT.summary_abuse: CTT is the Portuguese national postal operator. As a parcel tracking and logistics service embedded on e-commerce sites, it has limited abuse surface. No notable abuse incidents documented.

CU Chat.description: CU Chat is a custom AI chatbot designed to provide 24/7 support, tailored to the specific needs of members.
CU Chat.history: CU Chat is an AI chatbot platform tailored for credit unions (CU) providing 24/7 automated member support. Specific founding details are not publicly prominent.
CU Chat.summary_abuse: CU Chat is a specialized AI chatbot for credit unions. No notable abuse surface or documented incidents.

CubeCart.description: CubeCart is a free ecommerce platform that businesses can use to build, manage, and market their online stores.
CubeCart.history: CubeCart is a British open-source PHP e-commerce shopping cart platform founded in 2003 by Devellion Ltd. It has been 100% designed and developed in the UK since launch, making it one of the longer-standing open-source e-commerce solutions. CubeCart is free and responsive, offering multi-currency support, country management, unlimited administrators, and a range of payment gateway and shipping module extensions. The platform currently powers approximately 2,350 online stores worldwide.
CubeCart.summary_abuse: CubeCart is a self-hosted open-source e-commerce platform. It has no external telemetry or tracking. Security has historically required regular patching due to the nature of e-commerce software. Merchants using CubeCart are responsible for their own PCI compliance when processing payments. No supply chain or abuse issues documented.

Cubilis.description: Cubilis is a management and reservation system for all types of accommodation, such as hotels, B&Bs and hostels.
Cubilis.history: Cubilis is a hotel channel manager and property management system founded in 2001 in Brugge (Bruges), Belgium, as a product of Stardekk. The platform connects accommodation businesses to over 200 online booking channels including Booking.com and Airbnb, and integrates with 50+ property management systems. In 2023 Stardekk was acquired by Lighthouse (formerly OTA Insights), making Cubilis part of the Lighthouse suite of hospitality intelligence and distribution tools.
Cubilis.summary_abuse: Cubilis is a B2B hospitality channel management platform handling reservation and pricing data for hotels. As a Belgian GDPR-regulated company, it processes guest data under EU privacy law. No documented privacy violations or abuse patterns. The Lighthouse acquisition provides institutional backing.

Cubyn.description: Cubyn is B2B logistics company headquartered in France.
Cubyn.history: Cubyn was founded in 2014 by Adrien Fernandez Baca and Mathieu Lemaire in Paris, France. The company pioneered first-mile logistics for e-commerce, offering on-demand courier collection of unwrapped goods. In 2019 it launched a full fulfillment service for B2B merchant clients. Cubyn won €500K in the Digital French Tour award in 2015 and subsequently raised multiple funding rounds from DN Capital, Partech, and 360 Capital Partners, reaching a total of $84M raised. The company operates a 25,000 m2 fulfillment center in France.
Cubyn.summary_abuse: Cubyn is a logistics and fulfillment provider handling physical goods and merchant order data. It processes shipping and parcel tracking data for e-commerce sellers. As a GDPR-regulated French company, it adheres to EU data protection standards. No documented privacy violations or abuse patterns.

CUE.description: CUE is a unified media enterprise platform tailored for news media organizations seeking efficiency and revenue growth in the media market.
CUE.history: CUE is a unified media enterprise platform for news organizations, developed by Norwegian company Stibo DX. It provides editorial workflow, content management, and publishing tools for media houses.
CUE.summary_abuse: CUE is an enterprise editorial platform for news media. It serves controlled enterprise clients and has no meaningful public abuse surface. No documented incidents.

Cufon.description: Cufon is a tool used to overlap real text with an image.
Cufon.history: Cufon is a JavaScript library for custom web font rendering created in 2009 by Simo Kinnunen as a pre-CSS3 solution for using non-standard fonts on websites. It works by converting font files to a proprietary vector format and rendering them using the HTML5 Canvas element or VML for Internet Explorer. Cufon became popular before native @font-face support was widespread. With the universal adoption of WOFF/WOFF2 fonts and CSS3 font-face, Cufon became obsolete. The project has been abandoned and is now considered deprecated.
Cufon.summary_abuse: Cufon is an abandoned JavaScript font rendering library. It has no telemetry, tracking, or data collection. Sites still using Cufon face compatibility issues with modern browsers but no security or privacy risks from the library itself. No supply chain issues documented.

Culqi.description: Culqi is a payment processing platform that enables businesses to accept card payments and receive funds on the same day.
Culqi.history: Culqi was founded in 2013 by Nicolas Di Pace and Amparo Nalvarte in San Isidro, Lima, Peru. The founders identified that most Latin American transactions were conducted in cash and online businesses were missing out on sales due to limited payment infrastructure. Culqi provides payment processing for credit, debit, and prepaid cards as well as digital wallets via POS systems and online payment gateways. The company raised $550K in funding and has expanded its services across Latin America.
Culqi.summary_abuse: Culqi is a payment gateway processing cardholder data and therefore must be PCI DSS compliant. It handles sensitive financial data including card numbers and transaction details. As a Peruvian company primarily serving Latin American markets, it is not subject to GDPR. No documented security breaches or data misuse. Standard payment gateway risk profile.

Curated.description: Curated is a platform that helps organize engaging content and support ongoing customer relationship development through consistent and relevant interactions.
Curated.history: Curated was founded in 2015 by Dave Verwer as a newsletter curation and publishing platform. In April 2016, it was acquired by Litmus, an email marketing analytics company, providing it with a larger operational home.
Curated.summary_abuse: Curated is a newsletter curation platform. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a tool for content publishers with no anonymous-signup or bulk-email features that spammers commonly exploit.

Curaytor.description: Curaytor is a digital marketing platform that provides online strategy, advertising, and lead generation solutions for businesses seeking to enhance their digital presence.
Curaytor.history: Curaytor was founded in 2013 in Boston, MA by Chris Smith and Jimmy Mackin as a digital marketing platform focused on real estate agents and teams. The company has grown to approximately $11M in revenue and 500+ customers, remaining independent.
Curaytor.summary_abuse: Curaytor is a real estate marketing and lead generation platform. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B tool serving licensed real estate professionals with subscription pricing and no anonymous access.

Custobar.description: Custobar is a customer data platform that enables personalized marketing by leveraging data to tailor campaigns and communications.
Custobar.history: Custobar was founded in 2014 in Helsinki, Finland as a customer data platform and marketing automation tool focused on retail and ecommerce businesses. It has grown to serve European retailers needing omnichannel personalization and remains an independent company.
Custobar.summary_abuse: Custobar is a customer data platform for retail marketers. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B tool handling first-party customer data for legitimate retail marketing purposes.

Custom Fonts.description: Custom Fonts plugin helps you easily embed custom fonts files (woff2, woff, ttf, svg, eot, otf) easily in your WordPress website.
Custom Fonts.history: Custom Fonts is a free WordPress plugin developed and maintained by Brainstorm Force, an Indian WordPress company best known for the Astra theme. The plugin allows users to host custom fonts locally, avoiding external requests to services like Google Fonts. It integrates with WordPress full-site editing and block themes.
Custom Fonts.summary_abuse: Custom Fonts is a WordPress plugin that hosts fonts locally on the user's server. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal. Being fully open-source and handling only font assets, it presents virtually no abuse risk.

Custom Twitter Feeds.history: Custom Twitter Feeds is a WordPress plugin by Smash Balloon that displays Twitter/X feeds on WordPress sites. It is distributed as a GPL-licensed free plugin with a paid Pro version. As Twitter rebranded to X in 2023 and restricted API access, the plugin's relevance has diminished significantly.
Custom Twitter Feeds.summary_abuse: Custom Twitter Feeds is a WordPress plugin that embeds social media feeds. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a display plugin without features that fraudsters commonly exploit. Twitter API usage does expose minimal data from public feeds.

CustomDonations.description: CustomDonations is a cloud-based service providing nonprofits with tools to create and manage online donation forms.
CustomDonations.history: CustomDonations is a cloud-based donation form service for nonprofits, universities, and churches to accept online donations. Founding year and detailed corporate history are not publicly disclosed.
CustomDonations.summary_abuse: CustomDonations is a nonprofit online donation platform. Donation platforms can be targeted for card testing attacks, but CustomDonations operates in the nonprofit sector with standard payment processing safeguards. No specific documented abuse incidents were found.

Customer Alliance.description: Customer Alliance is a cloud-based platform facilitating customer reviews and feedback management and analysis.
Customer Alliance.history: Customer Alliance was co-founded in 2009 by Torsten Sabel and Moritz Klussmann in Berlin, Germany. The company provides cloud-based customer review and feedback management software, serving primarily the hospitality and retail industries in European markets.
Customer Alliance.summary_abuse: Customer Alliance is a review and feedback management platform for businesses. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B tool for collecting and managing customer reviews rather than hosting user-generated content that fraudsters exploit.

Customer Stories.description: Customer Stories is a customer marketing automation platform designed to support modern marketing and sales teams in streamlining engagement and outreach efforts.
Customer Stories.history: Customer Stories is a small customer marketing automation platform aimed at marketing and sales teams. Corporate ownership, founding year, and funding history are not publicly disclosed.
Customer Stories.summary_abuse: Customer Stories is a customer marketing automation tool. It has no notable abuse profile; it is a B2B tool for managing customer advocacy and case studies rather than providing features that fraudsters exploit.

Customer.io.description: Customer.io is an automated messaging platform for marketers.
Customer.io.history: Founded in 2012 by Colin Nederkoorn and John Allison after they met at a New York venture-funded startup and identified the gap between marketer needs and developer-locked customer data. Bootstrapped and profitable for years, headquartered in Portland, Oregon area. Grew to over $55M in annual revenue and is widely used for behavioral email automation. No major acquisitions or ownership changes since founding.
Customer.io.summary_abuse: Customer.io is a behavioral email and messaging automation platform. Like all email automation platforms, it can be used for spam or phishing if abused, but Customer.io requires verified domains and implements anti-abuse policies including AUP enforcement. Its primary user base is legitimate SaaS and e-commerce businesses. Abuse risk is moderate for an email platform but the vendor actively enforces its terms.

CustomerSure.description: CustomerSure is a software platform designed to gather, manage, and respond to customer feedback, enhancing satisfaction and driving sales growth.
CustomerSure.history: CustomerSure was founded in 2010 in Newcastle upon Tyne, UK as a voice-of-customer and customer feedback platform. The company has remained independent and focused on helping B2B companies gather and act on customer feedback; no notable ownership changes or acquisitions.
CustomerSure.summary_abuse: CustomerSure is a B2B customer feedback and survey platform with no notable abuse signal. It collects survey responses from customers of its clients and has no consumer-facing content-hosting surface that fraudsters would target.

CustomFit.description: CustomFit is a no-code website personalization platform that enables marketers to increase conversions by customizing content for specific audience segments.
CustomFit.history: Founded in 2019 in Bengaluru, India, CustomFit.ai offers no-code A/B testing and website personalization for D2C and B2B brands. The company raised seed funding from LetsVenture and other investors and grew to $1.3M ARR by 2024 with a small team of 14 employees.
CustomFit.summary_abuse: CustomFit.ai is a personalization and A/B testing SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. It operates as a tool to optimize website conversion rates and has no features that could be directly weaponized for phishing or fraud. No documented abuse incidents.

Customily.description: Customily is an online product personalisation software.
Customily.history: Customily was founded in 2017 to provide product personalization and customization software for e-commerce merchants, integrating with Shopify, WooCommerce, Etsy, and Amazon. No notable ownership changes since founding.
Customily.summary_abuse: Customily is a product personalization tool for e-commerce merchants with no notable abuse signal. Its function is enabling print-on-demand and custom product design workflows, not content hosting or user-generated content with fraud potential.

Cux.description: Cux is a UX automation tool designed to enhance conversion rates by analysing and utilising behavioural patterns.
Cux.history: CUX was founded in 2019 by Kamil and Paulina Walkowiak in Wroclaw, Poland as a digital experience analytics and UX automation platform. It remains an independent Polish startup with no notable ownership changes.
Cux.summary_abuse: Cux is a UX analytics and session-recording tool with no notable abuse signal. It is deployed on client websites to analyze visitor behavior and has no content-hosting or user-generated content features that would attract fraudulent use.

Cvent.description: Cvent is an event management software that offers a platform for managing in-person, virtual, and hybrid events.
Cvent.history: Founded in September 1999 by Reggie Aggarwal in Tysons Corner, Virginia. After near-bankruptcy following the dot-com bust and 9/11 attacks with 80% staff cuts, the company recovered by 2003. Received $136 million from New Enterprise Associates in 2011 and went public on the NYSE in August 2013. Acquired by Vista Equity Partners for $1.65 billion in 2016, and subsequently taken private again. Acquired by Blackstone for $4.6 billion in June 2023 with minority investment from ADIA.
Cvent.summary_abuse: Cvent is a leading enterprise event management platform used by Fortune 500 companies and large associations. It has no notable abuse signal given its enterprise-focused sales model and strong identity verification requirements. No documented cases of significant platform abuse or fraud.

Cwicly.description: Cwicly is an advanced professional design and block toolkit that integrates directly with the WordPress editor.
Cwicly.history: Cwicly was a premium WordPress Gutenberg block builder created around 2021 by founder Louis-Alexander Desiré. In March 2024, the founder announced unexpected closure citing relentless criticism from WordPress influencers over its Gutenberg-based approach. All orders after January 1, 2024 were refunded. The plugin was subsequently made available for free download, but development is officially discontinued.
Cwicly.summary_abuse: Cwicly is a deprecated WordPress block-building plugin with no notable abuse signal. Its functionality is limited to front-end WordPress editing and it poses no abuse risk as it is a developer tool with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

CXDP.description: CXDP is a platform that enables businesses to create, manage, and deliver personalized marketing communications across multiple channels based on customer data and engagement insights.
CXDP.history: CXDP is a Russian-market Customer Experience Data Platform operated by OKSK LLC, headquartered at Moscow's Skolkovo innovation center. The platform combines marketing automation with customer data management for multi-channel communications. It has been running industry events since 2019 in Russia, Belarus, and Kazakhstan.
CXDP.summary_abuse: CXDP is a marketing automation and mass-communication platform primarily serving the Russian market. Such platforms can be misused for spam or phishing if customer data policies are lax. Russian-headquartered companies operate outside GDPR and are not subject to standard EU/US regulatory frameworks for data protection. No specific documented abuse incidents are associated with this product, but its marketing automation features inherently carry email and SMS mass-send capabilities.

Cxense.description: Cxense was an AI-powered data management and intelligent personalisation platform.
Cxense.history: Cxense was founded around 2010 in Oslo, Norway as an AI-powered data management and personalization platform. In October 2019, Piano Software acquired Cxense, integrating its DMP and personalization capabilities into the broader Piano customer experience platform. Cxense is now a Piano company and no longer operates independently.
Cxense.summary_abuse: Cxense is a DMP and personalization platform used by media publishers; no notable abuse signal beyond the general privacy-related concerns of audience data collection and targeting. It is now part of Piano and targets enterprise media organizations.

CYBERBIZ.description: CYBERBIZ is an ecommerce hosted solution based in Taiwan, offering cloud-based tools for setting up and managing online stores.
CYBERBIZ.history: CYBERBIZ was founded in 2016 in Taiwan as a cloud-based e-commerce platform targeted at the Taiwanese and broader Asian market. The platform is a full-stack hosted solution for online stores.
CYBERBIZ.summary_abuse: CYBERBIZ is a Taiwanese e-commerce platform. E-commerce platforms can host fraudulent storefronts; CYBERBIZ operates primarily in the Taiwanese market with local merchant accountability. No major documented abuse incidents.

CyberChimps Responsive.description: CyberChimps Responsive is a modern, lightweight, fully customizable, fast and responsive WordPress theme.
CyberChimps Responsive.history: CyberChimps Inc was founded around 2010-2011 in San Francisco, California. The company originally acquired the Responsive WordPress theme from Emil Uzelac and ThemeID when it had reached 500,000 downloads, continuing development of what became one of the most downloaded free WordPress themes with over 4.5 million downloads and 11 million sites launched.
CyberChimps Responsive.summary_abuse: CyberChimps Responsive is a free WordPress theme with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer tool that runs on the user's own WordPress server and has no consumer-facing hosted surface that could be exploited for phishing or fraud.

Cybersource.description: Cybersource is an ecommerce credit card payment system solution.
Cybersource.history: Founded in 1994 in Foster City, California as an early internet payment gateway. Went public in 1999. In 2007, acquired Authorize.Net for $565 million, expanding into the small business market. In April 2010, Visa acquired CyberSource for approximately $2 billion, completing the deal in July 2010. At the time of acquisition, CyberSource processed approximately 25% of all US eCommerce transactions. Now serves over 450,000 businesses worldwide as Visa's merchant services platform.
Cybersource.summary_abuse: CyberSource is Visa's enterprise payment gateway and fraud management platform. While payment infrastructure can be implicated in fraud chains, CyberSource is a regulated financial services provider with strong KYC requirements, PCI-DSS certification, and sophisticated fraud detection tools. It predominantly serves legitimate enterprises and has robust anti-abuse infrastructure. Isolated misuse by merchants is possible but the vendor responds to fraud reports and enforces compliance.

Cybot.description: Cybot is a tool that facilitates compliance with data privacy laws for website operators and marketers.
Cybot.history: Cybot A/S was founded in 2012 in Copenhagen, Denmark, launching Cookiebot as one of the first automated cookie consent management platforms, predating GDPR by years. After GDPR came into effect in 2018, Cookiebot became one of the most widely deployed consent management platforms in Europe. In 2021, Cybot merged with Usercentrics, combining two leading consent platforms to form a global market leader in consent management.
Cybot.summary_abuse: Cybot (Cookiebot) is a GDPR consent management platform with no notable abuse signal. Its purpose is to enable legal cookie consent, and it processes consent records on behalf of website operators. As a privacy-infrastructure tool, it is not a vector for phishing or fraud. No documented abuse incidents.

Cylindo.description: Cylindo is a provider of automated content creation for 3D assets and photorealistic images, facilitating product visualization that can be utilized in various settings.
Cylindo.history: Cylindo was founded in 2012 in Copenhagen, Denmark as a 3D product visualization platform for retail. In April 2022, Cylindo was acquired by Chaos (the maker of V-Ray rendering software) to create an end-to-end visualization platform spanning 3D commerce and beyond.
Cylindo.summary_abuse: Cylindo is a 3D product visualization platform serving furniture and retail brands with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool with no consumer-facing content hosting or user-generated content surface.

Czater.description: Czater is an live chat solution with extended CRM and videochat features.
Czater.history: Czater was founded in 2015 in Warsaw, Poland as a live chat solution with CRM and video chat features. The company remains a small independent Polish startup serving primarily local SMB clients.
Czater.summary_abuse: Czater is a small live chat platform with no notable abuse signal. Its customer service chat widget does not offer content hosting or features commonly exploited by fraudsters.
